**URBAN TOUR**

High School Talent Search

Official Event Programme  |  10:00 AM – 5:00 PM  |  Urban News on PPP TV Kenya

**1.  PRODUCTION TEAM**

**Hosts (On Stage — Entire Event)**

| **HOST** | **HOST** |
| --- | --- |
| Eugine Micah | Lucy Ogunde |

**Production Crew**

| **ROLE** | **NAME** | **ROLE** | **NAME** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Camera Operator 1 | Bico | Camera Operator 2 | George |
| Camera Operator 3 | Lallez | Reels & Social Content | Rania |
| Sound Engineer (Mics, Lapels, Stage Audio) | Pauline | Resident DJ — Full Event | DJ Xavi |

**Guest Partners — The Experience ****Hub  (****3 Hype Sets Across the Day)**

| **NAME** | **ROLE** | **NAME** | **ROLE** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Hype Ola | Hypeman | Gospel Hypeman | Gospel Energy |
| DJ Carian | Guest DJ | Nyarangi + Dancers | Dance Crew Lead |

**Driq**** Academy — Official ****Modeling**** Partner**

| **REPRESENTATIVE** | **REPRESENTING** | **ROLE** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Mr & Mrs Kasarani | Kasarani | Title Holders — Prestige Walk |
| Mr & Mrs Ruiru | Ruiru | Title Holders — Prestige Walk |
| Driq Academy Models | Academy | Category Coordination & Stage Support |

**2.  PRE-EVENT SETUP**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **6:00 AM** | 3 hrs | **Stage ****&**** Sound Setup** | DJ Xavi, Pauline, camera crew — full rig, sound check, mic & lapel test, camera positioning |
| **9:00 AM** | 30 min | **Final Crew Briefing** | All team on site. Run-through of programme. MCs prep, Driq Academy models warm-up, Experience Hub briefed |
| **9:30 AM** | 30 min | **Student Registration ****&**** Warm-Up** | Students sign up for categories. DJ Xavi plays pre-show mix. Camera crew captures b-roll |
| **9:55 AM** | 5 min | **All Crew — Final Positions** | Eugine & Lucy mic'd and on standby. DJ Xavi on decks. Hype Ola & Experience Hub in position. Cameras rolling |

**3.  FULL PROGRAMME — 10:00 AM TO 5:00 PM**

**Phase 1 — Opening ****Ceremony  |****  10:00 AM – 10:53 AM**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **10:00 AM** | 15 min | **Grand Opening — MCs Take the Stage** | Eugine & Lucy open the show. DJ Xavi drops hype intro. HYPE SET #1 — Experience Hub fires up the crowd. Rania on reels from first minute |
| **10:15 AM** | 5 min | **National Anthem** | All students, staff and guests stand. MCs facilitate |
| **10:20 AM** | 5 min | **School Anthem** | Students lead the school anthem on stage. Full camera coverage |
| **10:25 AM** | 5 min | **East African Anthem** | If applicable. MCs facilitate. Skipped if not part of school tradition |
| **10:30 AM** | 5 min | **Opening Prayer** | Student or invited spiritual leader delivers opening prayer |
| **10:35 AM** | 10 min | **Principal's Address** | MCs introduce the Principal. Principal delivers address. Urban News Certificate of Partnership + Gift Hamper presented to Principal on camera by Eugine & Lucy |
| **10:45 AM** | 8 min | **Deputy Principal's Address** | MCs introduce Deputy Principal. Brief address. Photo moment with MCs on stage |
| **10:53 AM** | 12 min | **MC Hype Set — Open the Floor** | Eugine & Lucy take command. DJ Xavi + Hype Ola. Full energy warm-up. MCs tease all coming categories. Crowd participation moment |

**Phase 2 — Talent Showcase Part ****1  |****  11:05 AM – 12:35 PM**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **11:05 AM** | 5 min | **Category Intro: Choir** | MCs introduce the Choir category. Build anticipation. DJ drops a tease |
| **11:10 AM** | 30 min | **CHOIR PERFORMANCE** | School choir performs. Full stage. MCs react between acts. DJ Xavi handles transitions |
| **11:40 AM** | 5 min | **MC Bridge + Crowd Reaction** | MCs celebrate the choir. Crowd reactions captured by Rania for reels |
| **11:45 AM** | 5 min | **Category Intro: Gospel** | MCs introduce Gospel category with energy and reverence |
| **11:50 AM** | 25 min | **GOSPEL CATEGORY** | Student gospel performers take the stage. After student acts: Gospel Hypeman (Experience Hub) delivers a special 10-minute gospel hype performance |
| **12:15 PM** | 20 min | **HYPE SET #2 — The Experience Hub** | Full Experience Hub: Hype Ola + Gospel Hypeman + DJ Carian. High energy crowd session. Students dancing. Rania on reels full focus |
| **12:35 PM** | 5 min | **Category Intro: Poetry** | MCs introduce poetry. Set the mood. Get the crowd leaning in |
| **12:40 PM** | 30 min | **POETRY CATEGORY** | Student poets perform. After student acts: Guest poet Kalamu Nyeusi performs. MCs react and celebrate each poet |

**Phase 3 — Drama ****&**** Midday ****Break  |****  1:10 PM – 2:10 PM**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **1:10 PM** | 10 min | **Category Intro: Drama** | MCs set the stage for drama. Hype the crowd for theatrical performances |
| **1:20 PM** | 30 min | **DRAMA CATEGORY** | Students perform dramatic pieces. MCs introduce each act. Full camera coverage |
| **1:50 PM** | 20 min | **Midday Break — DJ Xavi Set** | DJ Xavi holds the floor. Students refresh. MCs mingle with crowd for social content. Camera crew captures candid b-roll |

**Phase 4 — Talent Showcase Part ****2  |****  2:10 PM – 3:45 PM**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **2:10 PM** | 5 min | **Category Intro: Rap** | MCs go hard on the intro. DJ Xavi drops a beat tease. Hype Ola calls the crowd up |
| **2:15 PM** | 35 min | **RAP CATEGORY** | Student rappers perform. After student acts: Guest artists Cosign Black then Denzz — back-to-back live sets |
| **2:50 PM** | 10 min | **Pre-Dance Set — Experience Hub Dancers** | Nyarangi + Dancers take the stage for 10 minutes BEFORE the dance category. Sets the bar. Hypes the crowd before student competitors take over |
| **3:00 PM** | 5 min | **Category Intro: Dance** | MCs take over from dancers. 'Now it's YOUR turn!' |
| **3:05 PM** | 40 min | **DANCE CATEGORY** | Students compete. MCs and DJ Xavi keep energy electric. Guest artists Supee Crew and XLIII (trio) each perform during the segment. Kahuti performs in flex slot. Crowd reaction moments captured |

**Phase 5 — ****Modeling**** Showcase (****Driq**** ****Academy)  |****  3:45 PM – 4:40 PM**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **3:45 PM** | 5 min | **Modeling**** Introduction** | MCs introduce Driq Academy. DJ Xavi drops runway music. Stage set for the runway |
| **3:50 PM** | 10 min | **RUNWAY: Cultural / Traditional Wear** | Students and Driq Academy models walk in traditional African attire. Crowd celebrates identity and heritage |
| **4:00 PM** | 10 min | **RUNWAY: Creative Wear** | Original, inventive, expressive fashion. Students interpret 'creative' their way |
| **4:10 PM** | 10 min | **RUNWAY: Cosplay** | Characters, heroes, villains, anime. DJ Xavi matches the mood. Crowd energy peaks |
| **4:20 PM** | 10 min | **RUNWAY: Professional Wear** | Power dressing. Corporate and formal. Students walk with confidence. MCs commentate |
| **4:30 PM** | 5 min | **Special Guest Walk** | Mr & Mrs Kasarani + Mr & Mrs Ruiru (Driq Academy) close the runway with a prestige walk. Standing ovation moment |
| **4:35 PM** | 5 min | **MC Bridge + HYPE SET #3** | Full Experience Hub final hype set. DJ Carian + Hype Ola + Gospel Hypeman + Dancers. Biggest energy of the day. Crowd peak before awards |

**Phase 6 — Church, Teacher Competition ****&**** ****Closing  |****  4:40 PM – 5:30 PM**

| **TIME** | **DURATION** | **ACTIVITY** | **DETAILS / NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **4:40 PM** | 10 min | **Church Moment — Mental Health Sermon** | MCs introduce the church guest. Short powerful sermon on mental health, resilience and purpose. Cameras on crowd reactions |
| **4:50 PM** | 10 min | **Teacher Competition — Best Dancer on Staff** | MCs challenge teachers to step up. DJ Xavi drops the beat. Students cheer for their favourite teacher. MCs and Principal judge together. Winner selected on crowd energy |
| **5:00 PM** | 15 min | **Awards Ceremony** | MCs anchor the awards. Category winners called up one by one. Principal presents Best Teacher award. Urban News certificates and gift hampers distributed on stage. Group photo |
| **5:15 PM** | 5 min | **Closing Remarks** | Eugine & Lucy close the show. Thank the school, Principal, all performers. Announce PPP TV broadcast date. 'Urban Tour will be back!' |
| **5:20 PM** | 10 min | **Post-Show Content Capture** | Winner interview on camera. Behind-the-scenes with top performers. Outro piece-to-camera by Eugine. Rania captures final reels. Group photos |

**4.  AWARDS BREAKDOWN**

All student awards are: Certificate + Gift Hamper for winners. Certificate only for runners-up. No cash prizes. The Urban News / PPP TV feature is the headline prize for every category winner.

| **CATEGORY** | **POSITION** | **AWARD** | **NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Choir** | Winner (Group) | Certificate + Gift Hamper | 1 cert per group + 1 shared hamper |
| **Gospel** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Urban News feature on PPP TV |
| **Gospel** | 1st Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Gospel** | 2nd Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Poetry** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Urban News feature on PPP TV |
| **Poetry** | 1st Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Poetry** | 2nd Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Drama** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Urban News feature on PPP TV |
| **Drama** | 1st Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Drama** | 2nd Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Rap** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Urban News feature on PPP TV |
| **Rap** | 1st Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Rap** | 2nd Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Dance** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Urban News feature on PPP TV |
| **Dance** | 1st Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Dance** | 2nd Runner-Up | Certificate | Participation recognition |
| **Modeling**** — Mrs of Institution** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Presented on camera by Principal, Eugine & Lucy |
| **Modeling**** — Mrs of Institution** | 1st Runner-Up | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Driq Academy + Urban News recognition |
| **Modeling**** — Mrs of Institution** | 2nd Runner-Up | Certificate | HOD Entertainment |
| **Modeling**** — Most Creative Wear** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Special award — any category eligible |
| **Modeling**** — Cultural / Traditional Wear** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Best traditional presentation |
| **Modeling**** — Cosplay** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Best cosplay presentation |
| **Modeling**** — Professional Wear** | Winner | Certificate + Gift Hamper | Best professional presentation |
| **Principal — School Partner Award** | N/A | Framed Certificate + Gift Hamper | Presented at Opening Ceremony on camera |
| **Best Teacher — Dance Competition** | Winner | Certificate | Presented by the Principal on stage |

**Total Certificates ****&**** Gift Hampers Required**

| **ITEM** | **CERTIFICATES** | **GIFT HAMPERS** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Category Winners — Gospel, Poetry, Drama, Rap, Dance (x5) | 5 | 5 |
| Runners-Up — 1st & 2nd across 5 categories | 10 | 0 |
| Choir — Group Certificate | 1 | 1 |
| Modeling — Mrs of Institution (Winner + 1st RU + 2nd RU) | 3 | 2 |
| Modeling — Most Creative Wear | 1 | 1 |
| Modeling — Cultural, Cosplay, Professional Wear Winners | 3 | 3 |
| Principal — Partner School Award | 1 | 1 |
| Best Teacher — Dance Competition | 1 | 1 |
| **TOTAL** | **25** | **14** |

**5.  GUEST ARTISTS ****&**** PERFORMERS**

| **ARTIST / ACT** | **CATEGORY SLOT** | **PERF. TIME** | **NOTES** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Kalamu Nyeusi | Poetry | ~10 min | Performs after student poets — closes the poetry category |
| Gospel Hypeman | Gospel + Hype Set #2 | ~10 min | Closes gospel category, then leads Experience Hub for Hype Set #2 |
| Cosign Black | Rap Category | ~10 min | Performs after student rappers |
| Denzz | Rap Category | ~8 min | Back-to-back with Cosign Black |
| Supee Crew | Dance Category | ~10 min | Performs during dance segment |
| XLIII (Trio) | Dance Category | ~8 min | Performs during dance segment |
| Kahuti | Dance / Flex Slot | ~10 min | Dance segment or flex to any high-energy slot as needed |
| Nyarangi + Dancers | Pre-Dance Set | 10 min | Must perform exactly 10 minutes BEFORE the dance category opens |
| Hype Ola | Hype Sets #1, #2, #3 | Throughout | On call for all three hype sets across the full day |
| DJ Carian | Hype Sets #2 & #3 | Throughout | Joins Hype Ola for Sets #2 and #3 |

**6.  CONTENT OUTPUT PER SCHOOL VISIT**

| **CONTENT TYPE** | **PLATFORM** | **RESPONSIBLE** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Full Event Episode (25-30 min edit) | PPP TV Kenya | Bico, George, Lallez |
| 8-12 Short-Form Clips (Reels/TikTok/Shorts) | Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts | Rania |
| Winner Interviews | YouTube + Social Media | George + Rania |
| Behind-the-Scenes Reel | Instagram Stories + Reels | Rania |
| School & Crowd Reaction Montage | TikTok + YouTube Shorts | Rania |
| Teaser Clip — Next School Visit | All Platforms | Rania + Lallez |
| Photography Gallery | Instagram Grid + School Gift | All Camera Crew |

**7.  MC MANDATE — EUGINE MICAH ****&**** LUCY OGUNDE**

| **#** | **RULE** |
| --- | --- |
| **1** | The MCs are on stage from the first second to the last second of the event — no exceptions. |
| **2** | No guest, performer, or official leaves the stage without being introduced by Eugine or Lucy first. |
| **3** | MCs bridge every segment — no dead air. DJ Xavi provides music cover if MCs need a moment. |
| **4** | MCs introduce every category, every performer, every official, and every award recipient. |
| **5** | MCs manage crowd energy — call-and-response, hype moments, and crowd direction throughout. |
| **6** | MCs are the face of Urban News on this day. Everything they do is content. |
| **7** | If a technical issue arises, MCs hold the crowd. Pauline handles sound. MCs never break character. |
| **8** | The stage presence of Eugine and Lucy is the brand. No one overshadows the hosts. |

# **THE ALIGNMENT**

Co-operative Bank of Kenya has built its brand on the idea of togetherness and community. Your banking products for youth include the Co-op Kwa Jirani agency banking model, mobile banking, and financial literacy programmes. You are present in communities across Kenya through over 6,000 Co-op Kwa Jirani agents.

Urban Gang Tour goes into communities across Kenya through their schools. We run a financial literacy pod called "Own Your Money" at every school we visit. We broadcast everything on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers. We are the kind of programme that turns students into future Co-op Bank customers.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING**

| **Financial Literacy Pod** | Co-op Bank owns the "Own Your Money" pod. Your facilitator leads a 30-minute session on savings, budgeting, and financial responsibility. Students learn about banking from Co-op before any other bank reaches them |
| --- | --- |
| **Branded Booth** | A Co-op Bank information stand at every event. Promote youth savings accounts, mobile banking, and financial products |
| **Stage and TV** | Co-op logo on the backdrop. Acknowledged as Official Banking Partner in every PPP TV episode (up to 8M viewers) |
| **Social Media** | Tagged across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts. Authentic content showing Co-op engaging youth |
| **Tree Planting** | Co-brand the tree planting session if aligned with Co-op's CSR environmental commitments |

**Contact: **customerservice@co-opbank.co.ke | +254 20 2776000

**NALA x UGT**  |  Partnership Proposal

**NALA NOODLES**

x

**URBAN GANG TOUR**

**OFFICIAL NOODLE PARTNER
PROPOSAL**

Gift of Potential. Gift of Purpose. Gift of Nala.

euginemicah@gmail.com | @urban_newsgang | April 2026

# **20% MARKET SHARE BY 2026. THE CLOCK IS TICKING.**

Nala Noodles launched in 2023 through Kapa Foods Innovations Limited, a joint venture between Kapa Oil Refineries and Sojitz Corporation. The target was clear: capture 20% of the East African instant noodle market by 2026.

That deadline is this year.

Indomie has been in Kenya since 2009. They have 17 years of head start. They have brand loyalty that runs deep. You cannot outspend them in advertising. You cannot out-distribute them overnight. But you can do something they are not doing: show up in schools, put Nala in students' hands, and build the kind of brand memory that shelf space alone will never create.

Urban Gang Tour is the fastest, most cost-effective way to do exactly that.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **WHY NALA NEEDS UGT**

**You cannot outspend Indomie. **But you can out-experience them. A student who eats Nala while watching her friend win a dance competition will remember that moment forever. That is brand equity you cannot buy with a billboard

**"****Nala****"**** means ****"****gift****"**** in Swahili. **Our events are literally a gift to students. The name alignment is perfect for co-branded messaging: "The Gift of Talent. The Gift of Purpose. The Gift of Nala"

**You are manufactured by Kapa Oil on Mombasa Road. **Our church partner Destiny Life Church is in Syokimau. Our team operates from this area. We are neighbours. Local partnership, national reach

**Your Thai technical partner brings quality. **Thai President Foods developed Nala's recipes for East African taste preferences. The product is good. It just needs to be in the right hands at the right moment. UGT creates that moment

**20% by 2026 is still possible. **But only if you move now. Every school UGT visits this term is a market you can claim before anyone else. 3 schools confirmed. More booking. Countrywide

# **WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING**

| **Official Noodle Partner** | Nala becomes the exclusive noodle brand at UGT events. No competing noodle brands on site |
| --- | --- |
| **Product Supply** | Nala supplies noodles at wholesale or sponsored rates for Urban Gang Snacks. Students buy affordable Nala all day |
| **Branded Booth** | A Nala tent or stand at every event. Branding, sampling, student engagement |
| **Live Cooking Station** | A live Nala cooking station. Sizzling noodles on camera. TikTok gold. The most shareable food content format on social media |
| **Gift Hamper Inclusion** | Nala packs in every winner's gift hamper. On stage, on camera, on PPP TV |
| **Co-Branded Messaging** | "The Gift of Talent. The Gift of Purpose. The Gift of Nala." Plays on the Swahili meaning. Used on stage, in social media, on PPP TV |
| **Stage Branding** | Nala logo on backdrop, teardrops, programmes. PPP TV credits in every broadcast |
| **Social Media** | 8 to 12 clips per event. Cooking station, hamper moments, students eating Nala. Tagged across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts |
| **Buy Kenya Build Kenya** | MCs highlight Nala as a proudly Kenyan product made right here in Nairobi. Students support local. That narrative on PPP TV is more powerful than any import brand story |
| **Photobooth Co-Brand** | Nala logo on Urban Gang Moments instant prints. Students take your brand home on a photo they keep |

# **SUPPORT OPTIONS**

| **Option A: Product Only** | Nala supplies noodles for Urban Gang Snacks and gift hampers. Zero cash. Product at wholesale or sponsored rates |
| --- | --- |
| **Option B: Product + Booth** | Everything in A, plus a branded Nala booth at each event. KES 20,000 to 50,000 per school for booth logistics |
| **Option C: Product + Booth + Cooking Station** | Everything in B, plus a live cooking station with branded aprons and signage. KES 50,000 to 80,000 per school |
| **Option D: Full Partnership** | Everything in C, plus KES 50,000 to 100,000 per school towards stage and sound production. Total: KES 100,000 to 180,000 per school. KES 300,000 to 540,000 for all 3 schools. Maximum branding, PPP TV, social media, cooking station, photobooth co-brand, and the "Gift of Nala" co-branded campaign |

# **WHAT NALA GETS**

| **Market Share Acceleration** | Direct product placement with the next generation of noodle buyers. Students who try Nala at UGT become Nala customers. At scale. At speed |
| --- | --- |
| **National TV** | PPP TV, up to 8 million viewers. Nala credited in every episode. The "Gift of Nala" messaging broadcast nationally |
| **Social Media Content** | 8 to 12 clips per event. Live cooking station, hamper reveals, students eating Nala. Authentic Gen Z content that gets shared |
| **Competitive Positioning** | Every school UGT visits is a market Nala claims first. Before Indomie can set foot in that school, Nala was already there |
| **Buy Kenya Build Kenya** | The proudly Kenyan narrative told on national TV and social media. Nala is not just another noodle. It is the Kenyan noodle |
| **Kapa Ecosystem** | This partnership can extend to other Kapa products. Kasuku cooking oil for the snack shop. Rina margarine. Tilly. The relationship grows beyond noodles |
| **Direct Sales** | Urban Gang Snacks sells Nala directly to students. Revenue, not just visibility |
| **Repeat Nationwide** | 3 schools this term. More next term. Countrywide. Compounding every term |

| **Term 3+** | Expanding countrywide |

# **THE CHANDARIA OPPORTUNITY**

We are proposing that Chandaria Industries becomes the Official Hygiene Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is exactly what that looks like at every school we visit:

## **1. Sanitary Pad Distribution**

Velvex sanitary pads and Rosy Girl pads distributed to students at every girls school. This can happen through the counselling tent run by Destiny Life Church counsellors, through a dedicated Chandaria hygiene booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand out to winners on stage. This is your existing CSR work amplified by three cameras, national TV, and social media.

## **2. Chandaria Hygiene Booth**

A branded Chandaria tent or booth at every event. Your space to educate students on menstrual hygiene, hand hygiene, and personal care. Display the full Velvex and Rosy range. You can staff it with your own team or we can manage it for you. This booth sits next to the main stage where foot traffic is guaranteed.

## **3. Chandaria Products in the Counselling Tent**

Velvex hand sanitizer, wet wipes, cotton wool, and facial tissue provided for the counselling tent and backstage areas. Branded and visible. Students and staff using your products throughout the day in the space where conversations about wellbeing happen.

## **4. Gift Hamper Integration**

Every talent category winner receives a certificate and a gift hamper on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. We want Chandaria products inside every hamper. Velvex sanitary pads, wet wipes, hand sanitizer, cotton wool. Your products in the hands of winners during the biggest moment of the day. That moment becomes a photo, a reel, and a TV broadcast.

## **5. Stage Backdrop and Venue Branding**

Chandaria's logo on the main T-shaped stage backdrop alongside UGT and partner branding. Chandaria branded teardrops and banners around the venue. Chandaria's logo on the printed event programme given to every student and teacher.

## **6. PPP TV Broadcast Credits**

Chandaria Industries acknowledged as Official Hygiene Partner in every Urban News episode. That is up to 8 million viewers per broadcast seeing your commitment to girls education and hygiene.

## **7. Social Media Coverage**

Every event produces 8 to 12 short form clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The hygiene booth, the pad distribution, the counselling tent moments. All of it becomes authentic content showing Chandaria serving real girls in real schools. This is not staged. This is real, and Gen Z can tell the difference.

## **8. Urban Gang Moments Photobooth Co-Branding**

We run a photobooth called Urban Gang Moments where students pay KES 20 for an instant printed photo. We can co-brand these prints with Chandaria's logo. Every student takes home a physical photo with your brand on it. That photo goes on their wall, on their WhatsApp, on their Instagram.

# **THE INDOMIE HEROES MODEL. NOW IN KENYA.**

In Nigeria, Indomie runs the Indomie Independence Day Award (IIDA). Every year, Dufil Prima Foods searches for children who have shown exceptional bravery, and celebrates them on a national stage. The IIDA has become one of Nigeria's most celebrated CSR initiatives. It has been running for over 12 editions. It is part of the reason Indomie consistently ranks as one of the top consumer brands in Nigeria, ahead of Coca-Cola and Maggi.

Urban Gang Tour does the same thing in Kenya. We go into schools, discover talented and courageous students, and celebrate them on stage, on camera, and on national TV. The format is different. The spirit is identical.

This proposal is about bringing the Indomie Heroes model to Kenya through Urban Gang Tour.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **THE PROPOSAL: THREE LAYERS**

## **Layer 1: Official Noodle Partner**

Indomie becomes the official noodle brand at every Urban Gang Tour event. Here is what that looks like:

| **Product Supply** | Indomie supplies noodles at wholesale or sponsored rates for sale through Urban Gang Snacks, our on-site snack shop. Students buy affordable Indomie all day |
| --- | --- |
| **Branded Booth** | An Indomie tent or stand at every event. Branding, sampling, direct engagement with students |
| **Live Cooking Station** | A live Indomie cooking station where students watch their noodles being prepared. This becomes TikTok content. Sizzling noodles on camera is the most shareable food content on social media |
| **Gift Hamper Inclusion** | Indomie packs inside every winner's gift hamper. On stage, on camera, on PPP TV to 8 million viewers |
| **Stage Branding** | Indomie logo on the backdrop, teardrops, printed programmes |
| **PPP TV Credits** | Indomie acknowledged as Official Noodle Partner in every broadcast |
| **Social Media** | 8 to 12 clips per event. Students eating Indomie, the cooking station sizzling, hampers being opened. All tagged across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts |

## **Layer 2: The Indomie UGT Award**

This is the big idea. Inspired by the IIDA in Nigeria, we co-create a special award at every school:

| **The Award** | The Indomie UGT Award for Courage and Character. Given to one student per school who has shown exceptional bravery, kindness, or leadership. Nominated by teachers and peers |
| --- | --- |
| **Who Presents It** | The MCs (Eugine and Lucy) announce it from the main stage. "This award is brought to you by Indomie." The student is called up. Applause. Certificate. Indomie branded gift hamper. Photo |
| **Why It Matters** | In Nigeria, the IIDA made Indomie synonymous with bravery and values. In Kenya, the Indomie UGT Award does the same. Your brand stops being just noodles. It becomes a symbol of courage in the eyes of every student in every school we visit |
| **PPP TV Feature** | The Indomie UGT Award winner is featured in the Urban News episode. Their story told to 8 million viewers. Indomie's name attached to an emotional, inspiring moment on national television |
| **Annual Compilation** | At the end of each year, we produce a compilation video of all Indomie UGT Award winners. Aired on PPP TV. Posted across all social media. A standalone Indomie campaign built entirely from UGT content |

## **Layer 3: Competitive Defence**

Nala Noodles launched in 2023 backed by Kapa Oil Refineries and Sojitz Corporation from Japan. They are targeting 20% of the East African noodle market by 2026. That deadline is this year. They are manufactured locally. They have the distribution power of Kapa Oil behind them.

Indomie has been in Kenya since 2009. You have 17 years of brand equity. But brand equity fades if someone else is showing up where the customers are. UGT puts Indomie in the hands of the next generation of noodle buyers before Nala can get there. Every school we visit is a market that Indomie owns first. That is not advertising. That is territorial defence.

KES 300,000 to 600,000 for all 3 schools. Indomie gets maximum branding, the award, the cooking station, PPP TV, and social media |

# **WHAT INDOMIE GETS**

| **Next-Gen Customer Acquisition** | Students eat Indomie during the best day of their school year. That memory creates a lifelong brand association. You are not buying eyeballs. You are buying loyalty |
| --- | --- |
| **National TV** | PPP TV, up to 8 million viewers. Indomie credited in every episode |
| **Social Media Content** | 8 to 12 clips per event. Live cooking station, hamper reveals, award moments. Content Gen Z actually watches and shares |

| **Direct Sales Channel** | Urban Gang Snacks sells your product directly to students. Revenue, not just visibility |

# **THE ALIGNMENT**

Mastercard Foundation's Young Africa Works strategy aims to enable 7 million young people in Kenya to access dignified and fulfilling work by 2030. 5 million of those must be young women. Your Kenya office in Nairobi is actively seeking youth-led organisations to partner with. Your June 2025 Request for Expression of Interest explicitly called for organisations that can recruit and manage youth-led organisations to accelerate work opportunities.

Urban Gang Tour is a youth-led organisation that goes into Kenyan high schools with 30 professionals and delivers talent competitions, mentorship pods on financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, and mental health, and awards concerts broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers. All three of our confirmed schools for Term 2 are girls schools.

# **PARTNERSHIP OPPORTUNITIES**

We welcome partners who are intentional, aligned with our vision, and interested in long-term collaboration. Here are the ways you can come on board:

## **1. Segment / Category Sponsorship**

Own a specific segment of the event day. Your brand becomes synonymous with that category across every school, every city, every event. Options include: Rap Category Sponsor, Dance Category Sponsor, Poetry Category Sponsor, Modelling Showcase Sponsor, Choir Sponsor, Drama Sponsor, Gospel Session Sponsor, Mathematics & Logic Competition Sponsor, Urban Pods Sponsor (one or more of the five pod topics), Teacher Appreciation Sponsor, Tree Planting Session Sponsor, and any other segment.

**What You Get: **Stage mentions, branded category intro graphics, branded certificates and awards for that category, TV and social media credits, logo on backdrop and teardrops, on-camera brand moment during your segment.

## **2. Awards ****&**** Prize Sponsorship**

Sponsor the gift hampers, certificates, trophies, sashes, crowns, or unique prizes for winners. Your brand is physically in the hands of every winner and visible in every award photo and video clip.

**What You Get: **Logo on certificates, branded gift hampers with your products, on-stage brand mention during awards, TV and social media coverage of winners holding your branded prizes.

## **3. Booth ****&**** Activation Sponsorship**

Set up a branded booth or activation zone at the event venue. Engage directly with students, sample products, run contests, or offer information sessions. This is prime face-to-face time with thousands of young consumers.

**What You Get: **Dedicated tent/booth space, branded signage, student foot traffic, on-camera mention, social media feature, and the ability to sell, sample, or interact directly with students.

## **4. Branding ****&**** Visibility Partnership**

Your logo and branding across UGT’s physical and digital assets. This includes the stage backdrop, teardrops, event banners, printed programmes, certificates, social media posts, TV broadcast credits on PPP TV, and Urban News mentions.

**What You Get: **Maximum brand visibility at every event, across every platform, in every piece of content UGT produces.

## **5. Vendor Partnership**

Bring your products to sell directly to students and event attendees. Whether you sell snacks, beverages, stationery, accessories, or any other product, UGT events give you a captive audience of thousands. Destiny Life Church is also encouraged to bring vendors, so you will be part of a vibrant vendor ecosystem.

**What You Get: **Dedicated vending space, foot traffic, on-camera mentions, and the opportunity to build brand loyalty with young consumers.

## **6. PR Package Partnership**

UGT’s campaigns are oriented at helping our partners gain visibility and awareness. We create custom PR packages tailored to your brand, leveraging Urban News on PPP TV, our social media platforms, and live event coverage to push your brand far beyond the event itself.

**What You Get: **TV mentions and adverts on PPP TV, dedicated social media posts, branded content, event reels featuring your brand, and a structured PR campaign built around your partnership.

## **7. Unique Element Sponsorship**

Create something entirely new and own it. Ideas include: a branded stage element, a special award category named after your brand, a VIP experience, a branded challenge or game for students, a digital literacy station, a career expo booth, or anything creative that aligns with UGT’s mission.

**What You Get: **A bespoke activation designed around your brand, full creative collaboration with the UGT team, and maximum differentiation from other partners.

## **8. Musician / Artist Facilitation**

Sponsor a performing musician or artist for the event. Your brand introduces the headline act and is associated with the biggest moment of the day.

**What You Get: **On-stage brand introduction of the artist, branded stage moment, TV and social media credits, and association with the most memorable performance of the day.

## **9. University / College / Tertiary Institution Partnership**

We specifically seek private and public universities, colleges, and tertiary institutions to come on board as education partners. Provide career guidance, scholarship information, campus tours, or student recruitment activations at UGT events.

**What You Get: **Direct access to high school students at the point of career decision-making, branded booth, speaking slot in a relevant Urban Pod, TV and social media coverage, and long-term pipeline to future students.

# **THE STRATEGY: WHY THIS KIT IS DIFFERENT**

This kit is built on one insight that separates UGT from every other Kenyan youth event: UGT has TWO audiences on social media, and both must be served simultaneously.

## **Audience 1: The Students (Gen Z, 13–25)**

They discover brands through TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube. They scroll past anything that feels forced, corporate, or fake. They want raw, real, funny, and relatable content. Comedy and memes are their #1 content type. BTS gets 4x more engagement than polished branded posts. They value purpose-driven brands — 53% of Gen Z wants brands that support mental health. They spend 95 minutes per day on TikTok and 58% use Instagram daily. They are the ones who make UGT go viral.

## **Audience 2: The Sponsors, Partners ****&**** Corporates**

They are watching the same social media pages to evaluate whether UGT is worth partnering with. They look for professionalism, scale, production quality, and proof of audience. They need to see numbers, energy, and evidence that their brand will get value. They make decisions based on what they see in your content, not just what’s in your proposal. They are the ones who fund UGT.

## **The Solution: One Feed, Two Signals**

**Every piece of content must be entertaining enough for a 15-year-old AND credible enough for a Safaricom marketing director. This is exactly what Coachella does — their feed is simultaneously cool to attendees and impressive to billion-dollar brand partners. This is the standard UGT must hit.**

# **BRAND VOICE ****&**** PERSONALITY**

## **The UGT Persona**

UGT speaks like a big brother/sister who made it and came back to show you the way. Not a teacher. Not a brand. Not a government programme. A person you trust who happens to have a stage, a camera crew, and national TV.

| **Always** | Bold, energetic, warm, purposeful, raw, Gen Z-fluent, aspirational, community-first |
| --- | --- |
| **Sometimes** | Funny, meme-literate, Swahili-mixed, hype, emotional, vulnerable, celebratory |
| **Never** | Corporate, stiff, preachy, condescending, generic, overly polished, desperate, formal |
| **Motto Use** | Work “From Potential to Purpose” into captions naturally. Don’t force it every time. Let it breathe. When it appears, it should land |
| **Language** | English + Sheng/Swahili naturally mixed. Speak like Kenyan youth actually speak. No code-switching for corporates — the authenticity IS the professionalism |

# **CONTENT PILLARS**

Everything UGT posts falls into one of these 6 pillars. Each pillar serves both audiences:

| **1. THE STAGE** | Talent performances, hype sets, crowd reactions, awards moments, guest artist sets. This is the money content. High energy, cinematic angles, crowd going crazy. For students: “I want to be there.” For sponsors: “That’s reach.” |
| --- | --- |
| **2. THE JOURNEY** | Student stories, winner follow-ups, Urban Gang Club moments, before/after transformations, pod conversations. The purpose content. For students: “They see me.” For sponsors: “This is real impact.” |
| **3. THE VIBE** | BTS at 5 AM setup, crew briefings, canteen food reviews, MC prep, DJ sound checks, team banter, Eugine and Lucy being themselves. The authenticity content. Raw, unfiltered, real. For students: “They’re like us.” For sponsors: “This team is professional.” |
| **4. THE DROP** | Announcements: next school reveal, artist lineup, new partner, merch drop, ticket or registration info, calendar updates. The FOMO content. Designed to be shared, saved, and screenshotted. For students: “I need to be there.” For sponsors: “They’re growing fast.” |
| **5. THE SERMON** | Mental health moments, mentor quotes, pod wisdom, church moments, Tai’s environmental messages, teacher appreciation, motivational content. The purpose content. For students: “This matters.” For sponsors: “This is CSR gold.” |
| **6. THE FLEX** | Production shots, crowd aerial views, stage setups, team size, TV episode stills, PPP TV broadcast moments, partner logos in action. The credibility content. For sponsors: “This is a real operation.” For students: “This is the biggest thing at my school.” |

## **TikTok Content Strategy**

TikTok content is NOT the same as Instagram content. TikTok rewards raw, fast, surprising, funny, and trend-aware content. The first 1.5 seconds decide everything.

**Content Types That Work (Ranked by Priority)**

| **1. Crowd Reactions** | Film the CROWD, not the stage. Students losing their minds during a rap battle, dance-off, or hype set. 15–20 sec. This is the #1 TikTok format for event accounts. The crowd IS the content |
| --- | --- |
| **2. Talent Clips** | Best 15-sec moment from each performer. Use trending audio when possible. Caption with the performer’s name. Students want to find themselves |
| **3. Transformation / Before-After** | 5 AM empty field → 10 AM packed stage. Student at registration → student winning award. Setup → showtime. These get saved and shared |
| **4. POV / Day-in-the-Life** | “POV: You’re an MC at Kenya’s biggest school event” (Eugine or Lucy POV). “POV: You’re a student and Urban Gang Tour just pulled up to your school” |
| **5. Comedy / Memes** | Funny team moments. MC fails and recoveries. Students reacting to their own performances. Teacher dance competition highlights. Comedy is Gen Z’s #1 content type |
| **6. Behind-the-Scenes** | 5 AM call time. Sound check drama. Last-minute stage fixes. Food at the school canteen. Raw, messy, real — never polished |
| **7. Trending Audio Remixes** | Take trending TikTok sounds and layer UGT footage over them. This is how you hack the algorithm. Rania and Nyarangi should track trends weekly |
| **8. Mini-Documentaries** | 60–90 sec mini-docs about a student’s journey, a pod topic, or the UGT mission. These build the “purpose” side for sponsors |

## **TikTok Posting Frequency**

**Normal Weeks: **4–5 posts per week minimum

**Event Week: **2–3 posts PER DAY (morning hype, live clips, post-event)

**Event Day: **Minimum 5 TikToks throughout the day (setup, opening, talent, crowd, awards)

**Post-Event: **Drip content daily for 2 weeks after each school (you have 8–12 clips per event — don’t dump them all at once)

# **CAMPAIGN CONCEPTS**

These are ready-to-execute campaign ideas modelled on how Coachella, AFROPUNK, Revolve Festival, and top Gen Z brands drive social engagement.

## **Campaign 1: #NextStopLoading**

**What: **Pre-event hype campaign. 7 days before each school visit, start a countdown

**Day 7: **Black screen, just the text “Next stop loading...” with a location pin emoji. No school name yet

**Day 5: **Silhouette of the school entrance or aerial view. Still no name. Caption: “Are you ready?”

**Day 3: **REVEAL the school name. Full branded graphic with date and time. Caption: “[School Name], Urban Gang Tour is pulling up 🔥”

**Day 1: **5 AM setup timelapse or crew packing the van. “Tomorrow, we change lives.”

**Why: **This is the Coachella playbook — anticipation drives engagement. Students will screenshot, share in WhatsApp groups, and tag friends. Sponsors see the hype building

## **Campaign 2: #UGTMoments**

**What: **User-generated content campaign. After every event, encourage students to post their own photos/videos with #UGTMoments

**Incentive: **Repost the best student content on UGT’s main feed. Being reposted by UGT (with 400K+ daily reach) is the incentive

**Photobooth Tie-In: **Urban Gang Moments photobooth prints have #UGTMoments and @urbangangtour watermarked. Every student who takes a photo becomes a brand ambassador

**Why: **Coachella’s #Coachella hashtag generates millions of posts because attendees WANT to be part of the narrative. UGT should own this in the Kenyan high school space

## **Campaign 3: #PotentialToPurpose**

**What: **Long-running impact series. Follow one student per school from their first UGT event through the follow-up programme

**Format: **60–90 sec mini-documentary style Reels/TikToks. Filmed during pods, competition, and then at the church conference day

**Why: **This is the content that sponsors share in their boardrooms. It proves ROI. It proves impact. And for students, it’s aspirational — they see someone like them being celebrated nationally

## **Campaign 4: #TeacherOfTheYear**

**What: **After the teacher dance competition, post the winning teacher’s clip with #TeacherOfTheYear

**Why: **Teachers share this content like wildfire. It gets to school WhatsApp groups, parent groups, and education media. It positions UGT as pro-education, not just entertainment. Sponsors in the education sector (publishers, stationery, edtech) will notice

## **Campaign 5: #UGTDanceChallenge**

**What: **Create a signature UGT dance move (choreographed by Nyarangi and the Experience Hub dancers). Teach it at every school. Film students doing it

**TikTok: **Post it with a trending sound. Challenge students to post their version. Best submissions get reposted

**Why: **This is how you build a movement. A dance challenge is shareable, repeatable, and puts UGT in the TikTok algorithm. When a challenge goes viral, it reaches millions of people who’ve never heard of UGT

# **EVENT-DAY SOCIAL MEDIA PLAYBOOK**

This is the minute-by-minute social media deployment for every school visit. Rania leads. Nyarangi assists. Eugine/Lucy contribute during their TV filming segments.

| **5:00 AM** | STORY: Dark stage, one light on. Caption: “It’s 5 AM. Stage isn’t built yet. By 10 AM, this place will be different. 🔥” |
| --- | --- |
| **7:00 AM** | STORY: Timelapse of stage going up. No caption needed — the visual speaks |

# **VISUAL RULES FOR SOCIAL**

| **Primary Look** | Dark backgrounds (#1A1A1A). White text. Magenta + orange accents. Bold. High contrast. NOT pastel. NOT corporate |
| --- | --- |
| **Fonts on Graphics** | Bold sans-serif only. All caps for headlines. Mixed case for body. Never script or decorative fonts |
| **Photo Treatment** | High contrast. Slightly desaturated with magenta/orange colour grade. Think concert photography, not school newsletter |
| **Video Treatment** | Fast cuts (1–2 sec per cut for TikTok). Dramatic transitions. Bass-heavy audio. Crowd reactions as B-roll |
| **Text on Video** | Bold white text with black outline or magenta highlight. Bottom-third placement. 3–5 words max per frame |
| **Carousel Style** | Dark background. One photo per slide. Text overlay in magenta/orange. Clean, magazine-editorial feel |
| **Stories Style** | Raw and real. Minimal text. Let the moment speak. Use Instagram’s native tools sparingly — stickers and polls for engagement only |
| **Profile Consistency** | Same profile picture across ALL platforms. Same username. Same bio structure. No exceptions |
| **Logo Use** | Logo appears on all branded graphics (posters, announcements, recap carousels). Never stretched, never on busy backgrounds |

# **CONTENT TEAM RESPONSIBILITIES**

| **Rania Martin** | PRIMARY social content creator. Shoots all Reels and TikToks during events. 8–12 clips per school. Edits same-day for immediate posting. Manages Instagram Stories on event days. Assisted by Nyarangi |
| --- | --- |
| **Nyarangi** | SECONDARY content support. Films BTS. Captures crowd reactions. Assists Rania with reels. Creates dance challenge content |
| **Eugine Micah** | Social media co-manager. Writes captions, strategy, and announcements. Creates posters/graphics. Shoots TV segments that double as social content. Approves all posts |
| **Lucy Ogunde** | Social media co-manager. Community engagement — responds to comments and DMs. Shoots TV segments that double as social content |
| **George Morgan** | Photography lead. Event photos for Instagram carousels and Facebook albums. Winner interview footage for YouTube/IGTV |
| **Tony Lallez** | TV segment footage shared to social. Teaser clips for upcoming schools. YouTube episode uploads |

# **METRICS THAT MATTER**

Track these monthly. They tell you if the strategy is working:

| **Reel Views (IG)** | Target: 10K+ average per Reel within 3 months |
| --- | --- |
| **TikTok Views** | Target: 50K+ average per TikTok within 3 months (TikTok has wider organic reach) |
| **Saves ****&**** Shares** | More important than likes. Saves = sponsors see value. Shares = students spread the word |
| **Follower Growth** | Target: +500/month on IG, +1000/month on TikTok in first 6 months |
| **UGC Volume** | #UGTMoments posts from students after each event. Target: 50+ per school |
| **DM Enquiries** | Partnership/sponsorship DMs are the leading indicator of revenue. Track weekly |
| **Comment Quality** | Are students tagging friends? Are sponsors asking “How do we partner?”? Read the comments |

# **OUR STORY**

Every young person in Kenya has potential. The question is: who will help them find their purpose?

Urban Gang Tour (UGT) was born from that question. We are a high school talent search, mentorship, awards, and concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan institutions — not to perform at students, but to perform with them. We discover talent, nurture dreams, build confidence, and leave behind a legacy that lasts long after we leave the school gates.

We are not in the business of just entertaining. We are here to educate, inform, nurture, and give young people a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams.

# **OUR VISION**

To become Kenya’s largest and most impactful youth talent and mentorship tour, reaching every institution in the country and eventually hosting mega concerts, colour festivals, and nationwide events that unite young people across all communities.

# **WHAT HAPPENS AT A UGT EVENT**

## **The Full-Day Experience (5:00 AM – 5:30 PM)**

**Urban Pods (8:10 AM): **Five simultaneous 30-minute mentorship breakouts led by industry professionals: Discover Your Talent, Own Your Money, Go Digital, Lead From Where You Are, and Mind Your Mind

**Keynote Address (9:00 AM): **A main speaker delivers an inspiring, relatable talk in raw Gen Z language, aligned with the day’s theme

**Tree Planting (9:20 AM): **In partnership with the Green Movement (led by Tai), students and guests plant trees with scouts taking long-term care

**Church Opening (10:00 AM): **Destiny Life Church’s Tribez and Lit Gen open the main event with gospel energy and crowd activation

**Talent Competitions: **Choir, Gospel, Poetry, Drama, Rap, Dance, Comedy, Maths & Logic, and more. Each category 20–25 minutes with hype sets in between

**Modelling Showcase: **Four runway categories — Cultural/Traditional, Creative, Cosplay, and Professional Wear

**Two Guest Musicians: **A curtain-raising artist and a headline act, each with their own full set

**Teacher Appreciation: **Coordination teachers recognised and awarded. Fun teacher dance competition voted by students

**TV Programme Production: **8 broadcast segments shot throughout the day for the Urban News episode on PPP TV

**Awards Ceremony: **Winners receive certificates, hampers, crowns, sashes, and trophies. Category winners featured on PPP TV

# **OUR PARTNERS**

## **Strategic Co-Production Partner: Destiny Life Church, Syokimau**

Destiny Life Church is embedded into every school visit and provides stage & sound setup, LED PAR can lighting, setup crew manpower, a main keynote speaker, a fully staffed counselling and guidance tent, their branded Tribez youth teams (one team per school), Lit Gen branding and DJ, transportation, and at times a headline gospel musician. Beyond UGT, we collaborate on missions to different Kenyan schools, mega events, and deeper church-community programmes. UGT leadership are full members of the church.

## **Broadcast Partner: PPP TV Kenya**

PPP TV is UGT’s official national broadcaster, averaging viewership of up to 8 million people. Through Urban News — hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde — UGT reaches a daily variable audience of 400,000 to millions of young Kenyans. PPP TV provides full broadcast coverage, marketing slots, mentions, and advertising.

## **Hype ****&**** Entertainment Partner: The Experience Hub**

Led by Hype Ola with DJ Carian, 5 dancers (including Nyarangi and Jeff), and a gospel hypeman (Gig Real). On event day, the Experience Hub deploys 30+ people for three high-energy hype sets and continuous crowd activation throughout the programme.

## **Environmental Partner: The Green Movement**

Led by Tai, the Green Movement facilitates tree planting sessions at every school visit, providing seedlings and guidance. School scouts or Kenya Girl Guides take long-term stewardship of the planted trees.

## **Events Partner: Faly Mark**

CEO of our events company partner. Provides stage and sound equipment with 50/50 transport and logistics cost-sharing.

# **THE OPPORTUNITY**

Chapati King has built a brand that young Kenyans love. Affordable, filling, and available everywhere. At UGT events, students spend the full day from morning to evening. They need real food. Not just snacks. Chapati with beans, chapati with stew, chapati rolls. The kind of food that fuels a student through a full day of competition and celebration.

# **THE ALIGNMENT**

NACADA's 2026 theme is "Empowered Communities, Brighter Futures." Urban Gang Tour's motto is "From Potential to Purpose." Both organisations exist to reach Kenya's young people before it is too late and give them the tools to make better choices.

NACADA's CEO, Dr. Anthony Omerikwa, said: "When we empower youth with purpose, resilience and decision-making skills, we are not just creating leaders. We are creating a critical deterrent against substance abuse." That sentence describes exactly what Urban Gang Tour does at every school we visit.

This proposal outlines how NACADA and UGT can work together to bring drug and substance abuse prevention directly into high schools across Kenya, at scale, on camera, and on national television.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Daily Reach** | 400,000 to millions via Urban News, hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Confirmed Schools** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals. Long term |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **THE PROPOSAL: A NACADA POD AT EVERY SCHOOL**

Urban Gang Tour runs 5 Urban Pod sessions at every school visit. These are 30-minute mentorship breakouts where professionals sit with students in small groups and have real conversations in Gen Z language. No lectures. No stiffness. Just honest talk that lands.

## **Current 5 Pod Topics**

| **Pod 1: Discover Your Talent** | Performing arts: music, dance, spoken word, comedy, modelling |
| --- | --- |
| **Pod 2: Own Your Money** | Entrepreneurship and financial literacy: pitching ideas, budgeting, side hustle culture |
| **Pod 3: Go Digital** | Digital literacy and creative tech: content creation, coding, AI, digital careers |
| **Pod 4: Lead From Where You Are** | Leadership, public speaking, and career mentorship |
| **Pod 5: Mind Your Mind** | Mental health, self-worth, and purpose. Ties into the counselling tent |

## **Proposed 6th Pod: NACADA Drug Prevention Pod**

We are proposing that NACADA owns a dedicated 6th pod at every school we visit. Here is what that looks like:

| **Duration** | 30 minutes. Same as all other pods. Runs simultaneously at 8:10 AM |
| --- | --- |
| **Format** | Small group conversation. 20 to 40 students per session. Relaxed, honest, peer-to-peer energy |
| **Facilitator** | NACADA provides a trained facilitator or peer educator. Someone who speaks Gen Z |
| **Topics** | Real talk on alcohol, drugs, peer pressure, laced edibles, prescription abuse, making drug-free choices. Aligned with NACADA's school-based prevention curriculum |
| **Materials** | NACADA brochures, posters, branded items distributed to students. Information on the 1192 helpline |
| **Recording** | Select sessions can be recorded as short-form content for social media and PPP TV |
| **Language** | English and Sheng mixed. Must feel like a conversation, not a government programme |

# **BEYOND THE POD: FULL EVENT INTEGRATION**

The pod is the centrepiece. But NACADA's presence at UGT events can extend across the full day:

| **NACADA Information Booth** | A branded stand at the event venue. Display materials, helpline cards, essay competition flyers. Students can walk up and ask questions all day |
| --- | --- |
| **Stage Announcement** | MCs (Eugine and Lucy) announce NACADA as the Official Drug Prevention Partner from the main stage. NACADA message delivered to the full student body |
| **Backdrop Branding** | NACADA logo on the main stage backdrop alongside UGT and partner logos |
| **PPP TV Credits** | NACADA acknowledged in every Urban News episode. Up to 8 million viewers |
| **Social Media** | NACADA tagged in event content. The pod session becomes a reel or TikTok that reaches hundreds of thousands |
| **Essay Competition Promotion** | We announce and promote the 2026 NACADA essay competition at every school. Flyers distributed. MCs encourage students to enter |
| **IDADA Alignment** | Our school dates (30 May, 1 June, 4 July) wrap around the International Day Against Drug Abuse (June 26). NACADA presence at our events in that window is perfectly timed |
| **Counselling Tent Referral** | Our counselling tent (run by Destiny Life Church) can refer students with substance concerns to NACADA resources and the 1192 helpline |

# **WHAT NACADA GETS**

| **Direct School Access** | Hundreds to thousands of students per event. Already assembled, already engaged, already listening. The hardest part of prevention outreach (getting young people in the room) is already done |
| --- | --- |
| **Repeat Nationwide Reach** | Every school we visit is a new activation for NACADA. 3 schools this term, more next term, expanding countrywide. This is sustained prevention access, not a one-off seminar |
| **National TV Visibility** | PPP TV broadcasts to up to 8 million viewers. NACADA's prevention message goes beyond the classroom and into living rooms across Kenya |
| **Social Media Content** | 8 to 12 clips per event. The NACADA pod becomes content that Gen Z actually watches and shares. Your message in the format they consume |
| **Essay Competition Pipeline** | We promote the competition at every school. More entries. More engagement. More young people thinking about drug-free choices |
| **IDADA Activation** | Visible, documented, broadcast prevention work in schools around June 26. Content for NACADA's own IDADA reporting and communications |
| **Alignment with Strategy** | NACADA's school-based prevention programme delivered through a vehicle that young people already trust and are excited about. Not another assembly hall lecture. A real experience |
| **Data and Documentation** | Every school visit is documented. Attendance, participation, content. NACADA can use this for reporting, funding proposals, and impact measurement |

# **WHY THIS WORKS**

**You need schools. We are already in them. **NACADA runs school-based prevention programmes. UGT is the vehicle that gets you into schools with a ready audience, professional production, and national broadcast. We are the delivery mechanism your curriculum needs.

**Prevention works best when it is sustained and repeated. **This is not a single awareness day. UGT runs term after term, school after school, countrywide. NACADA's message compounds with every visit.

**Young people trust peers, not programmes. **The pod format is designed to feel like a conversation between equals. A NACADA facilitator who speaks Gen Z will reach students in ways that a classroom poster never will.

**The timing is perfect. **President Ruto has directed NACADA to strengthen the fight against drug abuse among young people. The 2026 essay competition is live. IDADA is in June. UGT's first school is on 30 May. Everything converges right now.

**National TV amplifies everything. **Whatever NACADA does at a UGT event is seen by millions on PPP TV and across social media. Your work is not just happening. It is being witnessed by the nation.

| **Facilitator** | 1 trained facilitator or peer educator per school event for the 30-minute Drug Prevention Pod |

| **Information Booth** | A pull-up banner or small display for the event venue (NACADA can provide or we can arrange space) |
| **Permission** | To use NACADA's name and logo as Official Drug Prevention Partner on our materials, backdrop, and broadcasts |

# **THE ALIGNMENT**

The U.S. Embassy Nairobi Public Diplomacy Section provides small grants for programmes that strengthen ties between the United States and Kenya. Your priority areas include promoting economic prosperity through innovation and entrepreneurship, and your target audience explicitly includes youth aged 15 to 30 and residents of underserved communities.

Urban Gang Tour runs mentorship pods on entrepreneurship (Own Your Money), digital skills (Go Digital), and leadership (Lead From Where You Are) in high schools across Kenya. We broadcast everything on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers. We are a registered Kenyan organisation (BN-6ASYVPW5) doing exactly the kind of work your small grants programme was designed to fund.

# **THE CHALLENGE EVERY BRAND FACES**

Kenya has 10,000+ secondary schools, hundreds of universities and colleges, and millions of young people making daily decisions about the brands they trust, the products they buy, and the companies they want to work for. Traditional advertising reaches them on screens they scroll past. UGT puts your brand in the room with them — on a stage they’re cheering for, in a tent they’re visiting, on a certificate they’re holding, and in content they’re sharing.

This is not a one-off event. This is a long-term, countrywide movement that extends beyond schools into universities, concerts, festivals, church missions, and community events. When you partner with UGT, your brand grows with us.

# **HOW TO PARTNER WITH US**

We don’t have a rigid one-size-fits-all package. We believe the best partnerships are co-created. Here are the ways your brand can join the movement:

## **1. Segment Sponsorship**

Own a specific segment of every event. Your brand becomes synonymous with that category across every school, every city, every event nationwide. Options include any talent category (Rap, Dance, Poetry, Gospel, Drama, Choir, Comedy, Maths & Logic), the Modelling Showcase, Urban Pods, Teacher Appreciation, Tree Planting, or any custom segment.

**Your Brand Gets: **Stage mentions by hosts, branded category intro, your logo on category certificates and awards, TV and social media credits, logo on backdrop, on-camera brand moment during your segment

## **2. Awards ****&**** Prize Sponsorship**

Supply the gift hampers, certificates, trophies, sashes, crowns, or unique prizes. Your brand is physically in the hands of every winner and visible in every award photo and video broadcast on PPP TV.

**Your Brand Gets: **Logo on certificates, branded gift hampers with your products, on-stage mention, TV and social coverage of winners holding your branded prizes

## **3. Booth ****&**** Activation**

Set up a branded booth or activation zone. Sample products, run contests, offer information, or engage directly with thousands of students face-to-face.

**Your Brand Gets: **Dedicated tent/booth space, branded signage, direct student engagement, on-camera mention, social media feature

## **4. Branding ****&**** Visibility**

Your logo across UGT’s physical and digital assets: stage backdrop, teardrops, banners, printed programmes, certificates, social media posts, TV broadcast credits, and Urban News mentions.

## **5. Vendor Partnership**

Sell your products directly to students and event attendees. Snacks, beverages, stationery, accessories, clothing — UGT events give you a captive audience. Destiny Life Church also brings vendors, creating a vibrant vendor ecosystem.

## **6. PR Package**

We build custom PR campaigns for our partners. TV mentions and adverts on PPP TV, dedicated social media posts, branded content, event reels featuring your brand, and a structured campaign that pushes your visibility far beyond the event.

## **7. Unique Element Creation**

Create something entirely new and own it: a branded award category, a student challenge, a digital literacy station, a career expo booth, a VIP experience, or anything creative that aligns with UGT’s mission. We co-design it with you.

## **8. Musician / Artist Facilitation**

Sponsor one of our two guest musicians. Your brand introduces the headline act and is associated with the biggest musical moment of the day.

## **9. University / College Partnership**

Provide career guidance, scholarship info, campus tours, or student recruitment at UGT events. Direct access to high school students at the point of career decision-making.

# **ALIGNMENT 1: GREEN INITIATIVE x TREE PLANTING**

KCB Foundation's Green Initiative supports community resilience through tree planting, water harvesting, and environmental awareness. You work with schools, government institutions, and youth groups. You planted 2,000 trees at Brookshine Schools with Chandaria Foundation and Global Peace Foundation. You supported 1,000 saplings at Oloosho-Oibor Primary School in Kajiado.

Urban Gang Tour plants trees at every single school we visit. Every event includes a tree planting session at 9:20 AM led by Tai, our Green Movement liaison. Students, scouts, Kenya Girl Guides, and the principal all participate. The session is filmed by three cameras and included in every PPP TV broadcast.

## **What KCB Can Do**

| **Co-Sponsor the Tree Planting** | KCB Foundation co-brands the tree planting session at every UGT school. "KCB x UGT Green Session." Your team can supply seedlings, send a facilitator, or simply provide branding and financial support for seedling procurement |
| --- | --- |
| **KCB Green Facilitator** | A KCB Foundation representative attends the tree planting and gives a 5 to 10 minute talk on environmental conservation and KCB's green commitments. Filmed for PPP TV |
| **KCB Green Branding** | KCB logo on the tree planting area, on tree tags, and in all environmental content produced for TV and social media |
| **Contribution to 15 Billion Trees** | Every tree planted at a UGT event is documented and can be counted toward KCB's contribution to the Presidential Directive. 50 to 100 trees per school, filmed and broadcast |

# **ALIGNMENT 2: 2JIAJIRI x OWN YOUR MONEY POD**

KCB Foundation's 2Jiajiri programme has trained over 35,000 youth and created over 150,000 jobs since 2016. It teaches vocational skills, financial literacy, business development, and entrepreneurship. KCB has set aside USD 500 million for this initiative.

Urban Gang Tour runs an Urban Pod at every school called "Own Your Money." This is a 30-minute mentorship session where a professional talks to students about entrepreneurship, budgeting, pitching ideas, and building a side hustle. It is exactly the same spirit as 2Jiajiri, but delivered to students before they even leave school.

## **What KCB Can Do**

| **Distribute Materials** | KCB brochures, savings guides, financial literacy workbooks, and information on the 2Jiajiri programme. Every student leaves the pod knowing that KCB has a pathway for them after school |
| **Pipeline to 2Jiajiri** | Students inspired by the pod become future 2Jiajiri applicants. You are reaching them years before they qualify, building brand awareness and aspiration for the programme |

# **ALIGNMENT 3: SCHOLARSHIPS x STUDENT CELEBRATION**

KCB Foundation reserves 10% of scholarship slots for persons with disabilities and ensures 50% go to girls. You work with school administrators and local communities to identify scholars. You provide full tuition, books, uniforms, mentorship, and psychosocial support.

Urban Gang Tour visits the schools where your scholars study. We can identify KCB scholars at every school we visit, invite them on stage, celebrate them in front of their peers, and feature them in our PPP TV episode. That is national recognition for a student who might otherwise be invisible.

| **Success Story Content** | KCB scholar stories filmed as mini-documentaries by our camera crew. Free professional content for KCB Foundation's own platforms |

# **WHY THIS PARTNERSHIP MATTERS**

65% of women in Kenya cannot afford safe menstrual products. That is 9.4 million women. One million school-age girls miss an average of four school days every month because of their period. 42% of Kenyan schoolgirls have never had access to a sanitary pad.

Binti Pads exists to change that. Urban Gang Tour exists to change that. We are both fighting the same fight. The only difference is the weapon. Binti uses pads. UGT uses a stage, a microphone, and a camera crew.

This proposal is about putting those weapons together.

# **WHY BINTI PADS**

We did not choose Binti Pads because you are the biggest. We chose you because you are the most real.

**Women-owned, women-led. **Binti was designed by women for women. In an industry dominated by male-owned multinationals, that matters. When Binti shows up at a girls school, it is not a corporation doing charity. It is a Kenyan woman who has been there, showing up for girls who are where she used to be.

**The founder****'****s story IS the motto. **Lorna Joyce grew up in Mukuru kwa Reuben. Lost her father and brother. Did laundry and hair to stay in school. Worked her way into aviation. Left a 13-year career to start making pads. That is From Potential to Purpose in the flesh. We want the girls at our events to hear that story.

**Affordable and accessible. **Binti regular pads at KES 75 and extra-long at KES 95 sit in exactly the price range that students and parents can realistically afford. You are not an out-of-reach international brand. You are the mid-market solution Kenya has been missing.

**Already in our zone. **Binti products are available in Nairobi, Kiambu, Kajiado, Machakos, and Mombasa. Our first three schools are in the Kiambu area. We are already in your distribution territory.

**400,000 women impacted. **Since 2022, Binti has reached 400,000 women. UGT can help you reach the next 600,000 faster than any marketing budget ever could.

**You need this as much as we do. **You rebooted in 2025 with a new local manufacturing model. You need growth channels that cost visibility, not millions in media spend. UGT is that channel: national TV, social media, direct school access, term after term, countrywide.

# **THE PROPOSAL**

Binti Pads becomes the Official Menstrual Health Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is exactly what that means:

## **1. Binti Pads Distributed at Every Girls School**

Binti pads and liners distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can happen through the counselling tent (run by Destiny Life Church counsellors), through a dedicated Binti booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand to winners on stage, on camera, on PPP TV. Every pad you distribute is seen by cameras, broadcast to millions, and posted across social media.

## **2. Binti Branded Booth**

A dedicated Binti Pads tent or booth at every event. Your space to talk to girls about menstrual health, period dignity, pad usage, and breaking stigma. Staff it with your own team or a trained educator. Students walk through, pick up products, ask questions, and learn. This is not a display. This is your existing mission happening at scale.

## **3. Menstrual Health Integration in the Mind Your Mind Pod**

We run a 30-minute Urban Pod on mental health and self-worth called Mind Your Mind. We can integrate a menstrual health education segment into this pod, led by a Binti representative. Alternatively, if the partnership grows, we can create a standalone Binti menstrual health pod. Either way, girls are hearing the Binti message in a small group setting where real conversations happen.

## **4. Binti Products in Every Gift Hamper**

Every talent category winner receives a certificate and a gift hamper on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Binti pads inside every hamper. Your products in the hands of winners during the most photographed, most filmed, most shared moment of the day.

## **5. Lorna Joyce as a Guest Speaker**

If Lorna is available, we would love to invite her to speak at select school events. Not as a product pitch. As a person. Her story of growing up in Mukuru, losing everything, fighting her way back, and building something that helps girls like the girl she used to be. That story will land harder than any TED Talk. And it will be filmed and broadcast to millions.

## **6. Stage Branding and PPP TV Credits**

Binti Pads logo on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops, on printed programmes. Binti acknowledged as Official Menstrual Health Partner in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. Up to 8 million viewers.

## **7. Social Media Coverage**

8 to 12 clips per event. The Binti booth, the pad distribution, the conversation at the counselling tent. Real girls, real schools, real impact. Tagged across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. This is the kind of authentic content that Gen Z shares and trusts, because it is real, not staged.

# **THE GAP**

The National Youth Service was established in 1964 with one mandate: train young citizens to serve the nation. Since then, NYS has trained hundreds of thousands of Kenyan youth in vocational skills ranging from engineering and agriculture to textiles and hospitality. Your 5-Point Vision includes Vocational Training and Social Transformation, Enterprise and Youth Economy, and National Service and Youth Re-socialization.

But here is the gap. NYS recruits youth aged 18 to 24. Most of them have already finished secondary school. By the time they walk through your gates, their dreams have already been shaped by whatever they were exposed to in high school. If nobody told them about vocational pathways, they arrive at NYS by accident or desperation, not by aspiration.

Urban Gang Tour reaches students at 13 to 18. We are inside their schools. We have their attention. We have cameras rolling. We have PPP TV broadcasting to 8 million viewers. And right now, nobody is using that moment to tell these students that NYS exists, that vocational skills are valuable, and that there is a dignified, funded, structured pathway waiting for them after KCSE.

That is the gap. And this proposal closes it.

# **WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING**

## **1. NYS Vocational Skills Pod**

A dedicated 30-minute pod session at every school led by an NYS facilitator. The facilitator talks to 20 to 40 students about what NYS offers: the trades available, what training looks like, what life after NYS can be, how to apply, and why vocational skills are just as valuable as a university degree. This is not a recruitment drive. It is a conversation. The kind that makes a student think "I have a future I did not know about."

| **Time** | 8:10 AM. Runs simultaneously with 4 other Urban Pods (financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, mental health) |
| --- | --- |
| **Duration** | 30 minutes |
| **Format** | Small group. 20 to 40 students. Informal. Gen Z language. Real stories from real NYS graduates |
| **Facilitator** | NYS provides 1 facilitator per event. Ideally a young NYS graduate who can relate to the students |
| **Materials** | NYS brochures, trade information sheets, recruitment dates, application process guides |
| **Recording** | Select sessions filmed as short-form content for PPP TV and social media |

## **2. NYS Information Booth**

A branded NYS booth or information stand open from 9:30 AM through the end of the event. Pull-up banners, brochures, trade samples, and a facilitator answering questions all day. Students and teachers can walk up, learn about NYS, and take home information. Peak foot traffic during tea break (9:35 AM), lunch (1:10 PM), and throughout the afternoon.

## **3. Stage Announcements**

Our MCs (Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde) announce NYS from the main stage at least twice during the event: during the opening ceremony and during the closing remarks. Hundreds to thousands of students hear about NYS in one moment. This is broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers.

## **4. Textile Institute x Modelling Showcase**

NYS operates the Textile and Garment Training Institute in Nairobi. We run a modelling showcase at every school with four runway categories: Cultural/Traditional Wear, Creative Wear, Cosplay, and Professional Wear. NYS can co-brand the modelling segment and showcase what the Textile Institute produces. If NYS can supply sample garments made by recruits, students walk the runway in NYS-made clothing. That is the most powerful recruitment tool you can imagine: a student wearing something made by someone their age at NYS and feeling proud doing it.

## **5. Post-Production NYS Content**

Our production team (3 cameras, PPP TV editor) produces a full Urban News episode from each school event. We can produce a standalone NYS awareness segment (2 to 5 minutes) from the event footage: the pod in action, the booth, students learning about NYS, the textile showcase moment. This is aired on PPP TV and posted across our social media. NYS can also use this content on your own platforms.
