# **WHO WE ARE**

Urban Gang Tour (UGT) is a Kenyan high school talent search, inspiration, mentorship, and awards programme that goes directly into institutions to discover, nurture, and celebrate young talent. Built on the motto “From Potential to Purpose,” UGT combines live performances, competitions, mentorship pods, arts and crafts displays, modelling showcases, and award concerts into a single high-energy experience that leaves a lasting impact on every student it reaches.

We are not in the business of just entertaining students — we are here to educate, inform, nurture, and give young people a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams.

**Registered Name: **Urban Gang Merch

**Registration Number: **BN-6ASYVPW5

**Registration Date: **26th August 2025 (eBusiness Portal, Kenya)

**Headquarters: **Nairobi, Kenya

# **OUR VISION**

To become Kenya’s largest and most impactful youth talent and mentorship tour, reaching every high school, university, college, and tertiary institution in the country — and eventually hosting mega concerts, colour festivals, and nationwide events that unite young people across all communities, including partnerships with churches and faith-based organisations.

# **WHAT WE DO**

## **The Urban Gang Tour Experience**

Each UGT school visit is a full-day immersive programme that includes:

**Urban Pods (Morning Sessions): **Intimate breakout sessions led by industry professionals across five core topics — Discover Your Talent, Own Your Money, Go Digital, Lead From Where You Are, and Mind Your Mind. Each pod runs for 30 minutes and gives students direct access to mentors in a relaxed, Gen Z-friendly environment.

**Keynote Address: **A main speaker delivers an inspiring, relatable talk aligned with the day’s theme, using raw, authentic language that connects with young people.

**Tree Planting: **In partnership with Kenya Wildlife Services (KWS), students and guests plant trees on school grounds, with the school’s scouts or Kenya Girl Guides taking long-term stewardship.

**Talent Competitions: **Categories include Rap, Singing, Dance, Poetry, Drama, Comedy, Choir, Traditional Art, Mathematics & Logic, and more depending on the school.

**Modelling Showcase: **Four runway categories — Cultural/Traditional Wear, Creative Wear, Cosplay, and Professional Wear — coordinated by our modelling partner.

**Hype Sessions: **High-energy crowd activation sets by The Experience Hub featuring DJs, hypemen, and dancers throughout the day.

**Church Session: **Our strategic partner Destiny Life Church provides an early-morning gospel hype session, a main speaker, a counselling and guidance tent, and their youth movement (Tribez) at every school visit.

**Teacher Appreciation: **Coordination teachers are recognised and awarded for their role in making the programme run efficiently, culminating in a fun teacher dance competition.

**Awards Ceremony: **Winners receive certificates and gift hampers. The overall modelling winner is crowned. Category winners earn a feature on Urban News (PPP TV Kenya).

# **OUR TEAM**

Every person on the UGT team has a defined role and is a partner invested in the vision.

# **OUR PARTNERS**

## **Strategic Co-Production Partner: Destiny Life Church, Syokimau**

Destiny Life Church is UGT’s anchor co-production partner. They are embedded into every school visit and provide:

**• Stage ****&**** Sound: **Full stage setup, PA system, and LED PAR can stage lighting

**• Setup Crew: **Manpower to assemble and dismantle stage, sound, and lighting rigs

**• Main Speaker: **A keynote speaker for the main address at every school visit

**• Counselling Tent: **Fully staffed guidance and counselling tent with trained counsellors

**• Tribez Youth Teams: **Their branded youth movement deploys a dedicated team to each school

**• Lit Gen Branding: **Their youth brand (Lit Gen) is visibly branded at every event

**• Transportation: **Transport for Tribez members and equipment

**• Musicians: **At times they facilitate a headline gospel musician as the key highlight

**• Vendors: **The church is encouraged to bring in vendors to each event

Beyond UGT, we are planning missions to different Kenyan schools, mega events, and deeper church-community collaborations together. UGT leadership are full members of Destiny Life Church.

## **Broadcast Partner: PPP TV Kenya**

PPP TV is UGT’s official national broadcaster, averaging viewership of up to 8 million people. Through the flagship show Urban News — hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde — UGT reaches a daily variable audience of 400,000 to millions of young Kenyan viewers. PPP TV provides full broadcast coverage, marketing slots, mentions, and advertising for UGT across its platforms.

## **Hype ****&**** Entertainment Partner: The Experience Hub**

Led by Hype Ola and DJ Carian, The Experience Hub provides three high-energy hype sets across each event day. Their team includes dancers (Nyarangi, Jeff, and others), a gospel hypeman (Gig Real), and additional hype specialists. They are responsible for crowd activation, energy management, and keeping the atmosphere electric throughout every school visit.

## **Environmental Partner: Kenya Wildlife Services (KWS)**

KWS facilitates the tree planting sessions at every school visit, providing seedlings and guidance. Schools’ scouts or Kenya Girl Guides take long-term care of the planted trees, creating a lasting environmental legacy at every institution UGT visits.

**Urban Gang Snacks: **Our own affordable snack shop set up at every event, selling cheap and popular snacks to students.

**Urban Gang Moments: **Our instant-print photobooth where students take photos and receive printed copies at KES 20 per picture.

**Urban Gang Merch: **Our official merchandise line sold at every event.

# **OUR REACH**

UGT leverages three powerful channels to reach young Kenyans:

**1. Urban News (PPP TV): **One of the biggest youth-focused shows in Kenya with daily viewership of 400K to millions

**2. PPP TV National Broadcast: **National broadcaster averaging up to 8 million viewers

# **YOU SELL. THEY BUY. WE ALL WIN.**

Urban Gang Tour goes into high schools across Kenya and spends the full day with students. Talent competitions, mentorship, modelling, hype sessions, and awards. We bring 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crew. And hundreds to thousands of hungry students.

That is where you come in.

We are looking for food vendors, snack sellers, and small business owners who want to set up shop at our events and sell directly to students. You bring your product. We bring you the crowd.

# **HOW IT WORKS**

| **The Event** | A full day programme at a high school. 5 AM to 5:30 PM. Students are on site from 8 AM. There is a tea break at 9:35 AM, lunch break at 1:10 PM, and foot traffic throughout the day |
| --- | --- |
| **Your Space** | We allocate you a vending area near the stage or in the designated vendor zone. You set up your own table, cart, or stall. We provide the space and the traffic. You provide everything else |
| **Your Responsibility** | You bring your own equipment, products, change, and staff. You set up and pack down your own space. You handle all your own sales. You keep all your revenue from sales |
| **Our Students** | Hundreds to thousands of students per event. They have pocket money. They are hungry. They are in a good mood. They want to eat. This is your ideal customer |
| **Timing** | Set up by 8 AM latest. Peak selling times: tea break (9:35 AM), lunch (1:10 PM), and throughout the afternoon. Pack down after 5:30 PM |

# **WHAT YOU CAN SELL**

Any food or snack that students love and that a school administration will allow on their grounds. Examples:

| **Hot Food** | Fries (chips), smokies, sausages, samosas, mandazi, chapati, bhajia, eggs |
| --- | --- |
| **Cold Snacks** | Crisps, biscuits, sweets, popcorn, candy floss, nuts, dried fruit |
| **Drinks** | Juice, water, soda, milkshake, smoothies, yoghurt |
| **Street Food** | Mutura, mahindi (roasted maize), mishkaki, chips mwitu |
| **Baked Goods** | Cakes, doughnuts, muffins, scones, cookies |
| **Other** | Ice cream, cotton candy, fresh fruit, packed lunch boxes |

If you sell it and students buy it, you are welcome. We just ask that it is hygienic, safe, and appropriate for a school setting.

# **HOW YOU SUPPORT THE MOVEMENT**

Vending at UGT events is not just a selling opportunity. It is a partnership. Here is how you can support the movement while growing your own business:

| **Option 1: Vendor Fee** | A small flat vendor fee per event (amount to be agreed). This helps us cover logistics and keeps the vendor ecosystem organised. It is affordable and fixed so you know your costs upfront |
| --- | --- |
| **Option 2: Product Contribution** | Donate a portion of your products to the event. For example: contribute 50 servings of fries to the Urban Gang Snacks shop, or provide snacks for the gift hampers we give to winners on stage |
| **Option 3: Percentage Share** | Agree on a small percentage of your event-day sales (for example 10%) that goes to UGT. You keep the rest. Simple and fair |
| **Option 4: In-Kind Support** | Help with setup, cleanup, or logistics. Lend equipment. Provide tables, chairs, or umbrellas. Every bit of support helps |
| **Option 5: Branding Support** | If you have branded materials (banners, flags, tablecloths), bring them. A well-branded vendor area makes the whole event look better, and that benefits everyone |

We are flexible. If you have another way you want to support the movement, tell us. We will figure it out together. The point is that when you vend at a UGT event, you are part of something bigger than just selling fries. You are part of a programme that is changing the lives of young people across Kenya.

# **WHAT YOU GET**

| **Captive Audience** | Hundreds to thousands of students with money to spend. No competition from the school canteen because we ARE the event |
| --- | --- |
| **Repeat Business** | We run events every term, across the country. Become a regular vendor and you travel with us to every school |
| **Visibility** | Your brand seen by students, teachers, and parents. If your setup looks good, our cameras will capture it. That means social media and potentially PPP TV |
| **Community** | You join a vendor ecosystem alongside other small businesses and our major partners. Networking, connections, and a community of hustlers who get it |
| **Priority for Returning Vendors** | Vendors who show up, sell clean, and support the movement get first priority for the next event. Consistency earns loyalty |

# **VENDOR RULES**

| **Hygiene** | You must maintain clean and hygienic food handling at all times. No exceptions. If a school or health official asks to inspect your setup, you must comply |
| --- | --- |
| **Pricing** | Keep prices student-friendly. These are high schoolers, not corporate events. Fair pricing means more sales and more repeat customers |
| **No Alcohol** | No alcoholic beverages of any kind. This is a school event |
| **No Tobacco** | No cigarettes, shisha, or tobacco products |
| **Waste Management** | You clean up after yourself. Bring your own bin bags. Leave the space cleaner than you found it |
| **Respect the Programme** | Do not set up in front of the stage or block student pathways. Follow the directions of our coordination team (MC Paps and Fred) |
| **Setup and Packdown** | Be set up by 8 AM. Do not start packing until 5:30 PM unless told otherwise by our team |
| **Collaboration** | Be friendly with other vendors. Share the space. Share the customers. We are all in this together |

# **UPCOMING EVENTS**

| **30 May 2026** | Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School |
| --- | --- |
| **1 June 2026** | Loreto Kiambu Girls High School |
| **4 July 2026** | Gathirimu Girls Technical High School |
| **Term 3 and beyond** | More schools every term. Countrywide expansion |

# **HOW TO SIGN UP**

It is simple. Contact us and tell us what you sell, how many events you want to attend, and which support option works for you. We will confirm your spot and send you the details.

**PARTNERSHIP PROPOSAL**

*Where Potential Meets Purpose.*

Prepared for Equity Group Foundation and Equity Bank Marketing

April 2026

# **FOUR ALIGNMENT POINTS. ONE PARTNERSHIP.**

Dr. James Mwangi said it at the 16th Annual Education and Leadership Congress, April 2025: "We are cultivating more than scholars. We are developing a movement of purpose-driven leaders who will redefine Kenya's future. Where potential meets opportunity."

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose. The language is identical. The mission is identical. The young people we are serving are identical.

Equity Group Foundation has raised KES 98 billion cumulatively. 60,009 scholars through Wings to Fly and Elimu. 23,825 through the Equity Leaders Program. 891 scholars at 199 universities across 36 countries. This is the most powerful youth institution in Kenya.

Urban Gang Tour goes into the schools where those scholars study, with 30 professionals, cameras, DJs, mentors, and a national TV broadcast. We are the vehicle that takes financial literacy, scholar recognition, and youth empowerment directly into those schools in a format that Gen Z actually connects with.

This proposal covers four alignment points between Equity Group Foundation and Urban Gang Tour. Each one stands on its own. Together, they make this the most natural partnership on either of our desks.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical. All girls schools |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **ALIGNMENT 1: OWN YOUR MONEY POD**

We run a 30-minute financial literacy pod at every school called "Own Your Money." A professional sits with 20 to 40 students and talks about savings, budgeting, entrepreneurship, and building a financial future. In Gen Z language. No lectures. Just real talk.

We want Equity Group Foundation to own this pod.

| **What Changes** | The pod is renamed to "Equity Financial Futures" or "Equity Own Your Money." Equity branding on the pod room. Equity materials in students' hands |
| --- | --- |
| **Who Leads It** | An Equity facilitator, ELP alumnus, or Wings to Fly graduate. Someone young enough for students to relate to, but accomplished enough to inspire them |
| **What They Teach** | Savings culture, budgeting, entrepreneurship, financial goal-setting, how to open a bank account, why financial literacy matters. Equity's own curriculum or a version of it |
| **Materials Distributed** | Equity branded savings guides, financial literacy workbooks, information on Wings to Fly, ELP, and Equity youth products |
| **Duration** | 30 minutes at 8:10 AM. Runs simultaneously with 4 other Urban Pods |
| **Recording** | Select sessions filmed as short-form content for PPP TV and social media |
| **Why This Matters** | Students learn about banking from Equity before any other bank reaches them. You are planting the seed at 13 to 18 years old. By the time they open their first account, Equity is already the name they trust |

# **ALIGNMENT 2: WINGS TO FLY SCHOLAR SPOTLIGHT**

Wings to Fly has 60,009 scholars across Kenya. Some of them are at the exact schools we visit. We can celebrate them.

| **What Happens** | At every school that has Wings to Fly or Elimu scholars, we invite them on stage during the main event. The MCs introduce them. The school applauds. A photo is taken. The moment is filmed and included in the PPP TV broadcast |
| --- | --- |
| **Why This Matters** | Wings to Fly scholars are often the brightest but quietest students. They do not get public recognition. UGT gives them a moment of celebration in front of their entire school, on camera, broadcast to 8 million viewers. That is life-changing visibility |
| **Scholarship Promotion** | Our MCs promote Wings to Fly and Elimu applications from the stage. Information distributed to students and teachers. QR codes to the application portal displayed on venue signage |
| **Content Produced** | Scholar spotlight moments edited into standalone videos for Equity's own platforms. Free professional content |

# **ALIGNMENT 3: ELP PRE-PIPELINE**

The Equity Leaders Program selects 750 top KCSE performers each year. They go through a 6-month paid internship at Equity branches and then proceed to university. The programme integrates financial literacy, mentorship, and leadership training.

Our Own Your Money pod is the pre-pipeline for ELP. We reach students at 13 to 18, 2 to 5 years before they sit for KCSE. By the time they qualify for ELP, they already know Equity. They already have the savings mindset. They already see Equity as the institution that believed in them first.

**The numbers: **If we visit 10 schools per year and each school has 500 students, that is 5,000 students exposed to Equity's financial literacy curriculum annually. Over 5 years, that is 25,000 students. Some of them will become ELP scholars. All of them will become Equity customers

# **ALIGNMENT 4: CONGRESS AND CONFERENCE CO-HOSTING**

Your 16th Annual Congress brought 3,200 scholars from 7 counties together for mentorship, leadership training, and celebration. We run a similar programme: after touring multiple schools, we host a conference day at Destiny Life Church in Syokimau where winners and top pod participants come together for mentorship, workshops, and gifts.

We could co-host this conference day with Equity Group Foundation. Your scholars meet our winners. Two movements of purpose-driven young people in one room. Financial literacy sessions led by Equity. Talent showcases by UGT winners. All filmed for PPP TV.

# **WHY EQUITY AND UGT FIT**

This kit is built differently from the first one. Here's what changed and why:

The Strategy Document (387 paragraphs) is built on actual research from Coachella 2026, AFROPUNK, Revolve Festival, eMarketer Gen Z data, Attest's 2026 media consumption study, Sprout Social's 2025 Index, and WGSN consumer trends. The core insight driving everything: UGT has two audiences on social media — students who make you viral and sponsors who fund you — and every post must serve both simultaneously. What's inside the document:

6 Content Pillars — The Stage (performance clips), The Journey (student stories), The Vibe (BTS), The Drop (announcements/FOMO), The Sermon (purpose/mental health), The Flex (production/credibility). Each pillar is designed to hook students AND impress sponsors at the same time.

Hashtag Architecture — Not a random list. A layered system modelled on Coachella's approach: master tags (#UrbanGangTour), campaign tags (#NextStopLoading, #UGTLive, #UGTat[School]), pillar tags (#UGTTalent, #UGTBTS), and discovery tags for each platform.

5 Campaign Concepts — #NextStopLoading (7-day countdown before each school, Coachella-style anticipation), #UGTMoments (student UGC tied to the photobooth), #PotentialToPurpose (mini-doc series for sponsor boardrooms), #TeacherOfTheYear (education sector bait), #UGTDanceChallenge (TikTok algorithm hack).

Subject:
A Partnership to Reach Kenya's Youth — Urban Gang Tour × Destiny Life Church
Dear Bishop Phillips Katutu,

Grace and peace to you and the entire Destiny Life Church family.

My name is Eugine Micah. I am the Creative Director and Lead Host of Urban Gang Tour (UGT), and I am also a member of Destiny Life Church. I am writing to you not just as someone presenting an idea, but as someone who is part of this house and who deeply believes that what God is doing through Destiny Life Church and what He is calling us to do through Urban Gang Tour are meant to work together.

I want to share a vision with you — and then ask for your partnership in making it real.

---

WHAT IS URBAN GANG TOUR?

Urban Gang Tour is Kenya's first high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into schools across the country. Our motto is "From Potential to Purpose" — and that is not just a tagline. It is a conviction.

We go into schools with a team of 30+ professionals — hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, mentors, and speakers — and we spend a full day with students. We run mentorship sessions (we call them Urban Pods) on topics like mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, and discovering talent. We run talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, choir, comedy, and modelling. We award the best performers with certificates and gifts. And every event is filmed and broadcast nationally on PPP TV Kenya through our show Urban News, which reaches up to 8 million viewers.

We are registered as Urban Gang Merch (BN-6ASYVPW5) and we have already confirmed three schools for Term 2, 2026:
• 30 May — Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
• 1 June — Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
• 4 July — Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

But Bishop, this is bigger than talent shows. We are building a movement that follows up with every school we visit — we establish Urban Gang Clubs with student leadership, we bring winners to speak on PPP TV, and we are planning a conference day for all winners and top participants from across the toured schools.

That conference day is where Destiny Life Church comes in.

---

WHY DESTINY LIFE CHURCH?

Bishop, I have watched what this church does with young people. The Lit Gen is not just a youth service — it is a movement. Every Sunday at 11:30 AM, I see young people being shaped by the Word, by worship, and by community. The Tribez teams carry an energy and an identity that is powerful. The counselling ministry, the Upendo outreach, the Equip and Transform conferences — all of it tells me that Destiny Life Church does not just talk about impacting the next generation. You are doing it.

Urban Gang Tour needs a spiritual home. We need a partner who understands that reaching young people is not just about entertainment — it is about transformation. And I believe that partner is Destiny Life Church.

Your 2026 theme is "The Year of Greater Works." Bishop, I believe this partnership is one of those greater works.

---

WHAT WE ARE ASKING FOR

We are asking Destiny Life Church to come alongside us as our Strategic Co-Production Partner. This is not a one-time favour. This is a long-term, deep, covenantal partnership. Here is what we would humbly request:

1. STAGE & SOUND SYSTEM — This is our primary need. We need DLC's stage and PA system for our school events. This is the foundation of everything we do.

2. LED PAR CAN STAGE LIGHTING — The PAR can lights that the church uses for services would transform our school events from a talent show into a production.

3. SETUP CREW — The manpower to assemble and dismantle the stage, sound, and lighting at each school venue.

4. A MAIN SPEAKER — We would love for DLC to provide a keynote speaker for each school visit. Someone who can speak to young people in their language — raw, real, and relatable — about purpose, mental health, and faith. This does not have to be a sermon. It can be a conversation. But it must come from the heart of someone who genuinely cares about young people.

5. COUNSELLING & GUIDANCE TENT — A fully staffed tent at every event where students can speak to trained counsellors. Bishop, the youth in Kenya are going through so much. This tent could save lives.

6. THE TRIBEZ — We would love for DLC to deploy a branded Tribez team to each school. Their energy, their identity, their worship — it sets the tone for the entire day. We envision the Tribez opening the main event with a gospel hype session that lights the place on fire.

7. LIT GEN BRANDING & DJ — The Lit Gen brand is powerful among young people. We would love for Lit Gen to have a visible, branded presence at every event — including a Lit Gen DJ for a jam session.

8. TRANSPORTATION — Assistance with transport for the Tribez team members and equipment.

9. MUSICIAN FACILITATION — When possible, we would love for DLC to facilitate a gospel musician as a headline act. This would be the spiritual highlight of the day.

10. VENDORS — We encourage DLC to bring vendors to our events to serve the students and community.

---

WHAT DESTINY LIFE CHURCH GETS

Bishop, this is not a one-way street. Here is what this partnership gives back to the church:

• DIRECT ACCESS TO THOUSANDS OF YOUNG PEOPLE — Every school visit puts DLC in front of hundreds to thousands of students who need exactly what the church offers. The counselling tent alone could be the beginning of a relationship that brings a young person to Christ.

• LIT GEN VISIBILITY — The Lit Gen brand will be seen by every student at every school, broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers, and featured across all our social media platforms. This is youth ministry at scale.

• TRIBEZ DEPLOYMENT — The Tribez get real-world experience in outreach, worship, and leadership. Every school visit is a training ground for the next generation of church leaders.

• NATIONAL TV EXPOSURE — DLC's name, brand, and mission will be featured in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. This is national visibility for the church's youth ministry.

• THE CONFERENCE DAY — We are planning a one-day conference at DLC's grounds in Syokimau where the best students from all toured schools come together for mentorship, talks, workshops, and gifts — all facilitated by the church. This is a direct pipeline of young people walking through DLC's doors.

• JOINT MISSIONS — Beyond the tour, we want to go on missions together to different Kenyan schools, host mega events, and expand the reach of both UGT and DLC's youth ministry.

• A RELATIONSHIP, NOT A TRANSACTION — Bishop, I am a member of this church. I am not approaching DLC as an outsider looking for resources. I am approaching as a son of this house who has been given a vision and wants to run with it alongside his church family.

---

THE BIGGER PICTURE

Bishop, imagine this: two years from now, DLC's Tribez are known in every high school in Kenya. Lit Gen is a brand that young people across the country recognise. Students who were counselled at a UGT event are now attending DLC's Sunday services. Winners from the tour are giving testimonies at the Transform conference. Urban Gang Clubs in schools across the country are connected to DLC's youth network.

This is not a dream. This is a plan. And it starts with the stage and the sound system.

I have attached two documents for your review:
1. DLC × UGT Partnership Proposal — Full details of the partnership, roles, and mutual benefits
2. DLC Event Roles & Programme — Exactly what DLC provides on each event day, with a timeline

Bishop, I would love the opportunity to sit down with you — even for 15 minutes — to walk through this vision face to face. I believe in my heart that this is a God-ordained partnership, and I pray that you will see what I see.

"For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do." — Ephesians 2:10

From Potential to Purpose.

Subject:
Chandaria x Urban Gang Tour: Hygiene Partner for Kenya's Girls Schools
Dear Chandaria Industries Marketing and Design Teams,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah, and alongside my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, which reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because we believe Chandaria Industries and Urban Gang Tour share something that most partnerships do not have from the start: the same mission for the same people.

Let me explain.

We have three schools confirmed for Term 2, 2026:

30th May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1st June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4th July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

All three are girls schools.

Chandaria Industries has been donating Rosy Girl sanitary pads to girls schools for years. You partnered with She Needs a Chance to host girls from Mukuru for menstrual hygiene education. You are a listed partner of the Menstrual Hygiene Day Organization. You donated 8,000 packs of Velvex sanitary pads to the Kisumu Youth Football Association. You have been doing this work quietly and consistently because you understand that a girl who misses school because she cannot afford a pad is a girl whose potential is being wasted.

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose.

We go into these girls schools with 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run the full day with them. Talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. Mentorship sessions on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. A counselling tent staffed by trained counsellors from our church partner, Destiny Life Church. And we film the whole thing and broadcast it on PPP TV to millions of Kenyans.

But here is what we do not have yet: a hygiene partner.

We are walking into girls schools where hundreds of students need exactly what Chandaria manufactures. Sanitary pads. Hand sanitizer. Wet wipes. Tissue. These are not luxuries. They are essentials. And right now, we have no partner providing them.

Here is what we are proposing.

We want Chandaria Industries to be the Official Hygiene Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is what that looks like in practice:

1. Velvex and Rosy Girl sanitary pads distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can be done through the counselling tent, through a dedicated Chandaria hygiene booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand out to winners on stage.

2. A Chandaria branded hygiene booth at every event. This is your space to educate students on menstrual hygiene, hand hygiene, and personal care. You can staff it with your own team or we can manage it on your behalf. This is your existing CSR programme happening at scale, on camera, on national TV.

3. Velvex hand sanitizer and wet wipes at the counselling tent and backstage areas. Branded and visible. Students and staff using your products throughout the day.

4. Chandaria products inside every gift hamper. Winners receive certificates and gift hampers on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Your products are physically in their hands during the biggest moment of the day.

5. Stage backdrop and teardrop branding. Chandaria's logo alongside UGT on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops around the venue, and on printed programmes given to every student.

6. PPP TV broadcast credits. Chandaria Industries acknowledged as Official Hygiene Partner in every Urban News episode. That is up to 8 million viewers seeing your CSR in action.

7. Social media coverage. Every event produces 8 to 12 short form clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The hygiene booth, the pad distribution, the counselling tent. All of it becomes content that shows Chandaria doing real work for real girls in real schools.

Why this matters for Chandaria.

You have been doing this CSR work for years. But most of it happens quietly. A donation here, a school visit there. What we are offering is a national platform to amplify what you are already doing. When Velvex pads are handed to a girl at a UGT event, that moment is captured on three cameras, edited into a PPP TV episode, posted across social media, and broadcast to millions. That is not just CSR. That is brand building at the highest level.

And here is the part that no other partnership can offer you: every single school on our current calendar is a girls school. You are not sponsoring a generic event and hoping your products are relevant. You are walking directly into the rooms where your products matter most.

Chandaria has been the largest tissue and hygiene manufacturer in East and Central Africa for over 55 years. You are ISO certified. You have Superbrands status. You won the FMCG Tissue Brand of the Year in 2024 and 2025. You are partnered with WWF Kenya. You manufacture 100% in Kenya. Your Buy Kenya Build Kenya commitment is real.

Urban Gang Tour is the vehicle that takes all of that and puts it in front of the next generation of Kenyan consumers and leaders. The girls we reach today are the women who will be buying your products for the next 50 years.

We have attached:

1. The UGT Partner Catalogue, which gives a full overview of the programme, our team, our reach, and all partnership opportunities.

Lucy and I would love to visit your offices and walk through this with your marketing and design teams in person. 20 to 30 minutes is all we need. We are happy to work around your schedule.

Please let us know a date and time that works, and we will be there.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah & Lucy Ogunde

We spend the full day with students running talent competitions, mentorship sessions we call Urban Pods, a counselling tent, and an awards ceremony. We run 5 Urban Pod sessions at every school. The current 5 pod topics are: Discover Your Talent, Own Your Money, Go Digital, Lead From Where You Are, and Mind Your Mind.

We want to add a 6th pod. And we want NACADA to own it.

We are proposing that NACADA partners with Urban Gang Tour to create a dedicated Drug and Substance Abuse Prevention Pod at every school we visit. A 30-minute session led by a NACADA facilitator or trained peer educator, talking to students in Gen Z language about the real dangers of alcohol and drug abuse. Not a classroom lecture. A conversation. The kind that actually lands with young people.

Your own CEO, Dr. Anthony Omerikwa, said it perfectly: "When we empower youth with purpose, resilience and decision-making skills, we are not just creating leaders. We develop decision-making skills through competition and leadership exposure. NACADA's prevention message fits inside our programme like it was designed for it.

Here is why this works for NACADA:

You already run school-based prevention programmes. UGT gives you a vehicle to reach schools at scale, with a built-in audience of hundreds to thousands of students who are already engaged, already listening, and already in a space where they trust the people talking to them.

The International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking is June 26, 2026. Our school calendar for this term includes visits on 30th May, 1st June, and 4th July. Every event is broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers. NACADA's prevention message does not just reach the students in the room. It reaches the nation.

NACADA is acknowledged on stage, on the backdrop, and in the PPP TV broadcast as the Official Drug Prevention Partner. NACADA can set up a branded booth or information stand at every event. We promote the 2026 essay competition and any other NACADA initiatives at every school.

Dear Kenya Wildlife Service Team,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. Together with my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, reaching up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because at every school we visit, we plant trees. And we want to do it properly, with KWS.

Here is the situation. Urban Gang Tour runs full day events in high schools across Kenya. We bring 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run talent competitions, mentorship sessions, modelling showcases, and awards ceremonies. Every event is filmed and broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers.

As part of every school visit, we run a tree planting session. Our current liaison is a team member called Tai, who leads what we call the Green Movement within our programme. He coordinates with students, scouts, Kenya Girl Guides, and the school principal to plant trees on school grounds. The scouts or Girl Guides then take long-term stewardship of the trees.

But right now we are doing this without an official partner who can supply the right seedlings, provide planting guidance, and ensure the trees actually survive and grow. That is where KWS comes in.

The Presidential Directive is targeting the planting of over 1.5 billion trees annually for 10 years. KWS has been at the forefront of this, hosting tree planting exercises at Nairobi National Park and across the country. Your mandate explicitly includes engaging the public, youth, and communities through education and outreach. Your partners include schools and institutions of learning.

Urban Gang Tour is a direct pipeline into those schools. We are already there. We already have the students assembled. We already have the scouts and Girl Guides mobilized. We already have the principal participating. We already film everything for national television. What we need is KWS to supply the seedlings, send a facilitator for the planting session, and bring the credibility and expertise of Kenya's leading conservation authority to what we are doing.

Here is what we are proposing:

KWS supplies indigenous tree seedlings for every school we visit. We are talking 50 to 100 seedlings per school, planted by students, guests, the principal, and scouts during our tree planting session at 9:20 AM each event day.

KWS sends a facilitator or ranger to lead the planting session and give a short talk on conservation, climate, and why trees matter. This does not have to be long. 10 to 15 minutes. But it has to come from KWS.

KWS is acknowledged as the Official Conservation Partner on stage, on the backdrop, in the printed programme, and in the PPP TV broadcast. Up to 8 million viewers seeing KWS doing exactly what the Presidential Directive calls for, in schools, with young people.

KWS can set up an information stand or display at the event to promote wildlife conservation, the National Parks, and the tree planting initiative.

We can co-brand the tree planting session as a KWS x UGT initiative. Every tree planted gets tagged. Every session gets filmed. Every school becomes a documented contribution to the 1.5 billion tree target.

This is not a one-off. We have three schools confirmed this term (30 May, 1 June, and 4 July) and we are booking more every week. This programme runs term after term, expanding countrywide. Every school we visit is another tree planting session. Another 50 to 100 trees. Another group of students who learn about conservation from KWS, not from a textbook.

We have attached the UGT Partner Catalogue for your review.

We would love to visit your offices and walk through this in person. Even 15 minutes is enough.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde

Strategized outreach targets aligned with UGT's needs
Strategized outreach targets aligned with UGT's needs
Based on everything I know about UGT, here is who you should be going after and why. I have grouped them by what they actually do for you.

Government Bodies That Fund Youth Programmes

The National Youth Service (NYS) runs skills training in schools. They can send facilitators to your Go Digital or Lead From Where You Are pods and they have budget for youth activations. The Ministry of Youth Affairs, Creative Economy, and Sports has grant funding for youth talent programmes. UGT is literally a youth talent programme. The Kenya Film Commission supports Kenyan content production and broadcast. You are producing content for PPP TV. They can fund your production costs. The Kenya Cultural Centre and Kenya National Theatre could co-brand the talent and drama categories.

Health and Wellbeing Partners

Beyond NACADA, reach out to Amref Health Africa. They run massive school health programmes across Kenya and have deep pockets for youth health education. They can own or co-own the Mind Your Mind pod. The Kenya Red Cross is an alternative to St John Ambulance for medical cover, and they already do school-based first aid training. Childline Kenya (116 helpline) works on child protection and mental health. They can co-brand the counselling tent alongside Destiny Life Church. The Movember Foundation funds mental health programmes for young men. When you eventually go to boys schools, that is a direct fit.

Telcos and Tech

Safaricom Foundation is the biggest CSR spender in Kenya. Their Blaze programme targets youth aged 18 to 26 with innovation and entrepreneurship training. Your Own Your Money and Go Digital pods are exactly Blaze's lane. They have spent up to KES 3 million on comparable youth activations. Airtel Kenya runs Airtel Rising Stars for youth talent. Your talent competition is their programme in a different format. Google Kenya and Microsoft Kenya both run digital literacy programmes for African youth. Your Go Digital pod is a ready vehicle for their content.

Media Partners Beyond PPP TV

Radio stations can amplify your reach. Radio Maisha, Kiss FM, NRG Radio, Homeboyz Radio. NRG and Homeboyz specifically target Gen Z. They can do live broadcasts from your events, run countdowns before school visits, and interview winners. Standard Group, Nation Media Group, and Royal Media Services can cover your events as news and entertainment content.

Education and Publishing

Longhorn Publishers, Kenya Literature Bureau, East African Educational Publishers, Oxford University Press East Africa. They all sell textbooks to the exact schools you are visiting. They can supply books for gift hampers, sponsor the Maths and Logic competition, and set up career booths. KNEC (Kenya National Examinations Council) is a stretch but they could endorse the academic elements of your programme.

Universities and Colleges

Kenyatta University, University of Nairobi, Strathmore University, USIU-Africa, Daystar University, Mount Kenya University, Zetech University, KCA University. All of them recruit from the high schools you visit. They can set up career booths, offer scholarships to winners, sponsor pods, and get direct access to students at the point of career decision-making. Private colleges like Moringa School, ALX Africa, and Multimedia University are even hungrier for students and will move faster.

Banks You Have Not Hit Yet

Equity Bank Foundation is the biggest one you are missing. Their Wings to Fly scholarship programme sponsors 26,000+ students. They run financial literacy programmes in schools. This kit is built differently from the first one. Here's what changed and why:

The Strategy Document (387 paragraphs) is built on actual research from Coachella 2026, AFROPUNK, Revolve Festival, eMarketer Gen Z data, Attest's 2026 media consumption study, Sprout Social's 2025 Index, and WGSN consumer trends. The core insight driving everything: UGT has two audiences on social media — students who make you viral and sponsors who fund you — and every post must serve both simultaneously. This is exactly what Coachella does.

What's inside the document:

6 Content Pillars — The Stage (performance clips), The Journey (student stories), The Vibe (BTS), The Drop (announcements/FOMO), The Sermon (purpose/mental health), The Flex (production/credibility). Each pillar is designed to hook students AND impress sponsors at the same time.

Hashtag Architecture — Not a random list. A layered system modelled on Coachella's approach: master tags (#UrbanGangTour), campaign tags (#NextStopLoading, #UGTLive, #UGTat[School]), pillar tags (#UGTTalent, #UGTBTS), and discovery tags for each platform.

5 Campaign Concepts — #NextStopLoading (7-day countdown before each school, Coachella-style anticipation), #UGTMoments (student UGC tied to the photobooth), #PotentialToPurpose (mini-doc series for sponsor boardrooms), #TeacherOfTheYear (education sector bait), #UGTDanceChallenge (TikTok algorithm hack).

Platform strategies written specifically for how each platform works: TikTok for virality (crowd reactions > stage shots, 8 content types ranked by priority), Instagram for brand building (weekly calendar, highlight structure, carousel style), Facebook as the credibility press room, WhatsApp as the direct relationship tool.

Event-Day Social Playbook — Minute-by-minute deployment from 5 AM to evening, telling Rania and Nyarangi exactly what to post and when.

6 Visual Templates — Event announcement post, #NextStopLoading teaser story, school reveal story, TikTok video thumbnail, carousel slide, and quote/motto graphic. All in UGT's dark-first magenta/orange visual system.

Subject:
A Partnership to Reach Kenya's Youth — Urban Gang Tour × Destiny Life Church
Dear Bishop Phillips Katutu,

Grace and peace to you and the entire Destiny Life Church family.

My name is Eugine Micah. I am the Creative Director and Lead Host of Urban Gang Tour (UGT), and I am also a member of Destiny Life Church. I am writing to you not just as someone presenting an idea, but as someone who is part of this house and who deeply believes that what God is doing through Destiny Life Church and what He is calling us to do through Urban Gang Tour are meant to work together.

I want to share a vision with you — and then ask for your partnership in making it real.

---

WHAT IS URBAN GANG TOUR?

Urban Gang Tour is Kenya's first high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into schools across the country. Our motto is "From Potential to Purpose" — and that is not just a tagline. It is a conviction.

We go into schools with a team of 30+ professionals — hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, mentors, and speakers — and we spend a full day with students. We run mentorship sessions (we call them Urban Pods) on topics like mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, and discovering talent. We run talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, choir, comedy, and modelling. We award the best performers with certificates and gifts. And every event is filmed and broadcast nationally on PPP TV Kenya through our show Urban News, which reaches up to 8 million viewers.

We are registered as Urban Gang Merch (BN-6ASYVPW5) and we have already confirmed three schools for Term 2, 2026:
• 30 May — Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
• 1 June — Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
• 4 July — Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

But Bishop, this is bigger than talent shows. We are building a movement that follows up with every school we visit — we establish Urban Gang Clubs with student leadership, we bring winners to speak on PPP TV, and we are planning a conference day for all winners and top participants from across the toured schools.

That conference day is where Destiny Life Church comes in.

---

WHY DESTINY LIFE CHURCH?

Bishop, I have watched what this church does with young people. The Lit Gen is not just a youth service — it is a movement. Every Sunday at 11:30 AM, I see young people being shaped by the Word, by worship, and by community. The Tribez teams carry an energy and an identity that is powerful. The counselling ministry, the Upendo outreach, the Equip and Transform conferences — all of it tells me that Destiny Life Church does not just talk about impacting the next generation. You are doing it.

Urban Gang Tour needs a spiritual home. We need a partner who understands that reaching young people is not just about entertainment — it is about transformation. And I believe that partner is Destiny Life Church.

Your 2026 theme is "The Year of Greater Works." Bishop, I believe this partnership is one of those greater works.

---

WHAT WE ARE ASKING FOR

We are asking Destiny Life Church to come alongside us as our Strategic Co-Production Partner. This is not a one-time favour. This is a long-term, deep, covenantal partnership. Here is what we would humbly request:

1. STAGE & SOUND SYSTEM — This is our primary need. We need DLC's stage and PA system for our school events. This is the foundation of everything we do.

2. LED PAR CAN STAGE LIGHTING — The PAR can lights that the church uses for services would transform our school events from a talent show into a production.

3. SETUP CREW — The manpower to assemble and dismantle the stage, sound, and lighting at each school venue.

4. A MAIN SPEAKER — We would love for DLC to provide a keynote speaker for each school visit. Someone who can speak to young people in their language — raw, real, and relatable — about purpose, mental health, and faith. This does not have to be a sermon. It can be a conversation. But it must come from the heart of someone who genuinely cares about young people.

5. COUNSELLING & GUIDANCE TENT — A fully staffed tent at every event where students can speak to trained counsellors. Bishop, the youth in Kenya are going through so much. This tent could save lives.

6. THE TRIBEZ — We would love for DLC to deploy a branded Tribez team to each school. Their energy, their identity, their worship — it sets the tone for the entire day. We envision the Tribez opening the main event with a gospel hype session that lights the place on fire.

7. LIT GEN BRANDING & DJ — The Lit Gen brand is powerful among young people. We would love for Lit Gen to have a visible, branded presence at every event — including a Lit Gen DJ for a jam session.

8. TRANSPORTATION — Assistance with transport for the Tribez team members and equipment.

9. MUSICIAN FACILITATION — When possible, we would love for DLC to facilitate a gospel musician as a headline act. This would be the spiritual highlight of the day.

10. VENDORS — We encourage DLC to bring vendors to our events to serve the students and community.

---

WHAT DESTINY LIFE CHURCH GETS

Bishop, this is not a one-way street. Here is what this partnership gives back to the church:

• DIRECT ACCESS TO THOUSANDS OF YOUNG PEOPLE — Every school visit puts DLC in front of hundreds to thousands of students who need exactly what the church offers. The counselling tent alone could be the beginning of a relationship that brings a young person to Christ.

• LIT GEN VISIBILITY — The Lit Gen brand will be seen by every student at every school, broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers, and featured across all our social media platforms. This is youth ministry at scale.

• TRIBEZ DEPLOYMENT — The Tribez get real-world experience in outreach, worship, and leadership. Every school visit is a training ground for the next generation of church leaders.

• NATIONAL TV EXPOSURE — DLC's name, brand, and mission will be featured in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. This is national visibility for the church's youth ministry.

Subject:
Chandaria x Urban Gang Tour: Hygiene Partner for Kenya's Girls Schools
Dear Chandaria Industries Marketing and Design Teams,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah, and alongside my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, which reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because we believe Chandaria Industries and Urban Gang Tour share something that most partnerships do not have from the start: the same mission for the same people.

Let me explain.

We have three schools confirmed for Term 2, 2026:

30th May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1st June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4th July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

All three are girls schools.

Chandaria Industries has been donating Rosy Girl sanitary pads to girls schools for years. You partnered with She Needs a Chance to host girls from Mukuru for menstrual hygiene education. You are a listed partner of the Menstrual Hygiene Day Organization. You donated 8,000 packs of Velvex sanitary pads to the Kisumu Youth Football Association. You have been doing this work quietly and consistently because you understand that a girl who misses school because she cannot afford a pad is a girl whose potential is being wasted.

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose.

We go into these girls schools with 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run the full day with them. Talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. Mentorship sessions on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. A counselling tent staffed by trained counsellors from our church partner, Destiny Life Church. And we film the whole thing and broadcast it on PPP TV to millions of Kenyans.

But here is what we do not have yet: a hygiene partner.

We are walking into girls schools where hundreds of students need exactly what Chandaria manufactures. Sanitary pads. Hand sanitizer. Wet wipes. Tissue. These are not luxuries. They are essentials. And right now, we have no partner providing them.

Here is what we are proposing.

We want Chandaria Industries to be the Official Hygiene Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is what that looks like in practice:

1. Velvex and Rosy Girl sanitary pads distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can be done through the counselling tent, through a dedicated Chandaria hygiene booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand out to winners on stage.

2. A Chandaria branded hygiene booth at every event. This is your space to educate students on menstrual hygiene, hand hygiene, and personal care. You can staff it with your own team or we can manage it on your behalf. This is your existing CSR programme happening at scale, on camera, on national TV.

3. Velvex hand sanitizer and wet wipes at the counselling tent and backstage areas. Branded and visible. Students and staff using your products throughout the day.

4. Chandaria products inside every gift hamper. Winners receive certificates and gift hampers on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Your products are physically in their hands during the biggest moment of the day.

5. Stage backdrop and teardrop branding. Chandaria's logo alongside UGT on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops around the venue, and on printed programmes given to every student.

6. PPP TV broadcast credits. Chandaria Industries acknowledged as Official Hygiene Partner in every Urban News episode. That is up to 8 million viewers seeing your CSR in action.

7. Social media coverage. Every event produces 8 to 12 short form clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The hygiene booth, the pad distribution, the counselling tent. All of it becomes content that shows Chandaria doing real work for real girls in real schools.

Why this matters for Chandaria.

You have been doing this CSR work for years. But most of it happens quietly. A donation here, a school visit there. What we are offering is a national platform to amplify what you are already doing. When Velvex pads are handed to a girl at a UGT event, that moment is captured on three cameras, edited into a PPP TV episode, posted across social media, and broadcast to millions. That is not just CSR. That is brand building at the highest level.

And here is the part that no other partnership can offer you: every single school on our current calendar is a girls school. You are not sponsoring a generic event and hoping your products are relevant. You are walking directly into the rooms where your products matter most.

Chandaria has been the largest tissue and hygiene manufacturer in East and Central Africa for over 55 years. You are ISO certified. You have Superbrands status. You won the FMCG Tissue Brand of the Year in 2024 and 2025. You are partnered with WWF Kenya. You manufacture 100% in Kenya. Your Buy Kenya Build Kenya commitment is real.

Urban Gang Tour is the vehicle that takes all of that and puts it in front of the next generation of Kenyan consumers and leaders. The girls we reach today are the women who will be buying your products for the next 50 years.

We have attached:

1. The UGT Partner Catalogue, which gives a full overview of the programme, our team, our reach, and all partnership opportunities.

Lucy and I would love to visit your offices and walk through this with your marketing and design teams in person. 20 to 30 minutes is all we need. We are happy to work around your schedule.

Please let us know a date and time that works, and we will be there.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah & Lucy Ogunde

KCB Foundation x Urban Gang Tour: Three Programmes, One Partnership, Every School in Kenya
Dear KCB Foundation Team,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. Together with my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, reaching up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because KCB Foundation runs three programmes that align perfectly with what we do in schools. Not loosely. Not as a stretch. Perfectly.

Your Green Initiative plants trees in schools. We plant trees at every school we visit.

Your 2Jiajiri programme teaches youth financial literacy and entrepreneurship. We run an Urban Pod at every school called "Own Your Money" that teaches students exactly that.

Your scholarship programme sponsors bright but needy students in secondary schools. We are inside those schools. We can identify your scholars, invite them on stage, celebrate them in front of their peers, and feature them on PPP TV to 8 million viewers.

Three of your programmes. One partnership. One vehicle to deliver all of them.

Your own Teddy Oracha said it at the Brookshine Schools tree planting: "We are not just planting trees. We are planting hope for a sustainable future." Our motto is From Potential to Purpose. We are saying the same thing.

We produce 8 to 12 social media clips per event across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.

30 May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

What we are proposing is that KCB Foundation becomes a strategic partner of Urban Gang Tour across all three programme areas. In practice:

Green Initiative: KCB co-brands the tree planting session at every school. Supplies seedlings or funding for seedling procurement. Sends a facilitator if available. Every tree is documented on camera and broadcast nationally. Visible contribution to the 15 billion tree Presidential Directive.

2Jiajiri: KCB owns the "Own Your Money" Urban Pod. A 2Jiajiri graduate or KCB business development officer leads a 30-minute session on financial literacy and entrepreneurship with students. Scholarships: At every school that has KCB scholars, we spotlight them on stage. Certificate. Applause. PPP TV feature. A KCB booth distributes scholarship information. Our MCs promote the programme to the entire student body.

All three delivered through one partnership. All three filmed and broadcast to 8 million viewers. All three repeated school after school, term after term, across the country.

2Jiajiri alone has trained 35,000 youth and created over 150,000 jobs. KCB has set aside USD 500 million for this initiative. Imagine what happens when those numbers meet national television, social media, and direct access to schools every term.

I have attached the KCB x UGT Partnership Proposal which breaks everything down across all three programme areas.

Subject:
Equity Group Foundation x Urban Gang Tour: Where Potential Meets Purpose
Dear Equity Group Foundation Team,

My name is Eugine Micah. I run Urban Gang Tour with my co-host Lucy Ogunde. We go into Kenyan high schools with 30 professionals and run talent competitions, mentorship sessions, and awards concerts. We broadcast on PPP TV Kenya to up to 8 million viewers.

I am writing because Dr. James Mwangi said something at the 16th Annual Congress two weeks ago that stopped me: "We are cultivating more than scholars. We are developing a movement of purpose-driven leaders who will redefine Kenya's future. Where potential meets opportunity."

Subject:
A Partnership to Reach Kenya's Youth — Urban Gang Tour × Destiny Life Church
Dear Bishop Phillips Katutu,

Grace and peace to you and the entire Destiny Life Church family.

My name is Eugine Micah. I am the Creative Director and Lead Host of Urban Gang Tour (UGT), and I am also a member of Destiny Life Church. I am writing to you not just as someone presenting an idea, but as someone who is part of this house and who deeply believes that what God is doing through Destiny Life Church and what He is calling us to do through Urban Gang Tour are meant to work together.

I want to share a vision with you — and then ask for your partnership in making it real.

---

WHAT IS URBAN GANG TOUR?

Urban Gang Tour is Kenya's first high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into schools across the country. Our motto is "From Potential to Purpose" — and that is not just a tagline. It is a conviction.

We go into schools with a team of 30+ professionals — hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, mentors, and speakers — and we spend a full day with students. We run mentorship sessions (we call them Urban Pods) on topics like mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, and discovering talent. We run talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, choir, comedy, and modelling. We award the best performers with certificates and gifts. And every event is filmed and broadcast nationally on PPP TV Kenya through our show Urban News, which reaches up to 8 million viewers.

We are registered as Urban Gang Merch (BN-6ASYVPW5) and we have already confirmed three schools for Term 2, 2026:
• 30 May — Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
• 1 June — Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
• 4 July — Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

But Bishop, this is bigger than talent shows. We are building a movement that follows up with every school we visit — we establish Urban Gang Clubs with student leadership, we bring winners to speak on PPP TV, and we are planning a conference day for all winners and top participants from across the toured schools.

That conference day is where Destiny Life Church comes in.

---

WHY DESTINY LIFE CHURCH?

Bishop, I have watched what this church does with young people. The Lit Gen is not just a youth service — it is a movement. Every Sunday at 11:30 AM, I see young people being shaped by the Word, by worship, and by community. The Tribez teams carry an energy and an identity that is powerful. The counselling ministry, the Upendo outreach, the Equip and Transform conferences — all of it tells me that Destiny Life Church does not just talk about impacting the next generation. You are doing it.

Urban Gang Tour needs a spiritual home. We need a partner who understands that reaching young people is not just about entertainment — it is about transformation. And I believe that partner is Destiny Life Church.

Your 2026 theme is "The Year of Greater Works." Bishop, I believe this partnership is one of those greater works.

---

WHAT WE ARE ASKING FOR

We are asking Destiny Life Church to come alongside us as our Strategic Co-Production Partner. This is not a one-time favour. This is a long-term, deep, covenantal partnership. Here is what we would humbly request:

1. STAGE & SOUND SYSTEM — This is our primary need. We need DLC's stage and PA system for our school events. This is the foundation of everything we do.

2. LED PAR CAN STAGE LIGHTING — The PAR can lights that the church uses for services would transform our school events from a talent show into a production.

3. SETUP CREW — The manpower to assemble and dismantle the stage, sound, and lighting at each school venue.

4. A MAIN SPEAKER — We would love for DLC to provide a keynote speaker for each school visit. Someone who can speak to young people in their language — raw, real, and relatable — about purpose, mental health, and faith. This does not have to be a sermon. It can be a conversation. But it must come from the heart of someone who genuinely cares about young people.

5. COUNSELLING & GUIDANCE TENT — A fully staffed tent at every event where students can speak to trained counsellors. Bishop, the youth in Kenya are going through so much. This tent could save lives.

6. THE TRIBEZ — We would love for DLC to deploy a branded Tribez team to each school. Their energy, their identity, their worship — it sets the tone for the entire day. We envision the Tribez opening the main event with a gospel hype session that lights the place on fire.

7. LIT GEN BRANDING & DJ — The Lit Gen brand is powerful among young people. We would love for Lit Gen to have a visible, branded presence at every event — including a Lit Gen DJ for a jam session.

8. TRANSPORTATION — Assistance with transport for the Tribez team members and equipment.

9. MUSICIAN FACILITATION — When possible, we would love for DLC to facilitate a gospel musician as a headline act. This would be the spiritual highlight of the day.

10. VENDORS — We encourage DLC to bring vendors to our events to serve the students and community.

---

WHAT DESTINY LIFE CHURCH GETS

Bishop, this is not a one-way street. Here is what this partnership gives back to the church:

• DIRECT ACCESS TO THOUSANDS OF YOUNG PEOPLE — Every school visit puts DLC in front of hundreds to thousands of students who need exactly what the church offers. The counselling tent alone could be the beginning of a relationship that brings a young person to Christ.

• LIT GEN VISIBILITY — The Lit Gen brand will be seen by every student at every school, broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers, and featured across all our social media platforms. This is youth ministry at scale.

• TRIBEZ DEPLOYMENT — The Tribez get real-world experience in outreach, worship, and leadership. Every school visit is a training ground for the next generation of church leaders.

• NATIONAL TV EXPOSURE — DLC's name, brand, and mission will be featured in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. This is national visibility for the church's youth ministry.

• THE CONFERENCE DAY — We are planning a one-day conference at DLC's grounds in Syokimau where the best students from all toured schools come together for mentorship, talks, workshops, and gifts — all facilitated by the church. This is a direct pipeline of young people walking through DLC's doors.

• JOINT MISSIONS — Beyond the tour, we want to go on missions together to different Kenyan schools, host mega events, and expand the reach of both UGT and DLC's youth ministry.

• A RELATIONSHIP, NOT A TRANSACTION — Bishop, I am a member of this church. I am not approaching DLC as an outsider looking for resources. I am approaching as a son of this house who has been given a vision and wants to run with it alongside his church family.

---

THE BIGGER PICTURE

Bishop, imagine this: two years from now, DLC's Tribez are known in every high school in Kenya. Lit Gen is a brand that young people across the country recognise. Students who were counselled at a UGT event are now attending DLC's Sunday services. Winners from the tour are giving testimonies at the Transform conference. Urban Gang Clubs in schools across the country are connected to DLC's youth network.

This is not a dream. This is a plan. And it starts with the stage and the sound system.

I have attached two documents for your review:
1. DLC × UGT Partnership Proposal — Full details of the partnership, roles, and mutual benefits
2. DLC Event Roles & Programme — Exactly what DLC provides on each event day, with a timeline

Bishop, I would love the opportunity to sit down with you — even for 15 minutes — to walk through this vision face to face. I believe in my heart that this is a God-ordained partnership, and I pray that you will see what I see.

"For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do." — Ephesians 2:10

From Potential to Purpose.

With deep respect and love for this house,

Eugine Micah
Creative Director & Lead Host
Urban Gang Tour
euginemicah@gmail.com
Urban News on PPP TV Kenya

Norda x Urban Gang Tour: Your Snacks in Every School in Kenya
Dear Evans,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. I host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, and I run a programme called Urban Gang Tour that goes into high schools across Kenya for full-day talent competitions, mentorship sessions, and awards concerts. Our show reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally, and our social media content hits 400,000 to millions daily.

I am writing to you because I believe Norda Industries and Urban Gang Tour are a perfect match, and I would love 20 minutes of your time to show you why.

Here is the short version.

You already know what it looks like when a snack brand meets a live youth audience. You did it with Chacho Ringoz and Churchill at the Greenspan Mall Kids Festival. That activation proved the model. What I am proposing is that same energy, but on a national scale, inside the schools where your customers actually are, broadcast on television to millions, and repeated school after school, term after term, across the entire country.

We visit schools with 30 people. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors. The students compete in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. We film everything and air it on PPP TV. We run mentorship pods on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. We award winners with certificates and gifts. And the whole thing runs from morning to evening.

Here is where Norda fits in.

We are setting up our own snack shop at every event called Urban Gang Snacks. We want Norda products to be the backbone of that shop. Ringoz, Bitez, Tamu Tamu, Cribaz, Urban Bites, Urban Pops, all of it. But beyond just stocking the shop, we want to build something bigger together.

I am talking about Norda having a branded booth at every school we visit. I am talking about Ringoz and Urban Bites being the official snack of the Urban Gang Tour, with your branding on our stage backdrop, on our certificates, on our teardrops, and in every PPP TV episode we produce. I am talking about your products being handed out as part of the gift hampers we give to every winner. I am talking about Norda being seen by thousands of students per event, on camera, on national TV, on Instagram, on TikTok, and in the hands of every young person who walks through our venue.

Your brand Urban Bites was built to be "the pulse of the urban youth lifestyle." Urban Gang Tour is literally that lifestyle in action.

What makes this different from a one-off activation:

This is not a single event. We have three schools confirmed for this term alone and we are booking more every week. This is a countrywide programme that runs term after term. Every school we go to is a new activation for Norda, a new audience, a new set of content, and a new wave of visibility. Your products will not just be seen once and forgotten. They will become a part of the culture of these events. Students will associate Ringoz and Urban Bites with the best day of their school year.

And here is the thing. We are not asking Norda to write us a cheque. We are asking you to come alongside us as a snacking partner. That can look like product supply for our snack shop, branded merch for our events, a Norda booth at our venues, or facilitation of specific elements like gift hampers or student giveaways. We sit down together and figure out what works for both of us.

I have attached two documents:

1. The Norda x UGT Partnership Proposal, which breaks down exactly what we offer, what Norda gets, and how the partnership works at every event.

2. The Norda Activation Sketch, which shows the physical layout of how Norda's booth and branding would look at a typical school event.

I would love to come to your offices on Mombasa Road and walk you through this in person. Even 20 minutes is enough. I know that once you see what we are building, you will want in.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah
Creative Director & Lead Host
Urban Gang Tour

Chandaria x Urban Gang Tour: Hygiene Partner for Kenya's Girls Schools
Dear Chandaria Industries Marketing and Design Teams,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah, and alongside my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, which reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because we believe Chandaria Industries and Urban Gang Tour share something that most partnerships do not have from the start: the same mission for the same people.

Let me explain.

We have three schools confirmed for Term 2, 2026:

30th May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1st June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4th July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

All three are girls schools.

Chandaria Industries has been donating Rosy Girl sanitary pads to girls schools for years. You partnered with She Needs a Chance to host girls from Mukuru for menstrual hygiene education. You are a listed partner of the Menstrual Hygiene Day Organization. You donated 8,000 packs of Velvex sanitary pads to the Kisumu Youth Football Association. You have been doing this work quietly and consistently because you understand that a girl who misses school because she cannot afford a pad is a girl whose potential is being wasted.

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose.

We go into these girls schools with 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run the full day with them. Talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. Mentorship sessions on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. A counselling tent staffed by trained counsellors from our church partner, Destiny Life Church. And we film the whole thing and broadcast it on PPP TV to millions of Kenyans.

But here is what we do not have yet: a hygiene partner.

We are walking into girls schools where hundreds of students need exactly what Chandaria manufactures. Sanitary pads. Hand sanitizer. Wet wipes. Tissue. These are not luxuries. They are essentials. And right now, we have no partner providing them.

Here is what we are proposing.

We want Chandaria Industries to be the Official Hygiene Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is what that looks like in practice:

1. Velvex and Rosy Girl sanitary pads distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can be done through the counselling tent, through a dedicated Chandaria hygiene booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand out to winners on stage.

2. A Chandaria branded hygiene booth at every event. This is your space to educate students on menstrual hygiene, hand hygiene, and personal care. You can staff it with your own team or we can manage it on your behalf. This is your existing CSR programme happening at scale, on camera, on national TV.

3. Velvex hand sanitizer and wet wipes at the counselling tent and backstage areas. Branded and visible. Students and staff using your products throughout the day.

4. Chandaria products inside every gift hamper. Winners receive certificates and gift hampers on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Your products are physically in their hands during the biggest moment of the day.

5. Stage backdrop and teardrop branding. Chandaria's logo alongside UGT on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops around the venue, and on printed programmes given to every student.

6. PPP TV broadcast credits. Chandaria Industries acknowledged as Official Hygiene Partner in every Urban News episode. That is up to 8 million viewers seeing your CSR in action.

7. Social media coverage. Every event produces 8 to 12 short form clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The hygiene booth, the pad distribution, the counselling tent. All of it becomes content that shows Chandaria doing real work for real girls in real schools.

Why this matters for Chandaria.

You have been doing this CSR work for years. But most of it happens quietly. A donation here, a school visit there. What we are offering is a national platform to amplify what you are already doing. When Velvex pads are handed to a girl at a UGT event, that moment is captured on three cameras, edited into a PPP TV episode, posted across social media, and broadcast to millions. That is not just CSR. That is brand building at the highest level.

And here is the part that no other partnership can offer you: every single school on our current calendar is a girls school. You are not sponsoring a generic event and hoping your products are relevant. You are walking directly into the rooms where your products matter most.

Chandaria has been the largest tissue and hygiene manufacturer in East and Central Africa for over 55 years. You are ISO certified. You have Superbrands status. You won the FMCG Tissue Brand of the Year in 2024 and 2025. You are partnered with WWF Kenya. You manufacture 100% in Kenya. Your Buy Kenya Build Kenya commitment is real.

Urban Gang Tour is the vehicle that takes all of that and puts it in front of the next generation of Kenyan consumers and leaders. The girls we reach today are the women who will be buying your products for the next 50 years.

We have attached:

1. The UGT Partner Catalogue, which gives a full overview of the programme, our team, our reach, and all partnership opportunities.

Lucy and I would love to visit your offices and walk through this with your marketing and design teams in person. 20 to 30 minutes is all we need. We are happy to work around your schedule.

Please let us know a date and time that works, and we will be there.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah & Lucy Ogunde
Urban Gang Tour
