NACADA x Urban Gang Tour: Drug Prevention in Every School We Visit
Dear NACADA Team,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. Together with my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, reaching up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because your 2026 theme is "Empowered Communities, Brighter Futures" and our motto is "From Potential to Purpose." That is not a coincidence. That is alignment.

Here is what we do. We go into high schools across Kenya with a team of 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We spend the full day with students running talent competitions, mentorship sessions we call Urban Pods, a counselling tent, and an awards ceremony. Every event is filmed and broadcast on PPP TV to millions.

We run 5 Urban Pod sessions at every school. These are 30-minute mentorship breakouts where professionals sit with students in small groups and talk to them in their own language, no lectures, no stiffness, just real conversations. The current 5 pod topics are: Discover Your Talent, Own Your Money, Go Digital, Lead From Where You Are, and Mind Your Mind.

We want to add a 6th pod. And we want NACADA to own it.

We are proposing that NACADA partners with Urban Gang Tour to create a dedicated Drug and Substance Abuse Prevention Pod at every school we visit. A 30-minute session led by a NACADA facilitator or trained peer educator, talking to students in Gen Z language about the real dangers of alcohol and drug abuse. Not a classroom lecture. A conversation. The kind that actually lands with young people.

Your own CEO, Dr. Anthony Omerikwa, said it perfectly: "When we empower youth with purpose, resilience and decision-making skills, we are not just creating leaders. We are creating a critical deterrent against substance abuse." That is exactly what Urban Gang Tour does. We empower youth with purpose. We build resilience through mentorship. We develop decision-making skills through competition and leadership exposure. NACADA's prevention message fits inside our programme like it was designed for it.

Here is why this works for NACADA:

You already run school-based prevention programmes. UGT gives you a vehicle to reach schools at scale, with a built-in audience of hundreds to thousands of students who are already engaged, already listening, and already in a space where they trust the people talking to them.

Your 2026 essay competition targets senior school students on the theme "Protecting Our Future: Making Drug-Free Choices in School and Beyond." We can promote this competition at every school we visit. Our MCs can announce it from the stage. We can display NACADA materials in the venue. The essay competition and UGT can feed each other.

The International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking is June 26, 2026. Our school calendar for this term includes visits on 30th May, 1st June, and 4th July. We are operating in the exact window around IDADA. A NACADA presence at our events around that date is not just relevant. It is timely.

Every event is broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers. NACADA's prevention message does not just reach the students in the room. It reaches the nation.

What we are asking:

NACADA provides a facilitator or trained peer educator for the Drug and Substance Abuse Prevention Pod at each school we visit. NACADA provides educational materials, brochures, and branded items for distribution. NACADA is acknowledged on stage, on the backdrop, and in the PPP TV broadcast as the Official Drug Prevention Partner. NACADA can set up a branded booth or information stand at every event. We promote the 2026 essay competition and any other NACADA initiatives at every school.

This is not a one-off event. Urban Gang Tour is a long-term, nationwide programme that runs term after term, school after school, across the entire country. We are expanding into universities, colleges, mega concerts, and community events. Wherever we go, NACADA can go with us. This is the kind of sustained, repeated access to young people that prevention science says actually works.

We have attached the UGT Partner Catalogue which gives a full overview of the programme, our team, our reach, our partners, and the opportunity.

We would love to sit down with your team and walk through this in detail. Even 20 minutes is enough. We are confident that once you see what we are building, you will want to be part of it.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde

KWS x Urban Gang Tour: Tree Planting in Every School We Visit
Dear Kenya Wildlife Service Team,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. Together with my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, reaching up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because at every school we visit, we plant trees. And we want to do it properly, with KWS.

Here is the situation. Urban Gang Tour runs full day events in high schools across Kenya. We bring 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run talent competitions, mentorship sessions, modelling showcases, and awards ceremonies. Every event is filmed and broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers.

As part of every school visit, we run a tree planting session. Our current liaison is a team member called Tai, who leads what we call the Green Movement within our programme. He coordinates with students, scouts, Kenya Girl Guides, and the school principal to plant trees on school grounds. The scouts or Girl Guides then take long-term stewardship of the trees.

But right now we are doing this without an official partner who can supply the right seedlings, provide planting guidance, and ensure the trees actually survive and grow. That is where KWS comes in.

The Presidential Directive is targeting the planting of over 1.5 billion trees annually for 10 years. KWS has been at the forefront of this, hosting tree planting exercises at Nairobi National Park and across the country. Your mandate explicitly includes engaging the public, youth, and communities through education and outreach. Your partners include schools and institutions of learning.

Urban Gang Tour is a direct pipeline into those schools. We are already there. We already have the students assembled. We already have the scouts and Girl Guides mobilized. We already have the principal participating. We already film everything for national television. What we need is KWS to supply the seedlings, send a facilitator for the planting session, and bring the credibility and expertise of Kenya's leading conservation authority to what we are doing.

Here is what we are proposing:

KWS supplies indigenous tree seedlings for every school we visit. We are talking 50 to 100 seedlings per school, planted by students, guests, the principal, and scouts during our tree planting session at 9:20 AM each event day.

KWS sends a facilitator or ranger to lead the planting session and give a short talk on conservation, climate, and why trees matter. This does not have to be long. 10 to 15 minutes. But it has to come from KWS.

KWS is acknowledged as the Official Conservation Partner on stage, on the backdrop, in the printed programme, and in the PPP TV broadcast. Up to 8 million viewers seeing KWS doing exactly what the Presidential Directive calls for, in schools, with young people.

KWS can set up an information stand or display at the event to promote wildlife conservation, the National Parks, and the tree planting initiative.

We can co-brand the tree planting session as a KWS x UGT initiative. Every tree planted gets tagged. Every session gets filmed. Every school becomes a documented contribution to the 1.5 billion tree target.

This is not a one-off. We have three schools confirmed this term (30 May, 1 June, and 4 July) and we are booking more every week. This programme runs term after term, expanding countrywide. Every school we visit is another tree planting session. Another 50 to 100 trees. Another group of students who learn about conservation from KWS, not from a textbook.

We have attached the UGT Partner Catalogue for your review.

We would love to visit your offices and walk through this in person. Even 15 minutes is enough.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde
Urban Gang Tour

Dear KCB Foundation Team,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. Together with my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, reaching up to 8 million viewers nationally.

Your Green Initiative plants trees in schools. We plant trees at every school we visit.

Your 2Jiajiri programme teaches youth financial literacy and entrepreneurship. We run an Urban Pod at every school called "Own Your Money" that teaches students exactly that.

Your scholarship programme sponsors bright but needy students in secondary schools. We are inside those schools. We can identify your scholars, invite them on stage, celebrate them in front of their peers, and feature them on PPP TV to 8 million viewers.

Three of your programmes. One partnership. One vehicle to deliver all of them.

Your own Teddy Oracha said it at the Brookshine Schools tree planting: "We are not just planting trees. We are planting hope for a sustainable future." Our motto is From Potential to Purpose. We are saying the same thing.

We run talent competitions, mentorship pods, a modelling showcase, tree planting, and an awards ceremony. Everything is filmed and broadcast on PPP TV. We produce 8 to 12 social media clips per event across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.

30 May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

Green Initiative: KCB co-brands the tree planting session at every school. Supplies seedlings or funding for seedling procurement. Sends a facilitator if available. Every tree is documented on camera and broadcast nationally. Visible contribution to the 15 billion tree Presidential Directive.

2Jiajiri: KCB owns the "Own Your Money" Urban Pod. A 2Jiajiri graduate or KCB business development officer leads a 30-minute session on financial literacy and entrepreneurship with students. Scholarships: At every school that has KCB scholars, we spotlight them on stage. Certificate. Applause. PPP TV feature. A KCB booth distributes scholarship information. Our MCs promote the programme to the entire student body.

All three delivered through one partnership. All three filmed and broadcast to 8 million viewers. All three repeated school after school, term after term, across the country.

2Jiajiri alone has trained 35,000 youth and created over 150,000 jobs. KCB has set aside USD 500 million for this initiative. Imagine what happens when those numbers meet national television, social media, and direct access to schools every term.

Subject:
A Partnership to Reach Kenya's Youth — Urban Gang Tour × Destiny Life Church
Dear Bishop Phillips Katutu,

Grace and peace to you and the entire Destiny Life Church family.

My name is Eugine Micah. I am the Creative Director and Lead Host of Urban Gang Tour (UGT), and I am also a member of Destiny Life Church. I am writing to you not just as someone presenting an idea, but as someone who is part of this house and who deeply believes that what God is doing through Destiny Life Church and what He is calling us to do through Urban Gang Tour are meant to work together.

I want to share a vision with you — and then ask for your partnership in making it real.

---

WHAT IS URBAN GANG TOUR?

Urban Gang Tour is Kenya's first high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into schools across the country. Our motto is "From Potential to Purpose" — and that is not just a tagline. It is a conviction.

We go into schools with a team of 30+ professionals — hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, mentors, and speakers — and we spend a full day with students. We run mentorship sessions (we call them Urban Pods) on topics like mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, and discovering talent. We run talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, choir, comedy, and modelling. We award the best performers with certificates and gifts. And every event is filmed and broadcast nationally on PPP TV Kenya through our show Urban News, which reaches up to 8 million viewers.

We are registered as Urban Gang Merch (BN-6ASYVPW5) and we have already confirmed three schools for Term 2, 2026:
• 30 May — Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
• 1 June — Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
• 4 July — Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

But Bishop, this is bigger than talent shows. We are building a movement that follows up with every school we visit — we establish Urban Gang Clubs with student leadership, we bring winners to speak on PPP TV, and we are planning a conference day for all winners and top participants from across the toured schools.

That conference day is where Destiny Life Church comes in.

---

WHY DESTINY LIFE CHURCH?

Bishop, I have watched what this church does with young people. The Lit Gen is not just a youth service — it is a movement. Every Sunday at 11:30 AM, I see young people being shaped by the Word, by worship, and by community. The Tribez teams carry an energy and an identity that is powerful. The counselling ministry, the Upendo outreach, the Equip and Transform conferences — all of it tells me that Destiny Life Church does not just talk about impacting the next generation. You are doing it.

Urban Gang Tour needs a spiritual home. We need a partner who understands that reaching young people is not just about entertainment — it is about transformation. And I believe that partner is Destiny Life Church.

Your 2026 theme is "The Year of Greater Works." Bishop, I believe this partnership is one of those greater works.

---

WHAT WE ARE ASKING FOR

We are asking Destiny Life Church to come alongside us as our Strategic Co-Production Partner. This is not a one-time favour. This is a long-term, deep, covenantal partnership. Here is what we would humbly request:

1. STAGE & SOUND SYSTEM — This is our primary need. We need DLC's stage and PA system for our school events. This is the foundation of everything we do.

2. LED PAR CAN STAGE LIGHTING — The PAR can lights that the church uses for services would transform our school events from a talent show into a production.

3. SETUP CREW — The manpower to assemble and dismantle the stage, sound, and lighting at each school venue.

4. A MAIN SPEAKER — We would love for DLC to provide a keynote speaker for each school visit. Someone who can speak to young people in their language — raw, real, and relatable — about purpose, mental health, and faith. This does not have to be a sermon. It can be a conversation. But it must come from the heart of someone who genuinely cares about young people.

5. COUNSELLING & GUIDANCE TENT — A fully staffed tent at every event where students can speak to trained counsellors. Bishop, the youth in Kenya are going through so much. This tent could save lives.

6. THE TRIBEZ — We would love for DLC to deploy a branded Tribez team to each school. Their energy, their identity, their worship — it sets the tone for the entire day. We envision the Tribez opening the main event with a gospel hype session that lights the place on fire.

7. LIT GEN BRANDING & DJ — The Lit Gen brand is powerful among young people. We would love for Lit Gen to have a visible, branded presence at every event — including a Lit Gen DJ for a jam session.

8. TRANSPORTATION — Assistance with transport for the Tribez team members and equipment.

9. MUSICIAN FACILITATION — When possible, we would love for DLC to facilitate a gospel musician as a headline act. This would be the spiritual highlight of the day.

10. VENDORS — We encourage DLC to bring vendors to our events to serve the students and community.

---

WHAT DESTINY LIFE CHURCH GETS

Bishop, this is not a one-way street. Here is what this partnership gives back to the church:

• DIRECT ACCESS TO THOUSANDS OF YOUNG PEOPLE — Every school visit puts DLC in front of hundreds to thousands of students who need exactly what the church offers. The counselling tent alone could be the beginning of a relationship that brings a young person to Christ.

• LIT GEN VISIBILITY — The Lit Gen brand will be seen by every student at every school, broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers, and featured across all our social media platforms. This is youth ministry at scale.

• TRIBEZ DEPLOYMENT — The Tribez get real-world experience in outreach, worship, and leadership. Every school visit is a training ground for the next generation of church leaders.

• NATIONAL TV EXPOSURE — DLC's name, brand, and mission will be featured in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. This is national visibility for the church's youth ministry.

• THE CONFERENCE DAY — We are planning a one-day conference at DLC's grounds in Syokimau where the best students from all toured schools come together for mentorship, talks, workshops, and gifts — all facilitated by the church. This is a direct pipeline of young people walking through DLC's doors.

• JOINT MISSIONS — Beyond the tour, we want to go on missions together to different Kenyan schools, host mega events, and expand the reach of both UGT and DLC's youth ministry.

• A RELATIONSHIP, NOT A TRANSACTION — Bishop, I am a member of this church. I am not approaching DLC as an outsider looking for resources. I am approaching as a son of this house who has been given a vision and wants to run with it alongside his church family.

---

THE BIGGER PICTURE

Bishop, imagine this: two years from now, DLC's Tribez are known in every high school in Kenya. Lit Gen is a brand that young people across the country recognise. Students who were counselled at a UGT event are now attending DLC's Sunday services. Winners from the tour are giving testimonies at the Transform conference. Urban Gang Clubs in schools across the country are connected to DLC's youth network.

This is not a dream. This is a plan. And it starts with the stage and the sound system.

I have attached two documents for your review:
1. DLC × UGT Partnership Proposal — Full details of the partnership, roles, and mutual benefits
2. DLC Event Roles & Programme — Exactly what DLC provides on each event day, with a timeline

Bishop, I would love the opportunity to sit down with you — even for 15 minutes — to walk through this vision face to face. I believe in my heart that this is a God-ordained partnership, and I pray that you will see what I see.

"For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do." — Ephesians 2:10

From Potential to Purpose.

With deep respect and love for this house,

Eugine Micah
Creative Director & Lead Host
Urban Gang Tour
euginemicah@gmail.com

Subject:
Norda x Urban Gang Tour: Your Snacks in Every School in Kenya
Dear Evans,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. I host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, and I run a programme called Urban Gang Tour that goes into high schools across Kenya for full-day talent competitions, mentorship sessions, and awards concerts. Our show reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally, and our social media content hits 400,000 to millions daily.

I am writing to you because I believe Norda Industries and Urban Gang Tour are a perfect match, and I would love 20 minutes of your time to show you why.

Here is the short version.

You already know what it looks like when a snack brand meets a live youth audience. You did it with Chacho Ringoz and Churchill at the Greenspan Mall Kids Festival. That activation proved the model. What I am proposing is that same energy, but on a national scale, inside the schools where your customers actually are, broadcast on television to millions, and repeated school after school, term after term, across the entire country.

We visit schools with 30 people. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors. The students compete in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. We film everything and air it on PPP TV. We run mentorship pods on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. We award winners with certificates and gifts. And the whole thing runs from morning to evening.

Here is where Norda fits in.

Chandaria x Urban Gang Tour: Hygiene Partner for Kenya's Girls Schools
Dear Chandaria Industries Marketing and Design Teams,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah, and alongside my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, which reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally.

Let me explain.

We have three schools confirmed for Term 2, 2026:

30th May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1st June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4th July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

All three are girls schools.

Chandaria Industries has been donating Rosy Girl sanitary pads to girls schools for years. You partnered with She Needs a Chance to host girls from Mukuru for menstrual hygiene education. You are a listed partner of the Menstrual Hygiene Day Organization. You donated 8,000 packs of Velvex sanitary pads to the Kisumu Youth Football Association. You have been doing this work quietly and consistently because you understand that a girl who misses school because she cannot afford a pad is a girl whose potential is being wasted.

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose.

We go into these girls schools with 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run the full day with them. Talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. Mentorship sessions on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. A counselling tent staffed by trained counsellors from our church partner, Destiny Life Church. And we film the whole thing and broadcast it on PPP TV to millions of Kenyans.

But here is what we do not have yet: a hygiene partner.

We are walking into girls schools where hundreds of students need exactly what Chandaria manufactures. Sanitary pads. Hand sanitizer. Wet wipes. Tissue. These are not luxuries. They are essentials. And right now, we have no partner providing them.

Here is what we are proposing.

We want Chandaria Industries to be the Official Hygiene Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is what that looks like in practice:

1. Velvex and Rosy Girl sanitary pads distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can be done through the counselling tent, through a dedicated Chandaria hygiene booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand out to winners on stage.

2. A Chandaria branded hygiene booth at every event. This is your space to educate students on menstrual hygiene, hand hygiene, and personal care. You can staff it with your own team or we can manage it on your behalf. This is your existing CSR programme happening at scale, on camera, on national TV.

3. Velvex hand sanitizer and wet wipes at the counselling tent and backstage areas. Branded and visible. Students and staff using your products throughout the day.

4. Chandaria products inside every gift hamper. Winners receive certificates and gift hampers on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Your products are physically in their hands during the biggest moment of the day.

5. Stage backdrop and teardrop branding. Chandaria's logo alongside UGT on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops around the venue, and on printed programmes given to every student.

6. PPP TV broadcast credits. Chandaria Industries acknowledged as Official Hygiene Partner in every Urban News episode. That is up to 8 million viewers seeing your CSR in action.

7. Social media coverage. Every event produces 8 to 12 short form clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The hygiene booth, the pad distribution, the counselling tent. All of it becomes content that shows Chandaria doing real work for real girls in real schools.

Why this matters for Chandaria.

You have been doing this CSR work for years. But most of it happens quietly. A donation here, a school visit there. What we are offering is a national platform to amplify what you are already doing. When Velvex pads are handed to a girl at a UGT event, that moment is captured on three cameras, edited into a PPP TV episode, posted across social media, and broadcast to millions. That is not just CSR. That is brand building at the highest level.

And here is the part that no other partnership can offer you: every single school on our current calendar is a girls school. You are not sponsoring a generic event and hoping your products are relevant. You are walking directly into the rooms where your products matter most.

Chandaria has been the largest tissue and hygiene manufacturer in East and Central Africa for over 55 years. You are ISO certified. You have Superbrands status. You won the FMCG Tissue Brand of the Year in 2024 and 2025. You are partnered with WWF Kenya. You manufacture 100% in Kenya. Your Buy Kenya Build Kenya commitment is real.

Urban Gang Tour is the vehicle that takes all of that and puts it in front of the next generation of Kenyan consumers and leaders. The girls we reach today are the women who will be buying your products for the next 50 years.

We have attached:

1. The UGT Partner Catalogue, which gives a full overview of the programme, our team, our reach, and all partnership opportunities.

Lucy and I would love to visit your offices and walk through this with your marketing and design teams in person. 20 to 30 minutes is all we need. We are happy to work around your schedule.

Please let us know a date and time that works, and we will be there.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah & Lucy Ogunde

Dear Kenya Wildlife Service Team,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah. Together with my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, reaching up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because at every school we visit, we plant trees. And we want to do it properly, with KWS.

Here is the situation. Urban Gang Tour runs full day events in high schools across Kenya. We bring 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run talent competitions, mentorship sessions, modelling showcases, and awards ceremonies. Every event is filmed and broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers.

As part of every school visit, we run a tree planting session. Our current liaison is a team member called Tai, who leads what we call the Green Movement within our programme. He coordinates with students, scouts, Kenya Girl Guides, and the school principal to plant trees on school grounds. The scouts or Girl Guides then take long-term stewardship of the trees.

But right now we are doing this without an official partner who can supply the right seedlings, provide planting guidance, and ensure the trees actually survive and grow. That is where KWS comes in.

The Presidential Directive is targeting the planting of over 1.5 billion trees annually for 10 years. KWS has been at the forefront of this, hosting tree planting exercises at Nairobi National Park and across the country. Your mandate explicitly includes engaging the public, youth, and communities through education and outreach. Your partners include schools and institutions of learning.

Urban Gang Tour is a direct pipeline into those schools. We are already there. We already have the students assembled. We already have the scouts and Girl Guides mobilized. We already have the principal participating. We already film everything for national television. What we need is KWS to supply the seedlings, send a facilitator for the planting session, and bring the credibility and expertise of Kenya's leading conservation authority to what we are doing.

Here is what we are proposing:

KWS supplies indigenous tree seedlings for every school we visit. We are talking 50 to 100 seedlings per school, planted by students, guests, the principal, and scouts during our tree planting session at 9:20 AM each event day.

KWS sends a facilitator or ranger to lead the planting session and give a short talk on conservation, climate, and why trees matter. This does not have to be long. 10 to 15 minutes. But it has to come from KWS.

KWS is acknowledged as the Official Conservation Partner on stage, on the backdrop, in the printed programme, and in the PPP TV broadcast. Up to 8 million viewers seeing KWS doing exactly what the Presidential Directive calls for, in schools, with young people.

KWS can set up an information stand or display at the event to promote wildlife conservation, the National Parks, and the tree planting initiative.

We can co-brand the tree planting session as a KWS x UGT initiative. Every tree planted gets tagged. Every session gets filmed. Every school becomes a documented contribution to the 1.5 billion tree target.

This is not a one-off. We have three schools confirmed this term (30 May, 1 June, and 4 July) and we are booking more every week. This programme runs term after term, expanding countrywide. Every school we visit is another tree planting session. Another 50 to 100 trees. Another group of students who learn about conservation from KWS, not from a textbook.

We have attached the UGT Partner Catalogue for your review.

We would love to visit your offices and walk through this in person. Even 15 minutes is enough.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde

That is a crowd. That is energy. That is the kind of environment where someone can faint from the heat, trip on a cable, have a panic attack backstage, or need basic medical attention. And right now, we do not have a medical partner on the ground.

Here is what we are proposing:

St John Ambulance becomes the Official Medical Partner of Urban Gang Tour. At every school we visit, St John provides:

A standby ambulance on site for the duration of the event (approximately 5 AM to 6 PM).

A first aid team of 2 to 3 trained responders stationed at the venue.

A first aid tent or station clearly branded with St John signage, positioned near the main stage and student areas.

A short first aid training session for students. This is the part that makes this different from just providing medical cover. We want St John to use 15 to 20 minutes during our event to teach students the basics of first aid. How to handle fainting. How to stop bleeding. Recovery position. CPR awareness. This can happen during our Urban Pod sessions in the morning, or as a standalone segment during the main event.

Support for establishing or strengthening the St John Cadet unit at each school we visit. You already run the Safe Schools Initiative. You already have national cadet competitions. Every school we go to is an opportunity to recruit new cadets, activate dormant units, or strengthen existing ones.

Re: NACADA x Urban Gang Tour: Drug Prevention in Every School We Visit
Dear NACADA Communications Team,

Thank you for the prompt response. We are glad to hear the proposal is under review.

Here is our comprehensive calendar for Term 2, 2026:

30th May 2026 - Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1st June 2026 - Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4th July 2026 - Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

All three are girls schools. The NACADA Drug Prevention Pod would run at 8:10 AM at each school (30 minutes, simultaneous with our other Urban Pods). NACADA would also have a branded booth open from 9:30 AM through the end of the event at approximately 5:30 PM.

Additional schools for Term 3, 2026 are currently being booked and we will share those dates as they are confirmed.

Regarding budgetary requirements from NACADA's end:

We have no financial ask from NACADA. What we need is people and materials. Specifically:

1. One NACADA facilitator or trained peer educator per school event to lead the 30-minute Drug Prevention Pod
2. Educational materials, brochures, and branded items for distribution to students (NACADA to determine quantities based on expected student numbers, typically 300 to 1,000 per school)

**1.  THE ALIGNMENT**

NACADA's 2026 theme is "Empowered Communities, Brighter Futures." Urban Gang Tour's motto is "From Potential to Purpose." Both organisations exist to reach Kenya's young people before it is too late and equip them with the tools, the resilience, and the purpose to make better choices.

This document outlines exactly how NACADA and Urban Gang Tour work together as Official Drug Prevention Partners. It details what NACADA provides, where NACADA appears throughout the event day, and the branding and visibility that NACADA receives at every school visit, on PPP TV, and across all social media platforms.

This is a non-financial partnership. NACADA does not need to commit any funds. What NACADA provides are people, materials, and permission to use the NACADA brand. In return, NACADA receives national visibility, direct access to thousands of students, PPP TV broadcast coverage to up to 8 million viewers, and sustained countrywide presence in Kenyan high schools term after term.

**2.  WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | **High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools** |
| --- | --- |
| Motto | From Potential to Purpose |
| Broadcast | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| Daily Reach | 400,000 to millions via Urban News, hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde |
| Team Size | 30+ professionals per event (hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, mentors, counsellors, security) |
| Content Per Event | 1 full TV episode (25 to 30 min) plus 8 to 12 social media clips across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts |
| Confirmed Schools | 30 May: Koinange Girls  │  1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls  │  4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| Key Fact | All three confirmed schools are girls schools. More being booked weekly. Expanding countrywide. |
| Partners | Destiny Life Church (co-production), PPP TV Kenya (broadcast), The Experience Hub (hype), The Green Movement (environmental) |
| Contact | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde  │  euginemicah@gmail.com  │  @urban_newsgang |

**3.  WHAT NACADA PROVIDES (NON-FINANCIAL)**

**NACADA****'****s contribution is entirely non-financial. Here are the four provisions we are requesting:**

**Provision 1: One Facilitator Per School**

A trained NACADA facilitator or peer educator to lead the Drug and Substance Abuse Prevention Pod at each of the three confirmed schools. The facilitator must speak in Gen Z-friendly language. Real talk. Real stories. Not a classroom lecture. The kind of conversation that actually lands with young people.

| **Detail** | **Requirement** |
| --- | --- |
| Arrival Time | 7:30 AM on event day for crew briefing at 8:00 AM |
| Pod Start Time | 8:10 AM (runs simultaneously with 4 other Urban Pods) |
| Duration | 30 minutes per session |
| Group Size | 20 to 40 students per session |
| Format | Informal. Small group. Conversational. English and Sheng mixed. No PowerPoints. |
| Topics to Cover | Alcohol, drugs, peer pressure, laced edibles, prescription abuse, the 1192 helpline, making drug-free choices |
| After the Pod | Facilitator available for one-on-one conversations with students from 8:40 AM to 9:30 AM |
| End of Day | Facilitator interviewed on camera for PPP TV episode and social media content |

**Provision 2: Educational Materials**

NACADA to supply printed and branded materials for distribution to students at every school. Distributed through the NACADA booth and the counselling tent throughout the day.

| **Material** | **Suggested Quantity Per School** | **Purpose** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| NACADA Brochures | 300 to 500 | Student takeaway with drug prevention information and helpline details |
| 1192 Helpline Cards | 300 to 500 | Wallet-sized card with the NACADA helpline. Students keep these. |
| 2026 Essay Competition Flyers | 200 to 300 | MCs promote the competition from stage. Flyers distributed at booth. |
| Branded Wristbands or Pens | 200 to 300 (if available) | Branded items students take home as reminders of the NACADA message |
| Posters for Display | 4 to 6 | Displayed around the venue and at the NACADA booth on event day |

**Provision 3: One Pull-Up Banner or Display Stand**

NACADA to provide one branded pull-up banner or display stand for the NACADA information booth. The booth is open from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM. NACADA can staff the booth or UGT can manage it on NACADA's behalf.

**Provision 4: Logo Permission**

NACADA to grant permission for Urban Gang Tour to use the official NACADA logo as Official Drug Prevention Partner across all event materials and platforms. All designs will be submitted to the NACADA Communications team for approval before any printing or publication. NACADA retains full control over how its brand is represented.

| **Asset** | **NACADA Branding Usage** |
| --- | --- |
| Event Posters | NACADA logo on all printed and digital event posters for every school visit |
| Stage Backdrop | NACADA logo on the main T-shaped stage backdrop. Visible in every camera frame and every PPP TV broadcast. |
| Printed Programmes | NACADA logo on the event programme distributed to every student and teacher |
| Social Media Graphics | NACADA logo on all event announcement graphics, recap posts, and content across Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and YouTube |
| PPP TV Credits | NACADA acknowledged as Official Drug Prevention Partner in every Urban News episode. Up to 8 million viewers. |
| Post-Production Video | NACADA logo, name, and 1192 helpline featured in the dedicated awareness video produced from event footage and aired on PPP TV |

**4.  FULL PROGRAMME WITH NACADA TOUCHPOINTS**

**The table below shows the complete event day programme and marks exactly where NACADA appears. NACADA is not a side note. NACADA is woven into the fabric of the entire day.**

# **20% MARKET SHARE BY 2026. THE CLOCK IS TICKING.**

Nala Noodles launched in 2023 through Kapa Foods Innovations Limited, a joint venture between Kapa Oil Refineries and Sojitz Corporation. The target was clear: capture 20% of the East African instant noodle market by 2026.

That deadline is this year.

Indomie has been in Kenya since 2009. They have 17 years of head start. They have brand loyalty that runs deep. You cannot outspend them in advertising. You cannot out-distribute them overnight. But you can do something they are not doing: show up in schools, put Nala in students' hands, and build the kind of brand memory that shelf space alone will never create.

Urban Gang Tour is the fastest, most cost-effective way to do exactly that.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **WHY NALA NEEDS UGT**

**You cannot outspend Indomie. **But you can out-experience them. A student who eats Nala while watching her friend win a dance competition will remember that moment forever. That is brand equity you cannot buy with a billboard

**"****Nala****"**** means ****"****gift****"**** in Swahili. **Our events are literally a gift to students. The name alignment is perfect for co-branded messaging: "The Gift of Talent. The Gift of Purpose. The Gift of Nala"

**You are manufactured by Kapa Oil on Mombasa Road. **Our church partner Destiny Life Church is in Syokimau. Our team operates from this area. We are neighbours. Local partnership, national reach

**Your Thai technical partner brings quality. **Thai President Foods developed Nala's recipes for East African taste preferences. The product is good. It just needs to be in the right hands at the right moment. UGT creates that moment

**20% by 2026 is still possible. **But only if you move now. Every school UGT visits this term is a market you can claim before anyone else. 3 schools confirmed. More booking. Countrywide

# **WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING**

| **Official Noodle Partner** | Nala becomes the exclusive noodle brand at UGT events. No competing noodle brands on site |
| --- | --- |
| **Product Supply** | Nala supplies noodles at wholesale or sponsored rates for Urban Gang Snacks. Students buy affordable Nala all day |
| **Branded Booth** | A Nala tent or stand at every event. Branding, sampling, student engagement |

| **Gift Hamper Inclusion** | Nala packs in every winner's gift hamper. On stage, on camera, on PPP TV |
| **Co-Branded Messaging** | "The Gift of Talent. The Gift of Purpose. The Gift of Nala." Plays on the Swahili meaning. Used on stage, in social media, on PPP TV |
| **Stage Branding** | Nala logo on backdrop, teardrops, programmes. PPP TV credits in every broadcast |
| **Social Media** | 8 to 12 clips per event. Cooking station, hamper moments, students eating Nala. Tagged across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts |
| **Buy Kenya Build Kenya** | MCs highlight Nala as a proudly Kenyan product made right here in Nairobi. Students support local. That narrative on PPP TV is more powerful than any import brand story |
| **Photobooth Co-Brand** | Nala logo on Urban Gang Moments instant prints. Students take your brand home on a photo they keep |

# **THE INDOMIE HEROES MODEL. NOW IN KENYA.**

In Nigeria, Indomie runs the Indomie Independence Day Award (IIDA). Every year, Dufil Prima Foods searches for children who have shown exceptional bravery, and celebrates them on a national stage. The IIDA has become one of Nigeria's most celebrated CSR initiatives. It has been running for over 12 editions. It is part of the reason Indomie consistently ranks as one of the top consumer brands in Nigeria, ahead of Coca-Cola and Maggi.

Urban Gang Tour does the same thing in Kenya. We go into schools, discover talented and courageous students, and celebrate them on stage, on camera, and on national TV. The format is different. The spirit is identical.

This proposal is about bringing the Indomie Heroes model to Kenya through Urban Gang Tour.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

# **THE PROPOSAL: THREE LAYERS**

## **Layer 1: Official Noodle Partner**

Indomie becomes the official noodle brand at every Urban Gang Tour event. Here is what that looks like:

| **Product Supply** | Indomie supplies noodles at wholesale or sponsored rates for sale through Urban Gang Snacks, our on-site snack shop. Students buy affordable Indomie all day |
| --- | --- |
| **Branded Booth** | An Indomie tent or stand at every event. Branding, sampling, direct engagement with students |
| **Live Cooking Station** | A live Indomie cooking station where students watch their noodles being prepared. This becomes TikTok content. Sizzling noodles on camera is the most shareable food content on social media |
| **Gift Hamper Inclusion** | Indomie packs inside every winner's gift hamper. On stage, on camera, on PPP TV to 8 million viewers |
| **Stage Branding** | Indomie logo on the backdrop, teardrops, printed programmes |
| **PPP TV Credits** | Indomie acknowledged as Official Noodle Partner in every broadcast |
| **Social Media** | 8 to 12 clips per event. Students eating Indomie, the cooking station sizzling, hampers being opened. All tagged across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts |

## **Layer 2: The Indomie UGT Award**

This is the big idea. Inspired by the IIDA in Nigeria, we co-create a special award at every school:

| **The Award** | The Indomie UGT Award for Courage and Character. Given to one student per school who has shown exceptional bravery, kindness, or leadership. Nominated by teachers and peers |
| --- | --- |
| **Who Presents It** | The MCs (Eugine and Lucy) announce it from the main stage. "This award is brought to you by Indomie." The student is called up. Applause. Certificate. Indomie branded gift hamper. Photo |
| **Why It Matters** | In Nigeria, the IIDA made Indomie synonymous with bravery and values. In Kenya, the Indomie UGT Award does the same. Your brand stops being just noodles. It becomes a symbol of courage in the eyes of every student in every school we visit |
| **PPP TV Feature** | The Indomie UGT Award winner is featured in the Urban News episode. Their story told to 8 million viewers. Indomie's name attached to an emotional, inspiring moment on national television |
| **Annual Compilation** | At the end of each year, we produce a compilation video of all Indomie UGT Award winners. Aired on PPP TV. Posted across all social media. A standalone Indomie campaign built entirely from UGT content |

## **Layer 3: Competitive Defence**

Nala Noodles launched in 2023 backed by Kapa Oil Refineries and Sojitz Corporation from Japan. They are targeting 20% of the East African noodle market by 2026. That deadline is this year. They are manufactured locally. They have the distribution power of Kapa Oil behind them.

Indomie has been in Kenya since 2009. You have 17 years of brand equity. But brand equity fades if someone else is showing up where the customers are. UGT puts Indomie in the hands of the next generation of noodle buyers before Nala can get there. Every school we visit is a market that Indomie owns first. That is not advertising. That is territorial defence.

# **THE GAP**

The National Youth Service was established in 1964 with one mandate: train young citizens to serve the nation. Since then, NYS has trained hundreds of thousands of Kenyan youth in vocational skills ranging from engineering and agriculture to textiles and hospitality. Your 5-Point Vision includes Vocational Training and Social Transformation, Enterprise and Youth Economy, and National Service and Youth Re-socialization.

But here is the gap. NYS recruits youth aged 18 to 24. Most of them have already finished secondary school. By the time they walk through your gates, their dreams have already been shaped by whatever they were exposed to in high school. If nobody told them about vocational pathways, they arrive at NYS by accident or desperation, not by aspiration.

Urban Gang Tour reaches students at 13 to 18. We are inside their schools. We have their attention. We have cameras rolling. We have PPP TV broadcasting to 8 million viewers. And right now, nobody is using that moment to tell these students that NYS exists, that vocational skills are valuable, and that there is a dignified, funded, structured pathway waiting for them after KCSE.

That is the gap. And this proposal closes it.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

# **WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING**

## **1. NYS Vocational Skills Pod**

A dedicated 30-minute pod session at every school led by an NYS facilitator. The facilitator talks to 20 to 40 students about what NYS offers: the trades available, what training looks like, what life after NYS can be, how to apply, and why vocational skills are just as valuable as a university degree. This is not a recruitment drive. It is a conversation. The kind that makes a student think "I have a future I did not know about."

| **Time** | 8:10 AM. Runs simultaneously with 4 other Urban Pods (financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, mental health) |
| --- | --- |
| **Duration** | 30 minutes |
| **Format** | Small group. 20 to 40 students. Informal. Gen Z language. Real stories from real NYS graduates |
| **Facilitator** | NYS provides 1 facilitator per event. Ideally a young NYS graduate who can relate to the students |
| **Materials** | NYS brochures, trade information sheets, recruitment dates, application process guides |
| **Recording** | Select sessions filmed as short-form content for PPP TV and social media |

## **2. NYS Information Booth**

A branded NYS booth or information stand open from 9:30 AM through the end of the event. Pull-up banners, brochures, trade samples, and a facilitator answering questions all day. Students and teachers can walk up, learn about NYS, and take home information. Peak foot traffic during tea break (9:35 AM), lunch (1:10 PM), and throughout the afternoon.

## **3. Stage Announcements**

Our MCs (Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde) announce NYS from the main stage at least twice during the event: during the opening ceremony and during the closing remarks. Hundreds to thousands of students hear about NYS in one moment. This is broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers.

## **4. Textile Institute x Modelling Showcase**

NYS operates the Textile and Garment Training Institute in Nairobi. We run a modelling showcase at every school with four runway categories: Cultural/Traditional Wear, Creative Wear, Cosplay, and Professional Wear. NYS can co-brand the modelling segment and showcase what the Textile Institute produces. If NYS can supply sample garments made by recruits, students walk the runway in NYS-made clothing. That is the most powerful recruitment tool you can imagine: a student wearing something made by someone their age at NYS and feeling proud doing it.

## **5. Post-Production NYS Content**

Our production team (3 cameras, PPP TV editor) produces a full Urban News episode from each school event. We can produce a standalone NYS awareness segment (2 to 5 minutes) from the event footage: the pod in action, the booth, students learning about NYS, the textile showcase moment. This is aired on PPP TV and posted across our social media. NYS can also use this content on your own platforms.

# **FOUR ALIGNMENT POINTS. ONE PARTNERSHIP.**

Dr. James Mwangi said it at the 16th Annual Education and Leadership Congress, April 2025: "We are cultivating more than scholars. We are developing a movement of purpose-driven leaders who will redefine Kenya's future. Where potential meets opportunity."

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose. The language is identical. The mission is identical. The young people we are serving are identical.

Equity Group Foundation has raised KES 98 billion cumulatively. 60,009 scholars through Wings to Fly and Elimu. 23,825 through the Equity Leaders Program. 891 scholars at 199 universities across 36 countries. This is the most powerful youth institution in Kenya.

Urban Gang Tour goes into the schools where those scholars study, with 30 professionals, cameras, DJs, mentors, and a national TV broadcast. We are the vehicle that takes financial literacy, scholar recognition, and youth empowerment directly into those schools in a format that Gen Z actually connects with.

This proposal covers four alignment points between Equity Group Foundation and Urban Gang Tour. Each one stands on its own. Together, they make this the most natural partnership on either of our desks.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical. All girls schools |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **ALIGNMENT 1: OWN YOUR MONEY POD**

We run a 30-minute financial literacy pod at every school called "Own Your Money." A professional sits with 20 to 40 students and talks about savings, budgeting, entrepreneurship, and building a financial future. In Gen Z language. No lectures. Just real talk.

We want Equity Group Foundation to own this pod.

| **What Changes** | The pod is renamed to "Equity Financial Futures" or "Equity Own Your Money." Equity branding on the pod room. Equity materials in students' hands |
| --- | --- |
| **Who Leads It** | An Equity facilitator, ELP alumnus, or Wings to Fly graduate. Someone young enough for students to relate to, but accomplished enough to inspire them |
| **What They Teach** | Savings culture, budgeting, entrepreneurship, financial goal-setting, how to open a bank account, why financial literacy matters. Equity's own curriculum or a version of it |
| **Materials Distributed** | Equity branded savings guides, financial literacy workbooks, information on Wings to Fly, ELP, and Equity youth products |
| **Duration** | 30 minutes at 8:10 AM. Runs simultaneously with 4 other Urban Pods |
| **Recording** | Select sessions filmed as short-form content for PPP TV and social media |
| **Why This Matters** | Students learn about banking from Equity before any other bank reaches them. You are planting the seed at 13 to 18 years old. By the time they open their first account, Equity is already the name they trust |

# **ALIGNMENT 2: WINGS TO FLY SCHOLAR SPOTLIGHT**

Wings to Fly has 60,009 scholars across Kenya. Some of them are at the exact schools we visit. We can celebrate them.

| **What Happens** | At every school that has Wings to Fly or Elimu scholars, we invite them on stage during the main event. The MCs introduce them. The school applauds. A photo is taken. The moment is filmed and included in the PPP TV broadcast |
| --- | --- |
| **Why This Matters** | Wings to Fly scholars are often the brightest but quietest students. They do not get public recognition. UGT gives them a moment of celebration in front of their entire school, on camera, broadcast to 8 million viewers. That is life-changing visibility |
| **Scholarship Promotion** | Our MCs promote Wings to Fly and Elimu applications from the stage. Information distributed to students and teachers. QR codes to the application portal displayed on venue signage |
| **Content Produced** | Scholar spotlight moments edited into standalone videos for Equity's own platforms. Free professional content |

# **ALIGNMENT 3: ELP PRE-PIPELINE**

The Equity Leaders Program selects 750 top KCSE performers each year. They go through a 6-month paid internship at Equity branches and then proceed to university. The programme integrates financial literacy, mentorship, and leadership training.

Our Own Your Money pod is the pre-pipeline for ELP. We reach students at 13 to 18, 2 to 5 years before they sit for KCSE. By the time they qualify for ELP, they already know Equity. They already have the savings mindset. They already see Equity as the institution that believed in them first.

**The numbers: **If we visit 10 schools per year and each school has 500 students, that is 5,000 students exposed to Equity's financial literacy curriculum annually. Over 5 years, that is 25,000 students. Some of them will become ELP scholars. All of them will become Equity customers

# **ALIGNMENT 4: CONGRESS AND CONFERENCE CO-HOSTING**

Your 16th Annual Congress brought 3,200 scholars from 7 counties together for mentorship, leadership training, and celebration. We run a similar programme: after touring multiple schools, we host a conference day at Destiny Life Church in Syokimau where winners and top pod participants come together for mentorship, workshops, and gifts.

We could co-host this conference day with Equity Group Foundation. Your scholars meet our winners. Two movements of purpose-driven young people in one room. Financial literacy sessions led by Equity. Talent showcases by UGT winners. All filmed for PPP TV.

# **3. HOW NACADA CAN SUPPORT THE PROGRAMME**

We understand NACADA operates within a government mandate and budget structure. We have designed multiple support options so you can choose what fits your capacity. These are not demands. These are a menu. Pick what works.

## **Option A: Facilitator and Materials Only (No Financial Cost)**

| **What NACADA Provides** | 1 trained facilitator or peer educator per school event. Educational materials, brochures, branded items, 1192 helpline cards, essay competition flyers. A pull-up banner for the NACADA booth |
| --- | --- |
| **What UGT Handles** | All event logistics, venue access, stage, sound, security, production, filming, broadcast, social media. Everything |
| **NACADA****'****s Cost** | Facilitator transport to school venue. Materials printing. That is it |
| **Best For** | If NACADA wants to participate with zero additional budget outlay beyond existing field operations |

## **Option B: Facilitator + Material Supply + Branded Items for Students**

| **What NACADA Provides** | Everything in Option A, plus: NACADA branded wristbands, pens, notebooks, or stickers for students (200 to 500 per school). These become physical reminders that students take home |
| --- | --- |
| **Estimated Cost to NACADA** | KES 20,000 to 50,000 per school for branded items production (depending on quantities and items selected) |
| **Best For** | If NACADA wants tangible student takeaways that extend the prevention message beyond the event day |

## **Option C: Segment Sponsorship**

| **What NACADA Sponsors** | NACADA sponsors a specific segment of the event day. For example: "The NACADA Awareness Session" branded as a standalone 15-minute stage segment during the main programme where the facilitator addresses the entire student body from the main stage (not just the pod). MCs introduce it. Cameras roll. PPP TV captures it |
| --- | --- |
| **Estimated Contribution** | KES 50,000 to 100,000 per school. This covers the segment branding, printed materials, and a contribution towards UGT's production costs |
| **Best For** | If NACADA wants maximum visibility on stage and on camera, beyond just the pod |

## **Option D: Production Support (Stage and Sound Contribution)**

| **What This Covers** | A financial contribution towards the cost of stage and sound setup for each school event. Stage and sound are the most expensive production elements and are the backbone of every event. A contribution here directly enables the programme to happen |
| --- | --- |
| **Estimated Contribution** | KES 50,000 to 150,000 per school towards stage and sound costs |
| **What NACADA Gets** | Everything in Option A plus: enhanced branding as a Production Support Partner. NACADA logo given prominent placement on the backdrop. Extended PPP TV credits. Social media posts specifically thanking NACADA for enabling the event |
| **Best For** | If NACADA has budget allocation for youth outreach activations and wants to directly enable the programme infrastructure |

## **Option E: Post-Production and Broadcasting Support**

| **What This Covers** | A financial contribution towards the post-production of NACADA-specific awareness content from the event footage. Our production team (3 cameras, PPP TV editor Rania Martin) produces a standalone NACADA awareness segment (2 to 5 minutes) from each school event. This segment is aired on Urban News on PPP TV and distributed across all UGT social media platforms |
| --- | --- |
| **Estimated Contribution** | KES 30,000 to 80,000 per school for post-production of the NACADA awareness segment |
| **What NACADA Gets** | A professionally produced awareness video using real footage from real schools with real students, aired on national TV to up to 8 million viewers and posted across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. This is content NACADA can also use on its own platforms |
| **Best For** | If NACADA wants broadcast-quality prevention content produced at a fraction of what it would cost to commission independently |

## **Option F: Full Partnership Package (Combination)**

| **What This Includes** | Everything from Options A through E combined. Facilitator, materials, branded student items, a stage segment, production support, and post-production awareness content |
| --- | --- |
| **Estimated Total** | KES 150,000 to 350,000 per school (covering all elements) |
| **What NACADA Gets** | The complete package. NACADA is embedded into every part of the event: pod, booth, stage segment, backdrop, PPP TV, social media, post-production content. Maximum impact. Maximum visibility. Maximum reach |
| **Across 3 Schools** | KES 450,000 to 1,050,000 for the full Term 2 programme (3 schools). This is a fraction of what a standalone campaign of this scale would cost |

# **5. POST-PRODUCTION: NACADA AWARENESS CAMPAIGNS ON PPP TV**

Every UGT school event is filmed by 3 professional cameras and edited by Rania Martin, a PPP TV editor. From the footage of each event, we produce a full 25 to 30 minute Urban News episode broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers.

For NACADA, we can produce additional awareness content from the same footage:

| **NACADA Awareness Segment** | A standalone 2 to 5 minute video produced from the event footage showing the NACADA pod in action, students engaging with the booth, the facilitator talking to young people, and the MCs announcing the drug prevention message from stage. Professionally edited. Broadcast quality |
| --- | --- |
| **PPP TV Airing** | This segment is included in the Urban News episode or aired as a standalone feature on PPP TV. Up to 8 million viewers |
| **Social Media Distribution** | The segment is cut into 15 to 60 second clips and distributed across UGT's Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and Facebook. Tagged with NACADA handles. Hashtagged with #NACADAKenya and campaign-specific tags |
| **NACADA****'****s Own Use** | All footage and edited content featuring NACADA is shared with NACADA for use on your own platforms, reports, presentations, and campaigns. Free of charge |
| **IDADA Content** | Around June 26 (IDADA), we produce a special NACADA awareness compilation from all school events to date. Aired on PPP TV and posted across all platforms. Timed for maximum relevance |
| **Essay Competition Promotion** | We produce a 30 to 60 second video promoting the NACADA essay competition, featuring our MCs encouraging students to enter. Aired on Urban News and posted on social media |

x

**URBAN GANG TOUR**

**PUBLIC DIPLOMACY SMALL GRANT APPLICATION**

Promoting Economic Prosperity Through Youth Empowerment

euginemicah@gmail.com | @urban_newsgang | April 2026

# **THE ALIGNMENT**

The U.S. Embassy Nairobi Public Diplomacy Section provides small grants for programmes that strengthen ties between the United States and Kenya. Your priority areas include promoting economic prosperity through innovation and entrepreneurship, and your target audience explicitly includes youth aged 15 to 30 and residents of underserved communities.

Urban Gang Tour runs mentorship pods on entrepreneurship (Own Your Money), digital skills (Go Digital), and leadership (Lead From Where You Are) in high schools across Kenya. We broadcast everything on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers. We are a registered Kenyan organisation (BN-6ASYVPW5) doing exactly the kind of work your small grants programme was designed to fund.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Schools (Term 2)** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Long term. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **PROGRAMME ALIGNMENT WITH PDS PRIORITIES**

| **Economic Prosperity** | Our Own Your Money Urban Pod teaches financial literacy, entrepreneurship, budgeting, and side-hustle culture to high school students. This is skills-based economic empowerment for the next generation |
| --- | --- |
| **Youth Empowerment** | Talent competitions, mentorship, awards, and follow-up programmes. We do not just visit schools. We establish Urban Gang Clubs and follow up with winners on national TV |
| **Innovation** | Our Go Digital pod covers content creation, coding basics, AI, and digital careers. We teach students to be creators, not just consumers of technology |
| **Underserved Communities** | We go to schools across Kenya, not just Nairobi. Our programme is expanding countrywide |

# **THE AMERICAN ELEMENT**

All PDS grants require an American cultural element or connection. Here is how UGT incorporates this:

**Entrepreneurship Model: **The Own Your Money pod is built on American entrepreneurship principles. We teach students the startup mindset, pitching, and lean business models

**Digital Skills: **The Go Digital pod introduces American tech platforms, coding concepts, and digital career paths that connect Kenyan students to the global digital economy

**Potential US Speaker: **We can incorporate an American entrepreneur or digital professional (in person or virtual) into the pod sessions at select schools

# **GRANT REQUEST**

**Amount: **$15,000 to $25,000 (typical PDS small grant range)

**Use: **Funding for pod facilitators, educational materials, content production, and school expansion for Term 2 and Term 3, 2026

**Application: **Submit to NairobiGrants@state.gov

**Eligibility: **Registered not-for-profit organisations. UGT is registered as Urban Gang Merch (BN-6ASYVPW5). Note: for-profit entities are not eligible for PDS grants, so UGT may need to apply through a partner NGO or register as an NGO

# **ALIGNMENT 1: GREEN INITIATIVE x TREE PLANTING**

KCB Foundation's Green Initiative supports community resilience through tree planting, water harvesting, and environmental awareness. You work with schools, government institutions, and youth groups. You planted 2,000 trees at Brookshine Schools with Chandaria Foundation and Global Peace Foundation. You supported 1,000 saplings at Oloosho-Oibor Primary School in Kajiado.

Urban Gang Tour plants trees at every single school we visit. Every event includes a tree planting session at 9:20 AM led by Tai, our Green Movement liaison. Students, scouts, Kenya Girl Guides, and the principal all participate. The session is filmed by three cameras and included in every PPP TV broadcast.

## **What KCB Can Do**

| **Contribution to 15 Billion Trees** | Every tree planted at a UGT event is documented and can be counted toward KCB's contribution to the Presidential Directive. 50 to 100 trees per school, filmed and broadcast |

# **ALIGNMENT 2: 2JIAJIRI x OWN YOUR MONEY POD**

KCB Foundation's 2Jiajiri programme has trained over 35,000 youth and created over 150,000 jobs since 2016. It teaches vocational skills, financial literacy, business development, and entrepreneurship. KCB has set aside USD 500 million for this initiative.

Urban Gang Tour runs an Urban Pod at every school called "Own Your Money." This is a 30-minute mentorship session where a professional talks to students about entrepreneurship, budgeting, pitching ideas, and building a side hustle. It is exactly the same spirit as 2Jiajiri, but delivered to students before they even leave school.

## **What KCB Can Do**

# **YOU SELL. THEY BUY. WE ALL WIN.**

Urban Gang Tour goes into high schools across Kenya and spends the full day with students. Talent competitions, mentorship, modelling, hype sessions, and awards. We bring 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crew. And hundreds to thousands of hungry students.

That is where you come in.

We are looking for food vendors, snack sellers, and small business owners who want to set up shop at our events and sell directly to students. You bring your product. We bring you the crowd.

# **HOW IT WORKS**

| **The Event** | A full day programme at a high school. 5 AM to 5:30 PM. Students are on site from 8 AM. There is a tea break at 9:35 AM, lunch break at 1:10 PM, and foot traffic throughout the day |
| --- | --- |
| **Your Space** | We allocate you a vending area near the stage or in the designated vendor zone. You set up your own table, cart, or stall. We provide the space and the traffic. You provide everything else |
| **Your Responsibility** | You bring your own equipment, products, change, and staff. You set up and pack down your own space. You handle all your own sales. You keep all your revenue from sales |
| **Our Students** | Hundreds to thousands of students per event. They have pocket money. They are hungry. They are in a good mood. They want to eat. This is your ideal customer |
| **Timing** | Set up by 8 AM latest. Peak selling times: tea break (9:35 AM), lunch (1:10 PM), and throughout the afternoon. Pack down after 5:30 PM |

# **WHAT YOU CAN SELL**

Any food or snack that students love and that a school administration will allow on their grounds. Examples:

| **Hot Food** | Fries (chips), smokies, sausages, samosas, mandazi, chapati, bhajia, eggs |
| --- | --- |
| **Cold Snacks** | Crisps, biscuits, sweets, popcorn, candy floss, nuts, dried fruit |
| **Drinks** | Juice, water, soda, milkshake, smoothies, yoghurt |
| **Street Food** | Mutura, mahindi (roasted maize), mishkaki, chips mwitu |
| **Baked Goods** | Cakes, doughnuts, muffins, scones, cookies |
| **Other** | Ice cream, cotton candy, fresh fruit, packed lunch boxes |

If you sell it and students buy it, you are welcome. We just ask that it is hygienic, safe, and appropriate for a school setting.

# **HOW YOU SUPPORT THE MOVEMENT**

Vending at UGT events is not just a selling opportunity. It is a partnership. Here is how you can support the movement while growing your own business:

| **Option 1: Vendor Fee** | A small flat vendor fee per event (amount to be agreed). This helps us cover logistics and keeps the vendor ecosystem organised. It is affordable and fixed so you know your costs upfront |
| --- | --- |
| **Option 2: Product Contribution** | Donate a portion of your products to the event. For example: contribute 50 servings of fries to the Urban Gang Snacks shop, or provide snacks for the gift hampers we give to winners on stage |
| **Option 3: Percentage Share** | Agree on a small percentage of your event-day sales (for example 10%) that goes to UGT. You keep the rest. Simple and fair |
| **Option 4: In-Kind Support** | Help with setup, cleanup, or logistics. Lend equipment. Provide tables, chairs, or umbrellas. Every bit of support helps |
| **Option 5: Branding Support** | If you have branded materials (banners, flags, tablecloths), bring them. A well-branded vendor area makes the whole event look better, and that benefits everyone |

We are flexible. If you have another way you want to support the movement, tell us. We will figure it out together. The point is that when you vend at a UGT event, you are part of something bigger than just selling fries. You are part of a programme that is changing the lives of young people across Kenya.

# **WHAT YOU GET**

| **Captive Audience** | Hundreds to thousands of students with money to spend. No competition from the school canteen because we ARE the event |
| --- | --- |
| **Repeat Business** | We run events every term, across the country. Become a regular vendor and you travel with us to every school |
| **Visibility** | Your brand seen by students, teachers, and parents. If your setup looks good, our cameras will capture it. That means social media and potentially PPP TV |
| **Community** | You join a vendor ecosystem alongside other small businesses and our major partners. Networking, connections, and a community of hustlers who get it |
| **Priority for Returning Vendors** | Vendors who show up, sell clean, and support the movement get first priority for the next event. Consistency earns loyalty |

# **VENDOR RULES**

| **Hygiene** | You must maintain clean and hygienic food handling at all times. No exceptions. If a school or health official asks to inspect your setup, you must comply |
| --- | --- |
| **Pricing** | Keep prices student-friendly. These are high schoolers, not corporate events. Fair pricing means more sales and more repeat customers |
| **No Alcohol** | No alcoholic beverages of any kind. This is a school event |
| **No Tobacco** | No cigarettes, shisha, or tobacco products |
| **Waste Management** | You clean up after yourself. Bring your own bin bags. Leave the space cleaner than you found it |
| **Respect the Programme** | Do not set up in front of the stage or block student pathways. Follow the directions of our coordination team (MC Paps and Fred) |
| **Setup and Packdown** | Be set up by 8 AM. Do not start packing until 5:30 PM unless told otherwise by our team |
| **Collaboration** | Be friendly with other vendors. Share the space. Share the customers. We are all in this together |
