# **WHY THIS PARTNERSHIP MATTERS**

65% of women in Kenya cannot afford safe menstrual products. That is 9.4 million women. One million school-age girls miss an average of four school days every month because of their period. 42% of Kenyan schoolgirls have never had access to a sanitary pad.

Binti Pads exists to change that. Urban Gang Tour exists to change that. We are both fighting the same fight. The only difference is the weapon. Binti uses pads. UGT uses a stage, a microphone, and a camera crew.

This proposal is about putting those weapons together.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |
| **Motto** | From Potential to Purpose |
| **Broadcast** | Urban News on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers nationally) |
| **Daily Reach** | 400,000 to millions via Urban News, hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde |
| **Team Size** | 30+ professionals per event |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Confirmed Schools** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Key Fact** | All three confirmed schools are GIRLS SCHOOLS |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals. Long term |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **WHY BINTI PADS**

We did not choose Binti Pads because you are the biggest. We chose you because you are the most real.

**Women-owned, women-led. **Binti was designed by women for women. In an industry dominated by male-owned multinationals, that matters. When Binti shows up at a girls school, it is not a corporation doing charity. It is a Kenyan woman who has been there, showing up for girls who are where she used to be.

**The founder****'****s story IS the motto. **Lorna Joyce grew up in Mukuru kwa Reuben. Lost her father and brother. Did laundry and hair to stay in school. Worked her way into aviation. Left a 13-year career to start making pads. That is From Potential to Purpose in the flesh. We want the girls at our events to hear that story.

**Affordable and accessible. **Binti regular pads at KES 75 and extra-long at KES 95 sit in exactly the price range that students and parents can realistically afford. You are not an out-of-reach international brand. You are the mid-market solution Kenya has been missing.

**Already in our zone. **Binti products are available in Nairobi, Kiambu, Kajiado, Machakos, and Mombasa. Our first three schools are in the Kiambu area. We are already in your distribution territory.

**400,000 women impacted. **Since 2022, Binti has reached 400,000 women. UGT can help you reach the next 600,000 faster than any marketing budget ever could.

**You need this as much as we do. **You rebooted in 2025 with a new local manufacturing model. You need growth channels that cost visibility, not millions in media spend. UGT is that channel: national TV, social media, direct school access, term after term, countrywide.

# **THE PROPOSAL**

Binti Pads becomes the Official Menstrual Health Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is exactly what that means:

## **1. Binti Pads Distributed at Every Girls School**

Binti pads and liners distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can happen through the counselling tent (run by Destiny Life Church counsellors), through a dedicated Binti booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand to winners on stage, on camera, on PPP TV. Every pad you distribute is seen by cameras, broadcast to millions, and posted across social media.

## **2. Binti Branded Booth**

A dedicated Binti Pads tent or booth at every event. Your space to talk to girls about menstrual health, period dignity, pad usage, and breaking stigma. Staff it with your own team or a trained educator. Students walk through, pick up products, ask questions, and learn. This is not a display. This is your existing mission happening at scale.

## **3. Menstrual Health Integration in the Mind Your Mind Pod**

We run a 30-minute Urban Pod on mental health and self-worth called Mind Your Mind. We can integrate a menstrual health education segment into this pod, led by a Binti representative. Alternatively, if the partnership grows, we can create a standalone Binti menstrual health pod. Either way, girls are hearing the Binti message in a small group setting where real conversations happen.

## **4. Binti Products in Every Gift Hamper**

Every talent category winner receives a certificate and a gift hamper on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Binti pads inside every hamper. Your products in the hands of winners during the most photographed, most filmed, most shared moment of the day.

## **5. Lorna Joyce as a Guest Speaker**

If Lorna is available, we would love to invite her to speak at select school events. Not as a product pitch. As a person. Her story of growing up in Mukuru, losing everything, fighting her way back, and building something that helps girls like the girl she used to be. That story will land harder than any TED Talk. And it will be filmed and broadcast to millions.

## **6. Stage Branding and PPP TV Credits**

Binti Pads logo on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops, on printed programmes. Binti acknowledged as Official Menstrual Health Partner in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. Up to 8 million viewers.

## **7. Social Media Coverage**

8 to 12 clips per event. The Binti booth, the pad distribution, the conversation at the counselling tent. Real girls, real schools, real impact. Tagged across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. This is the kind of authentic content that Gen Z shares and trusts, because it is real, not staged.

# **WHAT BINTI PADS GETS**

| **Direct Access to Girls Schools** | Hundreds to thousands of girls per event. Face to face. In their school. UGT handles all logistics, production, and access. Binti just shows up with pads and purpose |
| --- | --- |
| **National TV Visibility** | PPP TV broadcasts to up to 8 million viewers. Binti credited as Official Menstrual Health Partner. The kind of media exposure that would normally cost millions in advertising |

| **Brand Growth Engine** | You could not compete with multinationals on ad spend. UGT gives you what money cannot buy: real moments with real consumers on real television. This is how small brands become household names |
| **Distribution Channel** | Urban Gang Snacks and the event vendor ecosystem create a direct point of sale. Students can buy Binti pads at the event. You are not just giving pads away. You are building a customer base |
| **Repeat Nationwide Reach** | 3 schools this term. More next term. Expanding countrywide. Every school is a new market for Binti. Your brand compounds with every visit |
| **Impact Documentation** | Every pad distributed is documented on camera. You can use this content for your own marketing, for funding applications, for partnerships with NGOs and corporates. Proof of impact, filmed professionally and broadcast nationally |
| **Founder Story Platform** | Lorna's story is the most powerful marketing Binti has. UGT gives her a stage in front of thousands of girls who need to hear it. On camera. On TV. On social media |

# **THE NUMBERS**

# **HOW WE WORK TOGETHER**

We understand Binti is a growing business, not a multinational with unlimited budgets. We are not asking for money. We are asking for pads, presence, and purpose. Here is how we can make this work for a business your size:

**Product at cost or donated: **Binti supplies pads at cost price or as donated product. We handle all distribution, logistics, and setup at the school

# **WHY WE NEED ST JOHN AMBULANCE**

Urban Gang Tour runs full-day events in high school fields across Kenya. 30 professionals on site. Hundreds to thousands of students. A full stage with sound and lighting. DJs, dancers, hype sessions, competitions. The energy is high. The crowds are big. The day is long.

That is the kind of environment that needs professional medical cover. And right now, we do not have it.

We are looking for the best. That is St John Ambulance.

# **WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING**

St John Ambulance becomes the Official Medical Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is exactly what that means at every school we visit:

## **1. Standby Ambulance**

A St John ambulance on site for the duration of the event, approximately 8 AM to 6 PM. Parked in a visible, accessible location near the main venue.

## **2. First Aid Team**

2 to 3 trained St John responders stationed at the venue throughout the day. Positioned near the stage, the student audience area, and the backstage zone.

## **3. First Aid Tent**

A clearly branded St John first aid station at the venue. Visible signage. Basic medical supplies. A safe space where students or staff can come for treatment, rest, or attention.

## **4. Student First Aid Training Session**

This is the part that makes this more than medical cover. We want St John to deliver a 15 to 20 minute first aid training session to students during the event. This can happen in the morning during our Urban Pod sessions, or as a standalone segment during the main programme. Teach them the basics: how to handle fainting, how to stop bleeding, the recovery position, CPR awareness. These are life skills that stay with them forever.

## **5. St John Cadet Recruitment and Activation**

St John already runs national cadet competitions in schools. Kyeni Girls High School won your most recent national cadet competition. Your Safe Schools Initiative has reached over 13,000 students. Every school we visit is an opportunity to recruit new cadets, activate dormant St John units, or strengthen existing ones. We can give you 5 minutes on the main stage to address the students and invite them to join.

The Presidential Directive targets the planting of over 1.5 billion trees annually for 10 years. Kenya Wildlife Service is at the forefront of this effort. Urban Gang Tour goes into high schools across Kenya every term and plants trees at every single school we visit.

Right now we are doing it without a formal conservation partner. We have the students, the scouts, the principals, the cameras, and the national TV broadcast. What we need is the seedlings, the expertise, and the credibility that only KWS can bring.

# **THE ALIGNMENT**

NACADA's 2026 theme is "Empowered Communities, Brighter Futures." Urban Gang Tour's motto is "From Potential to Purpose." Both organisations exist to reach Kenya's young people before it is too late and give them the tools to make better choices.

NACADA's CEO, Dr. Anthony Omerikwa, said: "When we empower youth with purpose, resilience and decision-making skills, we are not just creating leaders. We are creating a critical deterrent against substance abuse." That sentence describes exactly what Urban Gang Tour does at every school we visit.

This proposal outlines how NACADA and UGT can work together to bring drug and substance abuse prevention directly into high schools across Kenya, at scale, on camera, and on national television.

# **WHO IS URBAN GANG TOUR**

| **Programme** | High school talent search, mentorship, competitions, and awards concerts going into Kenyan schools |
| --- | --- |

| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode plus 8 to 12 social media clips |
| **Confirmed Schools** | 30 May: Koinange Girls │ 1 June: Loreto Kiambu Girls │ 4 July: Gathirimu Girls Technical |
| **Scope** | Countrywide. Schools, universities, concerts, festivals. Long term |
| **Contact** | Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde │ euginemicah@gmail.com │ @urban_newsgang |

# **THE PROPOSAL: A NACADA POD AT EVERY SCHOOL**

Urban Gang Tour runs 5 Urban Pod sessions at every school visit. These are 30-minute mentorship breakouts where professionals sit with students in small groups and have real conversations in Gen Z language. No lectures. No stiffness. Just honest talk that lands.

## **Current 5 Pod Topics**

| **Pod 1: Discover Your Talent** | Performing arts: music, dance, spoken word, comedy, modelling |
| --- | --- |
| **Pod 2: Own Your Money** | Entrepreneurship and financial literacy: pitching ideas, budgeting, side hustle culture |
| **Pod 3: Go Digital** | Digital literacy and creative tech: content creation, coding, AI, digital careers |
| **Pod 4: Lead From Where You Are** | Leadership, public speaking, and career mentorship |
| **Pod 5: Mind Your Mind** | Mental health, self-worth, and purpose. Ties into the counselling tent |

## **Proposed 6th Pod: NACADA Drug Prevention Pod**

We are proposing that NACADA owns a dedicated 6th pod at every school we visit. Here is what that looks like:

| **Duration** | 30 minutes. Same as all other pods. Runs simultaneously at 8:10 AM |
| --- | --- |
| **Format** | Small group conversation. 20 to 40 students per session. Relaxed, honest, peer-to-peer energy |
| **Facilitator** | NACADA provides a trained facilitator or peer educator. Someone who speaks Gen Z |
| **Topics** | Real talk on alcohol, drugs, peer pressure, laced edibles, prescription abuse, making drug-free choices. Aligned with NACADA's school-based prevention curriculum |
| **Materials** | NACADA brochures, posters, branded items distributed to students. Information on the 1192 helpline |
| **Recording** | Select sessions can be recorded as short-form content for social media and PPP TV |
| **Language** | English and Sheng mixed. Must feel like a conversation, not a government programme |

# **BEYOND THE POD: FULL EVENT INTEGRATION**

The pod is the centrepiece. But NACADA's presence at UGT events can extend across the full day:

# **THE VISION**

*“Train up a child in the way he should go; even when he is old he will not depart from it.” — Proverbs 22:6*

Urban Gang Tour exists to go into Kenya’s schools and give young people a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams. Our motto — “From Potential to Purpose” — mirrors what Destiny Life Church’s Lit Gen already does every Sunday at 11:30 AM: taking young people and helping them discover their God-given gifts and live righteous, purposeful lives.

This proposal outlines a strategic co-production partnership where DLC and UGT join forces to reach Kenya’s youth at a scale neither can achieve alone. DLC brings the spiritual foundation, the stage, the sound, the counsellors, and the anointed youth movement. UGT brings the platform, the production team, the national TV broadcast, and the direct access to schools across the country.

Together, we do not just entertain students. We transform them.

# **THE CONFERENCE DAY — AT DLC SYOKIMAU**

After we have toured multiple schools, UGT will organise a one-day conference or talks day hosted at Destiny Life Church grounds in Syokimau. Here is what happens:

# **THE VISION**

*“Train up a child in the way he should go; even when he is old he will not depart from it.” — Proverbs 22:6*

Urban Gang Tour exists to go into Kenya’s schools and give young people a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams. Our motto — “From Potential to Purpose” — mirrors what Destiny Life Church’s Lit Gen already does every Sunday at 11:30 AM: taking young people and helping them discover their God-given gifts and live righteous, purposeful lives.

This proposal outlines a strategic co-production partnership where DLC and UGT join forces to reach Kenya’s youth at a scale neither can achieve alone. DLC brings the spiritual foundation, the stage, the sound, the counsellors, and the anointed youth movement. UGT brings the platform, the production team, the national TV broadcast, and the direct access to schools across the country.

Together, we do not just entertain students. We transform them.

# **THE STRATEGY: WHY THIS KIT IS DIFFERENT**

This kit is built on one insight that separates UGT from every other Kenyan youth event: UGT has TWO audiences on social media, and both must be served simultaneously.

## **Audience 1: The Students (Gen Z, 13–25)**

They discover brands through TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube. They scroll past anything that feels forced, corporate, or fake. They want raw, real, funny, and relatable content. Comedy and memes are their #1 content type. BTS gets 4x more engagement than polished branded posts. They value purpose-driven brands — 53% of Gen Z wants brands that support mental health. They spend 95 minutes per day on TikTok and 58% use Instagram daily. They are the ones who make UGT go viral.

## **Audience 2: The Sponsors, Partners ****&**** Corporates**

They are watching the same social media pages to evaluate whether UGT is worth partnering with. They look for professionalism, scale, production quality, and proof of audience. They need to see numbers, energy, and evidence that their brand will get value. They make decisions based on what they see in your content, not just what’s in your proposal. They are the ones who fund UGT.

## **The Solution: One Feed, Two Signals**

**Every piece of content must be entertaining enough for a 15-year-old AND credible enough for a Safaricom marketing director. This is exactly what Coachella does — their feed is simultaneously cool to attendees and impressive to billion-dollar brand partners. This is the standard UGT must hit.**

# **BRAND VOICE ****&**** PERSONALITY**

## **The UGT Persona**

UGT speaks like a big brother/sister who made it and came back to show you the way. Not a teacher. Not a brand. Not a government programme. A person you trust who happens to have a stage, a camera crew, and national TV.

| **Always** | Bold, energetic, warm, purposeful, raw, Gen Z-fluent, aspirational, community-first |
| --- | --- |
| **Sometimes** | Funny, meme-literate, Swahili-mixed, hype, emotional, vulnerable, celebratory |
| **Never** | Corporate, stiff, preachy, condescending, generic, overly polished, desperate, formal |
| **Motto Use** | Work “From Potential to Purpose” into captions naturally. Don’t force it every time. Let it breathe. When it appears, it should land |
| **Language** | English + Sheng/Swahili naturally mixed. Speak like Kenyan youth actually speak. No code-switching for corporates — the authenticity IS the professionalism |

# **CONTENT PILLARS**

Everything UGT posts falls into one of these 6 pillars. Each pillar serves both audiences:

| **1. THE STAGE** | Talent performances, hype sets, crowd reactions, awards moments, guest artist sets. This is the money content. High energy, cinematic angles, crowd going crazy. For students: “I want to be there.” For sponsors: “That’s reach.” |
| --- | --- |
| **2. THE JOURNEY** | Student stories, winner follow-ups, Urban Gang Club moments, before/after transformations, pod conversations. The purpose content. For students: “They see me.” For sponsors: “This is real impact.” |
| **3. THE VIBE** | BTS at 5 AM setup, crew briefings, canteen food reviews, MC prep, DJ sound checks, team banter, Eugine and Lucy being themselves. The authenticity content. Raw, unfiltered, real. For students: “They’re like us.” For sponsors: “This team is professional.” |
| **4. THE DROP** | Announcements: next school reveal, artist lineup, new partner, merch drop, ticket or registration info, calendar updates. The FOMO content. Designed to be shared, saved, and screenshotted. For students: “I need to be there.” For sponsors: “They’re growing fast.” |
| **5. THE SERMON** | Mental health moments, mentor quotes, pod wisdom, church moments, Tai’s environmental messages, teacher appreciation, motivational content. The purpose content. For students: “This matters.” For sponsors: “This is CSR gold.” |
| **6. THE FLEX** | Production shots, crowd aerial views, stage setups, team size, TV episode stills, PPP TV broadcast moments, partner logos in action. The credibility content. For sponsors: “This is a real operation.” For students: “This is the biggest thing at my school.” |

## **TikTok Content Strategy**

TikTok content is NOT the same as Instagram content. TikTok rewards raw, fast, surprising, funny, and trend-aware content. The first 1.5 seconds decide everything.

**Content Types That Work (Ranked by Priority)**

| **1. Crowd Reactions** | Film the CROWD, not the stage. Students losing their minds during a rap battle, dance-off, or hype set. 15–20 sec. This is the #1 TikTok format for event accounts. The crowd IS the content |
| --- | --- |
| **2. Talent Clips** | Best 15-sec moment from each performer. Use trending audio when possible. Caption with the performer’s name. Students want to find themselves |
| **3. Transformation / Before-After** | 5 AM empty field → 10 AM packed stage. Student at registration → student winning award. Setup → showtime. These get saved and shared |
| **4. POV / Day-in-the-Life** | “POV: You’re an MC at Kenya’s biggest school event” (Eugine or Lucy POV). “POV: You’re a student and Urban Gang Tour just pulled up to your school” |
| **5. Comedy / Memes** | Funny team moments. MC fails and recoveries. Students reacting to their own performances. Teacher dance competition highlights. Comedy is Gen Z’s #1 content type |
| **6. Behind-the-Scenes** | 5 AM call time. Sound check drama. Last-minute stage fixes. Food at the school canteen. Raw, messy, real — never polished |
| **7. Trending Audio Remixes** | Take trending TikTok sounds and layer UGT footage over them. This is how you hack the algorithm. Rania and Nyarangi should track trends weekly |
| **8. Mini-Documentaries** | 60–90 sec mini-docs about a student’s journey, a pod topic, or the UGT mission. These build the “purpose” side for sponsors |

## **TikTok Posting Frequency**

**Normal Weeks: **4–5 posts per week minimum

**Event Week: **2–3 posts PER DAY (morning hype, live clips, post-event)

**Event Day: **Minimum 5 TikToks throughout the day (setup, opening, talent, crowd, awards)

**Post-Event: **Drip content daily for 2 weeks after each school (you have 8–12 clips per event — don’t dump them all at once)

# **CAMPAIGN CONCEPTS**

These are ready-to-execute campaign ideas modelled on how Coachella, AFROPUNK, Revolve Festival, and top Gen Z brands drive social engagement.

## **Campaign 1: #NextStopLoading**

**What: **Pre-event hype campaign. 7 days before each school visit, start a countdown

**Day 7: **Black screen, just the text “Next stop loading...” with a location pin emoji. No school name yet

**Day 5: **Silhouette of the school entrance or aerial view. Still no name. Caption: “Are you ready?”

**Day 3: **REVEAL the school name. Full branded graphic with date and time. Caption: “[School Name], Urban Gang Tour is pulling up 🔥”

**Day 1: **5 AM setup timelapse or crew packing the van. “Tomorrow, we change lives.”

**Why: **This is the Coachella playbook — anticipation drives engagement. Students will screenshot, share in WhatsApp groups, and tag friends. Sponsors see the hype building

## **Campaign 2: #UGTMoments**

**What: **User-generated content campaign. After every event, encourage students to post their own photos/videos with #UGTMoments

**Incentive: **Repost the best student content on UGT’s main feed. Being reposted by UGT (with 400K+ daily reach) is the incentive

**Photobooth Tie-In: **Urban Gang Moments photobooth prints have #UGTMoments and @urbangangtour watermarked. Every student who takes a photo becomes a brand ambassador

**Why: **Coachella’s #Coachella hashtag generates millions of posts because attendees WANT to be part of the narrative. UGT should own this in the Kenyan high school space

## **Campaign 3: #PotentialToPurpose**

**What: **Long-running impact series. Follow one student per school from their first UGT event through the follow-up programme

**Format: **60–90 sec mini-documentary style Reels/TikToks. Filmed during pods, competition, and then at the church conference day

**Why: **This is the content that sponsors share in their boardrooms. It proves ROI. It proves impact. And for students, it’s aspirational — they see someone like them being celebrated nationally

## **Campaign 4: #TeacherOfTheYear**

**What: **After the teacher dance competition, post the winning teacher’s clip with #TeacherOfTheYear

**Why: **Teachers share this content like wildfire. It gets to school WhatsApp groups, parent groups, and education media. It positions UGT as pro-education, not just entertainment. Sponsors in the education sector (publishers, stationery, edtech) will notice

## **Campaign 5: #UGTDanceChallenge**

**What: **Create a signature UGT dance move (choreographed by Nyarangi and the Experience Hub dancers). Teach it at every school. Film students doing it

**TikTok: **Post it with a trending sound. Challenge students to post their version. Best submissions get reposted

**Why: **This is how you build a movement. A dance challenge is shareable, repeatable, and puts UGT in the TikTok algorithm. When a challenge goes viral, it reaches millions of people who’ve never heard of UGT

# **EVENT-DAY SOCIAL MEDIA PLAYBOOK**

# **VISUAL RULES FOR SOCIAL**

| **Primary Look** | Dark backgrounds (#1A1A1A). White text. Magenta + orange accents. Bold. High contrast. NOT pastel. NOT corporate |
| --- | --- |
| **Fonts on Graphics** | Bold sans-serif only. All caps for headlines. Mixed case for body. Never script or decorative fonts |
| **Photo Treatment** | High contrast. Slightly desaturated with magenta/orange colour grade. Think concert photography, not school newsletter |
| **Video Treatment** | Fast cuts (1–2 sec per cut for TikTok). Dramatic transitions. Bass-heavy audio. Crowd reactions as B-roll |
| **Text on Video** | Bold white text with black outline or magenta highlight. Bottom-third placement. 3–5 words max per frame |
| **Carousel Style** | Dark background. One photo per slide. Text overlay in magenta/orange. Clean, magazine-editorial feel |
| **Stories Style** | Raw and real. Minimal text. Let the moment speak. Use Instagram’s native tools sparingly — stickers and polls for engagement only |
| **Profile Consistency** | Same profile picture across ALL platforms. Same username. Same bio structure. No exceptions |
| **Logo Use** | Logo appears on all branded graphics (posters, announcements, recap carousels). Never stretched, never on busy backgrounds |

# **CONTENT TEAM RESPONSIBILITIES**

| **Rania Martin** | PRIMARY social content creator. Shoots all Reels and TikToks during events. 8–12 clips per school. Edits same-day for immediate posting. Manages Instagram Stories on event days. Assisted by Nyarangi |
| --- | --- |
| **Nyarangi** | SECONDARY content support. Films BTS. Captures crowd reactions. Assists Rania with reels. Creates dance challenge content |
| **Eugine Micah** | Social media co-manager. Writes captions, strategy, and announcements. Creates posters/graphics. Shoots TV segments that double as social content. Approves all posts |
| **Lucy Ogunde** | Social media co-manager. Community engagement — responds to comments and DMs. Shoots TV segments that double as social content |
| **George Morgan** | Photography lead. Event photos for Instagram carousels and Facebook albums. Winner interview footage for YouTube/IGTV |

# **METRICS THAT MATTER**

Track these monthly. They tell you if the strategy is working:

| **Reel Views (IG)** | Target: 10K+ average per Reel within 3 months |
| --- | --- |
| **TikTok Views** | Target: 50K+ average per TikTok within 3 months (TikTok has wider organic reach) |
| **Saves ****&**** Shares** | More important than likes. Saves = sponsors see value. Shares = students spread the word |
| **Follower Growth** | Target: +500/month on IG, +1000/month on TikTok in first 6 months |
| **UGC Volume** | #UGTMoments posts from students after each event. Target: 50+ per school |
| **DM Enquiries** | Partnership/sponsorship DMs are the leading indicator of revenue. Track weekly |
| **Comment Quality** | Are students tagging friends? Are sponsors asking “How do we partner?”? Read the comments |

# **SECTION 3: ABOUT SECTION CONTENT**

## **3.1 Mission**

To discover, nurture, and celebrate the talent and potential of every young person in Kenya — giving them a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams. From Potential to Purpose.

## **3.2 Vision**

To become Kenya’s largest and most impactful youth talent and mentorship tour, reaching every school, university, college, and tertiary institution countrywide — then expanding into mega concerts, colour festivals, church missions, and a nationwide youth movement.

## **3.3 Our Platform**

Urban News on PPP TV Kenya — hosted by Eugine Micah & Lucy Ogunde. Up to 8 million viewers nationally. Every school visit becomes a full TV episode (25–30 minutes) plus 8–12 social media clips distributed across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Facebook, and X.

## **3.4 Quote**

**Quote: **“We are not in the business of just entertaining students — we are here to educate, inform, nurture, and give young people a reason to make it.”

**Attribution: **— Eugine Micah, Creative Director

## **3.5 What Makes UGT Different**

**Not a one-off: **We visit schools term after term. We establish Urban Gang Clubs. We return

**Not just entertainment: **Mentorship pods, tree planting, counselling, teacher appreciation, career exposure

**National TV: **Every event becomes a PPP TV episode broadcast to millions

**Compound effect: **Urban Gang Clubs keep the brand and mission alive in schools year-round

**30+ professionals: **Full production team with hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, security, church team

## **4.2 The 5 Urban Pod Topics**

Choir, Gospel, Poetry, Drama, Rap, Dance, Comedy, Traditional Art, Mathematics & Logic, and more depending on the school. Each category runs 20–25 minutes. After every 2–3 categories there is a hype set.

Four runway categories: Cultural/Traditional Wear, Creative Wear, Cosplay, Professional Wear. Models have 10 minutes to change between categories. MC Paps (Flip the Script) oversees coordination.

## **9.4 Key Selling Points for Sponsors**

# **2. LOGO**

## **Primary Logo**

The UGT logo is a 3D stylised text mark: “URBAN” (white/silver) on top, “GANG” (orange) on bottom, with “TOUR” on a magenta ribbon badge in the centre. The source file is 1024×1024px.

## **Logo Variations (Included in Kit)**

| **logo_dark_bg_1200.png** | Standard logo on dark background (#1A1A1A). Primary usage |
| --- | --- |
| **logo_white_bg_1200.png** | Logo on white background. For formal documents and light contexts |
| **logo_magenta_bg_1200.png** | Logo on magenta background. For high-energy promotional materials |
| **logo_horizontal_2400x600.png** | Horizontal lockup with logo + text + motto. For headers and banners |
| **favicon_512.png** | App icon / favicon (512px). For website and app |
| **favicon_192.png** | Smaller app icon (192px). For Android/web manifest |
| **favicon_32.png** | Browser tab favicon (32px) |

## **Logo Usage Rules**

**DO: **Use the logo on dark backgrounds whenever possible. The logo was designed for dark contexts

**DO: **Maintain the logo’s original proportions. Never stretch or compress it

**DO: **Keep clear space around the logo equal to at least 20% of the logo’s width on all sides

**DON’T: **Never recolour the logo. The magenta/orange/white colours are fixed

**DON’T: **Never place the logo on busy or patterned backgrounds without a solid backing

**DON’T: **Never use the logo at sizes smaller than 60px wide for print or 40px for digital

**DON’T: **Never add effects like drop shadows, glows, or outlines to the logo

# **3. COLOUR PALETTE**

The UGT colour system is built on two primary brand colours (magenta and orange) plus a dark foundation and supporting accents.

## **Primary Colours**

| **Primary Magenta** | #C7238E │ RGB(199, 35, 142) │ Used for headings, CTAs, highlights, borders, links, and the primary brand accent |
| --- | --- |
| **Secondary Orange** | #F5A623 │ RGB(245, 166, 35) │ Used for subheadings, secondary accents, mottos, dividers, and warm emphasis |

## **Foundation Colours**

| **Dark / Body Text** | #1A1A1A │ RGB(26, 26, 26) │ Primary background colour. Body text on light backgrounds |
| --- | --- |
| **White** | #FFFFFF │ RGB(255, 255, 255) │ Text on dark backgrounds. Clean space |
| **Light Pink** | #FDF0F7 │ RGB(253, 240, 247) │ Alternating table rows. Soft highlight areas. Subtle brand warmth |

## **Accent Colours**

| **Church Purple** | #7B1FA2 │ RGB(123, 31, 162) │ Destiny Life Church sessions, gospel segments, spiritual content |
| --- | --- |
| **Green Movement** | #2E7D32 │ RGB(46, 125, 50) │ Tree planting, environmental segments, Tai’s Green Movement content |

## **Colour Usage Rules**

**Dominance: **Magenta is always the dominant brand colour. Orange is the secondary. Never reverse this hierarchy

**Backgrounds: **Default to dark (#1A1A1A) backgrounds. The brand is designed dark-first

**Text: **White text on dark backgrounds. Dark text on light backgrounds. Magenta or orange for accents only

**Maximum: **Never use more than 3 colours in a single graphic (e.g., magenta + orange + white, or magenta + dark + white)

# **4. TYPOGRAPHY**

## **Font Stack**

| **Primary Font** | Arial (all documents, presentations, digital assets, and printed materials) |
| --- | --- |
| **Website Font** | Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif |
| **Code / Monospace** | Courier New (used in email signatures and technical content) |

## **Type Scale**

| **Hero / Display** | 52–80pt Bold │ Used for cover page titles and website hero text |
| --- | --- |
| **Heading 1** | 32–42pt Bold │ Section headings in documents and pages |
| **Heading 2** | 24–28pt Bold │ Subsection headings |
| **Body Text** | 20–22pt Regular │ Standard paragraph text |
| **Small Text** | 14–18pt Regular │ Captions, footers, metadata |
| **Micro Text** | 11–14pt Regular │ Legal text, version numbers, fine print |

## **Typography Rules**

**Headings: **Always bold. Always in brand colours (magenta for H1, orange for H2)

**Body: **Regular weight. Dark colour on light backgrounds, white on dark backgrounds

**Never: **Use decorative, serif, or script fonts. The brand is clean and modern

**Emphasis: **Use bold for emphasis. Use magenta colour for key terms. Never use underline for emphasis

# **5. VISUAL ELEMENTS**

## **Colour Bars**

Horizontal colour bars are used as section dividers throughout all UGT materials. A magenta bar sits above content sections, and an orange bar sits below. Bars are 6–12pt thick depending on context.

## **Tables**

All tables use magenta header rows with white text, and alternating light pink (#FDF0F7) and white data rows. Borders are light grey (#E0E0E0). This is consistent across all documents.

## **Cards / Containers**

On dark backgrounds, content cards use a subtle semi-transparent background (rgba white at 3–5% opacity) with a thin border (white at 8% opacity) and 12px border radius. Cards may have a coloured left border (3–4px) in magenta, orange, purple, or green depending on the content category.

## **Stage Branding**

**Backdrop: **UGT logo centred on dark background. Sponsor logos arranged below or beside

**Teardrops: **Magenta and orange branded teardrop flags flanking the stage

**Stage Shape: **T-shaped, 36–42 boards. Always maximise branding coverage

# **WHAT IF YOUR BRAND COULD DO THIS?**

Imagine thousands of students chanting your brand name from a stage. Imagine your logo on a certificate that a 16-year-old frames and hangs on their wall. Imagine your product in the hands of every winner, captured on camera and broadcast to 8 million viewers on national television. Imagine a club in every school in Kenya carrying your brand alongside ours, year after year.

That’s not a dream. That’s what Urban Gang Tour delivers — event after event, school after school, nationwide.

# **WHO YOUR BRAND REACHES**

| **Primary Audience** | High school students aged 13–18 (hundreds to thousands per event) |
| --- | --- |
| **Secondary Audience** | Teachers, school staff, parents, and community members at events |
| **TV Audience** | Up to 8 million PPP TV viewers nationally |
| **Digital Audience** | 400K– millions daily via Urban News + social media content engine |
| **Content Per Event** | 1 full TV episode (25–30 min) + 8–12 social clips + photography |
| **Post-Event Reach** | 2 winners on PPP TV per school + Urban Gang Clubs in every school |
| **Growth Trajectory** | 3 schools this term → expanding to universities, concerts, festivals countrywide |

# **WHY UGT, NOT ANOTHER EVENT**

**You’re In the Room: **Not buying eyeballs on a screen. You’re on the stage, in the tent, in the student’s hands. Direct, physical, memorable

**National TV: **Every event becomes a PPP TV episode. Your brand is on television, not just at the event

**Content Machine: **8–12 clips per event, distributed across every platform Gen Z actually uses

**Trusted Voices: **Our hosts, DJs, and performers are trusted by the youth community. Association with UGT is association with credibility

**Year-Round Presence: **Urban Gang Clubs keep your brand visible in schools between visits. The church conference day extends your reach further

**Compound Effect: **This is long-term. Every school, every term, your brand compounds. You’re not renting attention — you’re building equity

# **OUR STORY**

Every young person in Kenya has potential. The question is: who will help them find their purpose?

Urban Gang Tour (UGT) was born from that question. We are a high school talent search, mentorship, awards, and concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan institutions — not to perform at students, but to perform with them. We discover talent, nurture dreams, build confidence, and leave behind a legacy that lasts long after we leave the school gates.

We are not in the business of just entertaining. We are here to educate, inform, nurture, and give young people a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams.

# **WHAT HAPPENS AT A UGT EVENT**

## **The Full-Day Experience (5:00 AM – 5:30 PM)**

**Urban Pods (8:10 AM): **Five simultaneous 30-minute mentorship breakouts led by industry professionals: Discover Your Talent, Own Your Money, Go Digital, Lead From Where You Are, and Mind Your Mind

**Keynote Address (9:00 AM): **A main speaker delivers an inspiring, relatable talk in raw Gen Z language, aligned with the day’s theme

**Tree Planting (9:20 AM): **In partnership with the Green Movement (led by Tai), students and guests plant trees with scouts taking long-term care

**Church Opening (10:00 AM): **Destiny Life Church’s Tribez and Lit Gen open the main event with gospel energy and crowd activation

**Talent Competitions: **Choir, Gospel, Poetry, Drama, Rap, Dance, Comedy, Maths & Logic, and more. Each category 20–25 minutes with hype sets in between

**Modelling Showcase: **Four runway categories — Cultural/Traditional, Creative, Cosplay, and Professional Wear

**Two Guest Musicians: **A curtain-raising artist and a headline act, each with their own full set

**Teacher Appreciation: **Coordination teachers recognised and awarded. Fun teacher dance competition voted by students

**TV Programme Production: **8 broadcast segments shot throughout the day for the Urban News episode on PPP TV

**Awards Ceremony: **Winners receive certificates, hampers, crowns, sashes, and trophies. Category winners featured on PPP TV

# **OUR PARTNERS**

## **Strategic Co-Production Partner: Destiny Life Church, Syokimau**

Destiny Life Church is embedded into every school visit and provides stage & sound setup, LED PAR can lighting, setup crew manpower, a main keynote speaker, a fully staffed counselling and guidance tent, their branded Tribez youth teams (one team per school), Lit Gen branding and DJ, transportation, and at times a headline gospel musician. Beyond UGT, we collaborate on missions to different Kenyan schools, mega events, and deeper church-community programmes. UGT leadership are full members of the church.

## **Broadcast Partner: PPP TV Kenya**

PPP TV is UGT’s official national broadcaster, averaging viewership of up to 8 million people. Through Urban News — hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde — UGT reaches a daily variable audience of 400,000 to millions of young Kenyans. PPP TV provides full broadcast coverage, marketing slots, mentions, and advertising.

## **Hype ****&**** Entertainment Partner: The Experience Hub**

Led by Hype Ola with DJ Carian, 5 dancers (including Nyarangi and Jeff), and a gospel hypeman (Gig Real). On event day, the Experience Hub deploys 30+ people for three high-energy hype sets and continuous crowd activation throughout the programme.

## **Environmental Partner: The Green Movement**

Led by Tai, the Green Movement facilitates tree planting sessions at every school visit, providing seedlings and guidance. School scouts or Kenya Girl Guides take long-term stewardship of the planted trees.

## **Events Partner: Faly Mark**

CEO of our events company partner. Provides stage and sound equipment with 50/50 transport and logistics cost-sharing.

# **THE CHALLENGE EVERY BRAND FACES**

Kenya has 10,000+ secondary schools, hundreds of universities and colleges, and millions of young people making daily decisions about the brands they trust, the products they buy, and the companies they want to work for. Traditional advertising reaches them on screens they scroll past. UGT puts your brand in the room with them — on a stage they’re cheering for, in a tent they’re visiting, on a certificate they’re holding, and in content they’re sharing.

This is not a one-off event. This is a long-term, countrywide movement that extends beyond schools into universities, concerts, festivals, church missions, and community events. When you partner with UGT, your brand grows with us.

# **HOW TO PARTNER WITH US**

We don’t have a rigid one-size-fits-all package. We believe the best partnerships are co-created. Here are the ways your brand can join the movement:

## **1. Segment Sponsorship**

Own a specific segment of every event. Your brand becomes synonymous with that category across every school, every city, every event nationwide. Options include any talent category (Rap, Dance, Poetry, Gospel, Drama, Choir, Comedy, Maths & Logic), the Modelling Showcase, Urban Pods, Teacher Appreciation, Tree Planting, or any custom segment.

**Your Brand Gets: **Stage mentions by hosts, branded category intro, your logo on category certificates and awards, TV and social media credits, logo on backdrop, on-camera brand moment during your segment

## **2. Awards ****&**** Prize Sponsorship**

Supply the gift hampers, certificates, trophies, sashes, crowns, or unique prizes. Your brand is physically in the hands of every winner and visible in every award photo and video broadcast on PPP TV.

**Your Brand Gets: **Logo on certificates, branded gift hampers with your products, on-stage mention, TV and social coverage of winners holding your branded prizes

## **3. Booth ****&**** Activation**

Set up a branded booth or activation zone. Sample products, run contests, offer information, or engage directly with thousands of students face-to-face.

**Your Brand Gets: **Dedicated tent/booth space, branded signage, direct student engagement, on-camera mention, social media feature

## **4. Branding ****&**** Visibility**

Your logo across UGT’s physical and digital assets: stage backdrop, teardrops, banners, printed programmes, certificates, social media posts, TV broadcast credits, and Urban News mentions.

## **5. Vendor Partnership**

Sell your products directly to students and event attendees. Snacks, beverages, stationery, accessories, clothing — UGT events give you a captive audience. Destiny Life Church also brings vendors, creating a vibrant vendor ecosystem.

## **6. PR Package**

We build custom PR campaigns for our partners. TV mentions and adverts on PPP TV, dedicated social media posts, branded content, event reels featuring your brand, and a structured campaign that pushes your visibility far beyond the event.

## **7. Unique Element Creation**

Create something entirely new and own it: a branded award category, a student challenge, a digital literacy station, a career expo booth, a VIP experience, or anything creative that aligns with UGT’s mission. We co-design it with you.

## **8. Musician / Artist Facilitation**

Sponsor one of our two guest musicians. Your brand introduces the headline act and is associated with the biggest musical moment of the day.

## **9. University / College Partnership**

Provide career guidance, scholarship info, campus tours, or student recruitment at UGT events. Direct access to high school students at the point of career decision-making.

## **Urban News TV Programme Segments (Shot During Event)**

Eugine and Lucy divide duties: one hosts on stage while the other shoots TV segments with Camera 3 (Lallez). They rotate throughout the day. The following segments are produced for the Urban News episode on PPP TV:

**1. Show Intro: **Hosts introduce the school, the event, and the day’s theme. Shot at school entrance or main stage

**2. Student Interview #1: **A student shares their talent/dream. Shot backstage or in the crowd

**3. Canteen / Food Experience: **Hosts explore the school canteen and Urban Gang Snacks. Fun, relatable content

**4. Club Visit #1: **Hosts visit a school club (e.g., journalism, debate). Teach hosting on camera

**5. Club Visit #2: **Second club visit. Content for clubs rivalry segment

**6. Principal / Deputy Interview: **Formal interview with school leadership. Shot in office or by the stage

**7. Student Interview #2: **A second student shares their story. Different from the first

**8. Show Outro: **Hosts wrap up the episode. Highlights, winners, and tease for next school

**Stage: **T-shaped, 36–42 boards (6 front, 6 middle, 6 back, extendable). Maximum branding on backdrop

**Full TV Episode: **25–30 min edit for PPP TV Kenya (Urban News) — Bico, George, Lallez

**Teaser Clip: **Next school visit promo — Rania + Lallez

# **9. MC MANDATE — EUGINE MICAH ****&**** LUCY OGUNDE**

**Rule 1: **MCs are on stage from 10:00 AM onwards. Before 10 AM, they divide duties between pods, TV filming, and student engagement

**Rule 2: **When one MC is filming TV segments, the other holds the stage. MC Paps covers if both are briefly off stage

**Rule 3: **No guest, performer, or official leaves the stage without being introduced by Eugine or Lucy first

**Rule 4: **MCs bridge every segment — no dead air. DJ Xavi provides music cover if MCs need a moment

**Rule 5: **MCs introduce every category, performer, official, and award recipient

**Rule 6: **MCs manage crowd energy — call-and-response, hype moments, crowd direction

**Rule 7: **MCs are the face of Urban News. Everything they do is content

**Rule 8: **If technical issues arise, MCs hold the crowd. Pauline handles sound. MCs never break character

**Rule 9: **The stage presence of Eugine and Lucy is the brand. No one overshadows the hosts

# **10. FOLLOW-UP STRATEGY**

The event day is not the end. UGT’s impact continues through a structured follow-up programme:

**Week 1 — TV Feature: **2 category winners from the school are hosted on PPP TV for a live interview on Urban News. Broadcast to 400K– millions of viewers. Their moment to shine nationally

**Week 2 — Urban Gang Club: **UGT establishes an Urban Gang Club in the school with elected student leadership. The club becomes UGT’s permanent presence in that institution — a pipeline for future talent, events, and engagement

**Week 3–4 — Church Conference Day: **A dedicated one-day conference or talks day hosted at Destiny Life Church grounds. Winners and the most engaging pod participants from ALL toured schools are invited. Activities include mentorship sessions, workshops, and talks facilitated by the church. Best performers are gifted. This is a milestone celebration of their journey

**Ongoing: **UGT stays connected with Urban Gang Clubs across all schools. Progress tracking. Repeat visits. Alumni network. The vision is long-term and countrywide

# **THE OPPORTUNITY**

Kenya has over 10,000 secondary schools and millions of young people making daily decisions about the brands they trust, the products they buy, and the companies they want to work for. Urban Gang Tour gives you direct, face-to-face access to this audience — not through a banner ad they scroll past, but through a live, high-energy experience they will remember and talk about.

This is a long-term, countrywide partnership — not a one-off activation. UGT visits schools, universities, colleges, and tertiary institutions across Kenya, and is expanding into mega concerts, colour festivals, community events, and church missions. Your brand rides with us everywhere we go.

# **WHAT MAKES UGT DIFFERENT**

**Direct Youth Access: **You are not buying eyeballs on a screen. You are in the room — on the stage, in the tent, in the student’s hands. Thousands of students per event, dozens of events per year, countrywide.

**National TV Coverage: **Every event is produced for broadcast on PPP TV Kenya (up to 8 million viewers). Your brand appears on television, not just at the event.

**Social Media Engine: **8–12 short-form clips per event across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts. Your brand is in the content that Gen Z actually watches and shares.

**Authentic Connection: **UGT is built by young people for young people. Our hosts, DJs, and performers are trusted voices in the youth community. Association with UGT is association with credibility.

**Long-Term Equity: **We form Urban Gang Clubs in schools, follow up with students, and return for repeat visits. Your brand’s presence compounds over time.

# **WHO WE ARE**

Urban Gang Tour (UGT) is a Kenyan high school talent search, inspiration, mentorship, and awards programme that goes directly into institutions to discover, nurture, and celebrate young talent. Built on the motto “From Potential to Purpose,” UGT combines live performances, competitions, mentorship pods, arts and crafts displays, modelling showcases, and award concerts into a single high-energy experience that leaves a lasting impact on every student it reaches.

We are not in the business of just entertaining students — we are here to educate, inform, nurture, and give young people a reason to believe, a reason to hope, and a reason to chase their dreams.

**Registered Name: **Urban Gang Merch

**Registration Number: **BN-6ASYVPW5

**Registration Date: **26th August 2025 (eBusiness Portal, Kenya)

**Headquarters: **Nairobi, Kenya

# **OUR VISION**

To become Kenya’s largest and most impactful youth talent and mentorship tour, reaching every high school, university, college, and tertiary institution in the country — and eventually hosting mega concerts, colour festivals, and nationwide events that unite young people across all communities, including partnerships with churches and faith-based organisations.

# **WHAT WE DO**

## **The Urban Gang Tour Experience**

Each UGT school visit is a full-day immersive programme that includes:

**Urban Pods (Morning Sessions): **Intimate breakout sessions led by industry professionals across five core topics — Discover Your Talent, Own Your Money, Go Digital, Lead From Where You Are, and Mind Your Mind. Each pod runs for 30 minutes and gives students direct access to mentors in a relaxed, Gen Z-friendly environment.

**Keynote Address: **A main speaker delivers an inspiring, relatable talk aligned with the day’s theme, using raw, authentic language that connects with young people.

**Tree Planting: **In partnership with Kenya Wildlife Services (KWS), students and guests plant trees on school grounds, with the school’s scouts or Kenya Girl Guides taking long-term stewardship.

**Talent Competitions: **Categories include Rap, Singing, Dance, Poetry, Drama, Comedy, Choir, Traditional Art, Mathematics & Logic, and more depending on the school.

**Modelling Showcase: **Four runway categories — Cultural/Traditional Wear, Creative Wear, Cosplay, and Professional Wear — coordinated by our modelling partner.

**Hype Sessions: **High-energy crowd activation sets by The Experience Hub featuring DJs, hypemen, and dancers throughout the day.

**Church Session: **Our strategic partner Destiny Life Church provides an early-morning gospel hype session, a main speaker, a counselling and guidance tent, and their youth movement (Tribez) at every school visit.

**Teacher Appreciation: **Coordination teachers are recognised and awarded for their role in making the programme run efficiently, culminating in a fun teacher dance competition.

**Awards Ceremony: **Winners receive certificates and gift hampers. The overall modelling winner is crowned. Category winners earn a feature on Urban News (PPP TV Kenya).

# **OUR TEAM**

Every person on the UGT team has a defined role and is a partner invested in the vision.

# **OUR PARTNERS**

## **Strategic Co-Production Partner: Destiny Life Church, Syokimau**

Destiny Life Church is UGT’s anchor co-production partner. They are embedded into every school visit and provide:

**• Stage ****&**** Sound: **Full stage setup, PA system, and LED PAR can stage lighting

**• Setup Crew: **Manpower to assemble and dismantle stage, sound, and lighting rigs

**• Main Speaker: **A keynote speaker for the main address at every school visit

**• Counselling Tent: **Fully staffed guidance and counselling tent with trained counsellors

**• Tribez Youth Teams: **Their branded youth movement deploys a dedicated team to each school

**• Lit Gen Branding: **Their youth brand (Lit Gen) is visibly branded at every event

**• Transportation: **Transport for Tribez members and equipment

**• Musicians: **At times they facilitate a headline gospel musician as the key highlight

**• Vendors: **The church is encouraged to bring in vendors to each event

Beyond UGT, we are planning missions to different Kenyan schools, mega events, and deeper church-community collaborations together. UGT leadership are full members of Destiny Life Church.

## **Broadcast Partner: PPP TV Kenya**

PPP TV is UGT’s official national broadcaster, averaging viewership of up to 8 million people. Through the flagship show Urban News — hosted by Eugine Micah and Lucy Ogunde — UGT reaches a daily variable audience of 400,000 to millions of young Kenyan viewers. PPP TV provides full broadcast coverage, marketing slots, mentions, and advertising for UGT across its platforms.

## **Hype ****&**** Entertainment Partner: The Experience Hub**

Led by Hype Ola and DJ Carian, The Experience Hub provides three high-energy hype sets across each event day. Their team includes dancers (Nyarangi, Jeff, and others), a gospel hypeman (Gig Real), and additional hype specialists. They are responsible for crowd activation, energy management, and keeping the atmosphere electric throughout every school visit.

## **Environmental Partner: Kenya Wildlife Services (KWS)**

KWS facilitates the tree planting sessions at every school visit, providing seedlings and guidance. Schools’ scouts or Kenya Girl Guides take long-term care of the planted trees, creating a lasting environmental legacy at every institution UGT visits.

# **OUR OWNED BRANDS**

**Urban Gang Snacks: **Our own affordable snack shop set up at every event, selling cheap and popular snacks to students.

**Urban Gang Moments: **Our instant-print photobooth where students take photos and receive printed copies at KES 20 per picture.

**Urban Gang Merch: **Our official merchandise line sold at every event.

# **OUR REACH**

UGT leverages three powerful channels to reach young Kenyans:

**1. Urban News (PPP TV): **One of the biggest youth-focused shows in Kenya with daily viewership of 400K to millions

**2. PPP TV National Broadcast: **National broadcaster averaging up to 8 million viewers

**3. Social Media: **Dedicated content across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and more — with reels, highlights, and behind-the-scenes content from every school visit

# **WHY PARTNER WITH URBAN GANG TOUR?**

Urban Gang Tour is not a one-off event — it is a countrywide movement that goes into schools, universities, colleges, and tertiary institutions across Kenya, and is expanding into mega concerts, colour festivals, church missions, and community events. When you partner with UGT, you are joining a long-term platform with deep, direct access to the most coveted demographic in Kenya: young people aged 13–25.

# **PARTNERSHIP OPPORTUNITIES**

We welcome partners who are intentional, aligned with our vision, and interested in long-term collaboration. Here are the ways you can come on board:

## **1. Segment / Category Sponsorship**

Own a specific segment of the event day. Your brand becomes synonymous with that category across every school, every city, every event. Options include: Rap Category Sponsor, Dance Category Sponsor, Poetry Category Sponsor, Modelling Showcase Sponsor, Choir Sponsor, Drama Sponsor, Gospel Session Sponsor, Mathematics & Logic Competition Sponsor, Urban Pods Sponsor (one or more of the five pod topics), Teacher Appreciation Sponsor, Tree Planting Session Sponsor, and any other segment.

**What You Get: **Stage mentions, branded category intro graphics, branded certificates and awards for that category, TV and social media credits, logo on backdrop and teardrops, on-camera brand moment during your segment.

## **2. Awards ****&**** Prize Sponsorship**

Sponsor the gift hampers, certificates, trophies, sashes, crowns, or unique prizes for winners. Your brand is physically in the hands of every winner and visible in every award photo and video clip.

**What You Get: **Logo on certificates, branded gift hampers with your products, on-stage brand mention during awards, TV and social media coverage of winners holding your branded prizes.

## **3. Booth ****&**** Activation Sponsorship**

Set up a branded booth or activation zone at the event venue. Engage directly with students, sample products, run contests, or offer information sessions. This is prime face-to-face time with thousands of young consumers.

**What You Get: **Dedicated tent/booth space, branded signage, student foot traffic, on-camera mention, social media feature, and the ability to sell, sample, or interact directly with students.

## **4. Branding ****&**** Visibility Partnership**

Your logo and branding across UGT’s physical and digital assets. This includes the stage backdrop, teardrops, event banners, printed programmes, certificates, social media posts, TV broadcast credits on PPP TV, and Urban News mentions.

**What You Get: **Maximum brand visibility at every event, across every platform, in every piece of content UGT produces.

## **5. Vendor Partnership**

Bring your products to sell directly to students and event attendees. Whether you sell snacks, beverages, stationery, accessories, or any other product, UGT events give you a captive audience of thousands. Destiny Life Church is also encouraged to bring vendors, so you will be part of a vibrant vendor ecosystem.

**What You Get: **Dedicated vending space, foot traffic, on-camera mentions, and the opportunity to build brand loyalty with young consumers.

## **6. PR Package Partnership**

UGT’s campaigns are oriented at helping our partners gain visibility and awareness. We create custom PR packages tailored to your brand, leveraging Urban News on PPP TV, our social media platforms, and live event coverage to push your brand far beyond the event itself.

**What You Get: **TV mentions and adverts on PPP TV, dedicated social media posts, branded content, event reels featuring your brand, and a structured PR campaign built around your partnership.

## **7. Unique Element Sponsorship**

Create something entirely new and own it. Ideas include: a branded stage element, a special award category named after your brand, a VIP experience, a branded challenge or game for students, a digital literacy station, a career expo booth, or anything creative that aligns with UGT’s mission.

**What You Get: **A bespoke activation designed around your brand, full creative collaboration with the UGT team, and maximum differentiation from other partners.

## **8. Musician / Artist Facilitation**

Sponsor a performing musician or artist for the event. Your brand introduces the headline act and is associated with the biggest moment of the day.

**What You Get: **On-stage brand introduction of the artist, branded stage moment, TV and social media credits, and association with the most memorable performance of the day.

## **9. University / College / Tertiary Institution Partnership**

We specifically seek private and public universities, colleges, and tertiary institutions to come on board as education partners. Provide career guidance, scholarship information, campus tours, or student recruitment activations at UGT events.

**What You Get: **Direct access to high school students at the point of career decision-making, branded booth, speaking slot in a relevant Urban Pod, TV and social media coverage, and long-term pipeline to future students.

# **THIS IS A LONG-TERM PARTNERSHIP**

UGT is building something that will last for years. We are not looking for one-time sponsors — we are looking for partners who want to grow with us. This programme is countrywide and extends beyond schools into universities, concerts, festivals, church missions, community events, and more.

When you come on board, you are joining a movement. We will work with you to find the best possible ways to push your brand further than just the show — on TV, on social media, and everywhere else. Your partnership with UGT will evolve as we grow, and together we will leave an impact on every young person we reach.

| **The Experience Hub** | Hype sessions, DJs, dancers, crowd activation across all events |

# **NEXT STEPS**

We want to hear from you. Whether you want to sponsor a segment, set up a booth, provide products, or create something entirely new with us, we are ready to build a partnership that works for both of us.

**Step 1: **Reach out to us expressing your interest and what you would like to do

This kit is built differently from the first one. Here's what changed and why:

The Strategy Document (387 paragraphs) is built on actual research from Coachella 2026, AFROPUNK, Revolve Festival, eMarketer Gen Z data, Attest's 2026 media consumption study, Sprout Social's 2025 Index, and WGSN consumer trends. The core insight driving everything: UGT has two audiences on social media — students who make you viral and sponsors who fund you — and every post must serve both simultaneously. This is exactly what Coachella does.

What's inside the document:

6 Content Pillars — The Stage (performance clips), The Journey (student stories), The Vibe (BTS), The Drop (announcements/FOMO), The Sermon (purpose/mental health), The Flex (production/credibility). Each pillar is designed to hook students AND impress sponsors at the same time.

Hashtag Architecture — Not a random list. A layered system modelled on Coachella's approach: master tags (#UrbanGangTour), campaign tags (#NextStopLoading, #UGTLive, #UGTat[School]), pillar tags (#UGTTalent, #UGTBTS), and discovery tags for each platform.

5 Campaign Concepts — #NextStopLoading (7-day countdown before each school, Coachella-style anticipation), #UGTMoments (student UGC tied to the photobooth), #PotentialToPurpose (mini-doc series for sponsor boardrooms), #TeacherOfTheYear (education sector bait), #UGTDanceChallenge (TikTok algorithm hack).

Platform strategies written specifically for how each platform works: TikTok for virality (crowd reactions > stage shots, 8 content types ranked by priority), Instagram for brand building (weekly calendar, highlight structure, carousel style), Facebook as the credibility press room, WhatsApp as the direct relationship tool.

Event-Day Social Playbook — Minute-by-minute deployment from 5 AM to evening, telling Rania and Nyarangi exactly what to post and when.

6 Visual Templates — Event announcement post, #NextStopLoading teaser story, school reveal story, TikTok video thumbnail, carousel slide, and quote/motto graphic. All in UGT's dark-first magenta/orange visual system.

Subject:
A Partnership to Reach Kenya's Youth — Urban Gang Tour × Destiny Life Church
Dear Bishop Phillips Katutu,

Grace and peace to you and the entire Destiny Life Church family.

My name is Eugine Micah. I am the Creative Director and Lead Host of Urban Gang Tour (UGT), and I am also a member of Destiny Life Church. I am writing to you not just as someone presenting an idea, but as someone who is part of this house and who deeply believes that what God is doing through Destiny Life Church and what He is calling us to do through Urban Gang Tour are meant to work together.

I want to share a vision with you — and then ask for your partnership in making it real.

---

WHAT IS URBAN GANG TOUR?

Urban Gang Tour is Kenya's first high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into schools across the country. Our motto is "From Potential to Purpose" — and that is not just a tagline. It is a conviction.

We go into schools with a team of 30+ professionals — hosts, DJs, dancers, camera crew, mentors, and speakers — and we spend a full day with students. We run mentorship sessions (we call them Urban Pods) on topics like mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, leadership, and discovering talent. We run talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, choir, comedy, and modelling. We award the best performers with certificates and gifts. And every event is filmed and broadcast nationally on PPP TV Kenya through our show Urban News, which reaches up to 8 million viewers.

We are registered as Urban Gang Merch (BN-6ASYVPW5) and we have already confirmed three schools for Term 2, 2026:
• 30 May — Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
• 1 June — Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
• 4 July — Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

But Bishop, this is bigger than talent shows. We are building a movement that follows up with every school we visit — we establish Urban Gang Clubs with student leadership, we bring winners to speak on PPP TV, and we are planning a conference day for all winners and top participants from across the toured schools.

That conference day is where Destiny Life Church comes in.

---

WHY DESTINY LIFE CHURCH?

Bishop, I have watched what this church does with young people. The Lit Gen is not just a youth service — it is a movement. Every Sunday at 11:30 AM, I see young people being shaped by the Word, by worship, and by community. The Tribez teams carry an energy and an identity that is powerful. The counselling ministry, the Upendo outreach, the Equip and Transform conferences — all of it tells me that Destiny Life Church does not just talk about impacting the next generation. You are doing it.

Urban Gang Tour needs a spiritual home. We need a partner who understands that reaching young people is not just about entertainment — it is about transformation. And I believe that partner is Destiny Life Church.

Your 2026 theme is "The Year of Greater Works." Bishop, I believe this partnership is one of those greater works.

---

WHAT WE ARE ASKING FOR

We are asking Destiny Life Church to come alongside us as our Strategic Co-Production Partner. This is not a one-time favour. This is a long-term, deep, covenantal partnership. Here is what we would humbly request:

1. STAGE & SOUND SYSTEM — This is our primary need. We need DLC's stage and PA system for our school events. This is the foundation of everything we do.

2. LED PAR CAN STAGE LIGHTING — The PAR can lights that the church uses for services would transform our school events from a talent show into a production.

3. SETUP CREW — The manpower to assemble and dismantle the stage, sound, and lighting at each school venue.

4. A MAIN SPEAKER — We would love for DLC to provide a keynote speaker for each school visit. Someone who can speak to young people in their language — raw, real, and relatable — about purpose, mental health, and faith. This does not have to be a sermon. It can be a conversation. But it must come from the heart of someone who genuinely cares about young people.

5. COUNSELLING & GUIDANCE TENT — A fully staffed tent at every event where students can speak to trained counsellors. Bishop, the youth in Kenya are going through so much. This tent could save lives.

6. THE TRIBEZ — We would love for DLC to deploy a branded Tribez team to each school. Their energy, their identity, their worship — it sets the tone for the entire day. We envision the Tribez opening the main event with a gospel hype session that lights the place on fire.

7. LIT GEN BRANDING & DJ — The Lit Gen brand is powerful among young people. We would love for Lit Gen to have a visible, branded presence at every event — including a Lit Gen DJ for a jam session.

8. TRANSPORTATION — Assistance with transport for the Tribez team members and equipment.

9. MUSICIAN FACILITATION — When possible, we would love for DLC to facilitate a gospel musician as a headline act. This would be the spiritual highlight of the day.

10. VENDORS — We encourage DLC to bring vendors to our events to serve the students and community.

---

WHAT DESTINY LIFE CHURCH GETS

Bishop, this is not a one-way street. Here is what this partnership gives back to the church:

• DIRECT ACCESS TO THOUSANDS OF YOUNG PEOPLE — Every school visit puts DLC in front of hundreds to thousands of students who need exactly what the church offers. The counselling tent alone could be the beginning of a relationship that brings a young person to Christ.

• LIT GEN VISIBILITY — The Lit Gen brand will be seen by every student at every school, broadcast on PPP TV to up to 8 million viewers, and featured across all our social media platforms. This is youth ministry at scale.

• TRIBEZ DEPLOYMENT — The Tribez get real-world experience in outreach, worship, and leadership. Every school visit is a training ground for the next generation of church leaders.

• NATIONAL TV EXPOSURE — DLC's name, brand, and mission will be featured in every Urban News episode on PPP TV. This is national visibility for the church's youth ministry.

Subject:
Chandaria x Urban Gang Tour: Hygiene Partner for Kenya's Girls Schools
Dear Chandaria Industries Marketing and Design Teams,

I hope this finds you well.

My name is Eugine Micah, and alongside my co-host Lucy Ogunde, I run Urban Gang Tour, a high school talent search, mentorship, and awards concert programme that goes directly into Kenyan schools. We also host Urban News on PPP TV Kenya, which reaches up to 8 million viewers nationally.

I am writing because we believe Chandaria Industries and Urban Gang Tour share something that most partnerships do not have from the start: the same mission for the same people.

Let me explain.

We have three schools confirmed for Term 2, 2026:

30th May: Senior Chief Koinange Girls High School
1st June: Loreto Kiambu Girls High School
4th July: Gathirimu Girls Technical High School

All three are girls schools.

Chandaria Industries has been donating Rosy Girl sanitary pads to girls schools for years. You partnered with She Needs a Chance to host girls from Mukuru for menstrual hygiene education. You are a listed partner of the Menstrual Hygiene Day Organization. You donated 8,000 packs of Velvex sanitary pads to the Kisumu Youth Football Association. You have been doing this work quietly and consistently because you understand that a girl who misses school because she cannot afford a pad is a girl whose potential is being wasted.

Our motto is From Potential to Purpose.

We go into these girls schools with 30 professionals. DJs, dancers, MCs, camera crews, mentors, counsellors. We run the full day with them. Talent competitions in rap, dance, poetry, gospel, drama, comedy, and modelling. Mentorship sessions on mental health, financial literacy, digital skills, and leadership. A counselling tent staffed by trained counsellors from our church partner, Destiny Life Church. And we film the whole thing and broadcast it on PPP TV to millions of Kenyans.

But here is what we do not have yet: a hygiene partner.

We are walking into girls schools where hundreds of students need exactly what Chandaria manufactures. Sanitary pads. Hand sanitizer. Wet wipes. Tissue. These are not luxuries. They are essentials. And right now, we have no partner providing them.

Here is what we are proposing.

We want Chandaria Industries to be the Official Hygiene Partner of Urban Gang Tour. Here is what that looks like in practice:

1. Velvex and Rosy Girl sanitary pads distributed to students at every girls school we visit. This can be done through the counselling tent, through a dedicated Chandaria hygiene booth, or as part of the gift hampers we hand out to winners on stage.

2. A Chandaria branded hygiene booth at every event. This is your space to educate students on menstrual hygiene, hand hygiene, and personal care. You can staff it with your own team or we can manage it on your behalf. This is your existing CSR programme happening at scale, on camera, on national TV.

3. Velvex hand sanitizer and wet wipes at the counselling tent and backstage areas. Branded and visible. Students and staff using your products throughout the day.

4. Chandaria products inside every gift hamper. Winners receive certificates and gift hampers on stage, on camera, broadcast on PPP TV. Your products are physically in their hands during the biggest moment of the day.

5. Stage backdrop and teardrop branding. Chandaria's logo alongside UGT on the main stage backdrop, on teardrops around the venue, and on printed programmes given to every student.

6. PPP TV broadcast credits. Chandaria Industries acknowledged as Official Hygiene Partner in every Urban News episode. That is up to 8 million viewers seeing your CSR in action.

7. Social media coverage. Every event produces 8 to 12 short form clips for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The hygiene booth, the pad distribution, the counselling tent. All of it becomes content that shows Chandaria doing real work for real girls in real schools.

Why this matters for Chandaria.

You have been doing this CSR work for years. But most of it happens quietly. A donation here, a school visit there. What we are offering is a national platform to amplify what you are already doing. When Velvex pads are handed to a girl at a UGT event, that moment is captured on three cameras, edited into a PPP TV episode, posted across social media, and broadcast to millions. That is not just CSR. That is brand building at the highest level.

And here is the part that no other partnership can offer you: every single school on our current calendar is a girls school. You are not sponsoring a generic event and hoping your products are relevant. You are walking directly into the rooms where your products matter most.

Chandaria has been the largest tissue and hygiene manufacturer in East and Central Africa for over 55 years. You are ISO certified. You have Superbrands status. You won the FMCG Tissue Brand of the Year in 2024 and 2025. You are partnered with WWF Kenya. You manufacture 100% in Kenya. Your Buy Kenya Build Kenya commitment is real.

Urban Gang Tour is the vehicle that takes all of that and puts it in front of the next generation of Kenyan consumers and leaders. The girls we reach today are the women who will be buying your products for the next 50 years.

We have attached:

1. The UGT Partner Catalogue, which gives a full overview of the programme, our team, our reach, and all partnership opportunities.

Lucy and I would love to visit your offices and walk through this with your marketing and design teams in person. 20 to 30 minutes is all we need. We are happy to work around your schedule.

Please let us know a date and time that works, and we will be there.

Warm regards,

Eugine Micah & Lucy Ogunde

Subject:
Equity Group Foundation x Urban Gang Tour: Where Potential Meets Purpose
Dear Equity Group Foundation Team,

My name is Eugine Micah. I run Urban Gang Tour with my co-host Lucy Ogunde. We go into Kenyan high schools with 30 professionals and run talent competitions, mentorship sessions, and awards concerts. We broadcast on PPP TV Kenya to up to 8 million viewers.

I am writing because Dr. James Mwangi said something at the 16th Annual Congress two weeks ago that stopped me: "We are cultivating more than scholars. We are developing a movement of purpose-driven leaders who will redefine Kenya's future. Where potential meets opportunity."

This kit is built differently from the first one. Here's what changed and why:

The Strategy Document (387 paragraphs) is built on actual research from Coachella 2026, AFROPUNK, Revolve Festival, eMarketer Gen Z data, Attest's 2026 media consumption study, Sprout Social's 2025 Index, and WGSN consumer trends. The core insight driving everything: UGT has two audiences on social media — students who make you viral and sponsors who fund you — and every post must serve both simultaneously. What's inside the document:

6 Content Pillars — The Stage (performance clips), The Journey (student stories), The Vibe (BTS), The Drop (announcements/FOMO), The Sermon (purpose/mental health), The Flex (production/credibility). Each pillar is designed to hook students AND impress sponsors at the same time.

Hashtag Architecture — Not a random list. A layered system modelled on Coachella's approach: master tags (#UrbanGangTour), campaign tags (#NextStopLoading, #UGTLive, #UGTat[School]), pillar tags (#UGTTalent, #UGTBTS), and discovery tags for each platform.

5 Campaign Concepts — #NextStopLoading (7-day countdown before each school, Coachella-style anticipation), #UGTMoments (student UGC tied to the photobooth), #PotentialToPurpose (mini-doc series for sponsor boardrooms), #TeacherOfTheYear (education sector bait), #UGTDanceChallenge (TikTok algorithm hack).
